Â 988kÂ 1466k
Wednesday, 23 October 2024Â -Â Strasbourg
1. Opening of the sitting
Â 2. Managing migration in an effective and holistic way through fostering returns (debate)
Â 3. Tackling the steel crisis: boosting competitive and sustainable European steel and maintaining quality jobs (debate)
Â 4. Resumption of the sitting
Â 5. Statement by the President
Â 6. Voting time
Â Â 6.1. Deforestation Regulation: provisions relating to the date of application (vote)
Â Â 6.2. Draft general budget of the European Union for the financial year 2025 â all sections (vote)
Â Â 6.3. General budget of the European Union for the financial year 2025 â all sections (A10-0008/2024 - Victor Negrescu, Niclas Herbst) (vote)
Â Â 6.4. Guidelines for the employment policies of the Member States (A10-0004/2024 - Li Andersson) (vote)
Â Â 6.5. Urgent need to revise the Medical Devices Regulation (RC-B10-0123/2024, B10-0121/2024, B10-0122/2024, B10-0123/2024, B10-0124/2024, B10-0125/2024, B10-0126/2024, B10-0127/2024, B10-0128/2024) (vote)
Â 7. Resumption of the sitting
Â 8. Approval of the minutes of the previous sitting
Â 9. Continued war crimes committed by the Russian Federation, notably killing Ukrainian prisoners of war (debate)
Â 10. U-turn on EU bureaucracy: the need to axe unnecessary burdens and reporting to unleash competitiveness and innovation (topical debate)
Â 11. Presentation of the Court of Auditors' annual report 2023 (debate)
Â 12. Findings of the Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women on Poland's abortion law (debate)
Â 13. Seven years from the assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia: lack of progress in restoring the rule of law in Malta (debate)
Â 14. The important role of cities and regions in the EU â for a green, social and prosperous local development (debate)
Â 15. Foreign interference and hybrid attacks: the need to strengthen EU resilience and internal security (debate)
Â 16. Proposals for Union acts
Â 17. EU actions against the Russian shadow fleets and ensuring a full enforcement of sanctions against Russia (debate)
Â 18. Need to strengthen rail travel and the railway sector in Europe (debate)
Â 19. Explanations of vote
Â 20. Agenda of the next sitting
Â 21. Approval of the minutes of the sitting

VORSITZ: SABINE VERHEYEN
VizeprÃ¤sidentin

1. Opening of the sitting
Video of the speeches

(Die Sitzung wird um 9.00 Uhr erÃ¶ffnet.)

Â 

2. Managing migration in an effective and holistic way through fostering returns (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Die PrÃ¤sidentin. â Nach der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache Ã¼ber die ErklÃ¤rung der Kommission zur wirksamen und ganzheitlichen Steuerung der Migration durch verstÃ¤rkte RÃ¼ckfÃ¼hrungen (2024/2882(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, I want to thank you for the possibility to have this debate on migration management. Of course, I agree with you that we need to manage migration in an effective and holistic way. That is what this Commission has been doing during this mandate and this is exactly what we agreed all together with the Pact on Migration and Asylum, which we are now focused on implementing fully. And at the same time, we have taken a whole route of approach, responding to various operational needs with targeted support to Member States and by acting in close cooperation with key partner countries.

And we started to see results. The pact is our common response to better manage migration in an orderly and effective way. It will better secure our external borders and ensure fair and efficient procedures, protect those in need and mean quicker returns for those who don't have the right to stay. When adopting the pact, we gave ourselves two years to put in place the necessary new rules, procedures and structures. We are now working closely with with each Member State to identify needs and priorities for actions at national level.

We know we must stay ambitious, including in our timeframe. Moving swiftly on the implementation of the pact could help Member States manage migration already better today. This is why the Commission is ready to accelerate the implementation of some elements of the pact.

In all cases, we will carefully maintain the important balance between solidarity and responsibility, as well as the internal and external dimensions of the pact. The external dimension and the whole of the root approach will continue to be top priority. We will continue to develop deeper and more effective, comprehensive and strategic partnerships with countries of origin and transit.

Comprehensive partnerships are working. On the Central Mediterranean route, arrivals are down by 64Â %. On the Western Balkans route, arrivals are down by 77Â %. These partnerships are covering mutually beneficial interests, macroeconomic stability, business environment, trade, investments, renewable energy, clean tech, digital connectivity. And in these partnerships, migration and security are embedded as a key component. We are working with our partners on operational support to counter migrant smuggling, border management to prevent irregular migration as well as readmission.

Reducing irregular migration should pave the way for more legal and safe pathways. Migrants are essential to our economies and societies. Let us not forget that most migrants are coming legally to the EU to work or to join their family and Europe's population is shrinking, as we all very well know. Four in five companies say it is difficult to find workers with the right skills. And three quarters of SMEs say they face skills shortages for at least one job role. We need to attract talent to Europe, open legal and safe pathways and integrate those coming in our society as part of us.

Today, you put returns on the agenda and I agree that the EU migration policy can only be sustainable and fair if those who do not have the right to stay in the EU are effectively returned. We have seen some progress. Effective returns increased by 18Â % in January to June of this year compared to the first half of last year, with almost 59Â 000 effective returns.

On the operational side, the high level network for returns and the EU Return Coordinator are working to implement the return roadmap, taking targeted action to make returns more effective, for instance, supporting Member States with the return of dangerous individuals, or using the return alerts from the Schengen Information System.

We see good practices across Member States emerging. Some already implement elements of the pact, such as a negative asylum decision being combined with an immediate return decision. Others look at incentives as part of return case management to encourage voluntary returns and to manage the risk of absconding, and Frontex is now covering almost 45Â % of all effective returns. Return specialists are part of the standing corps.

On the external side, ArticleÂ 25(a) of the Visa Code mechanism is a tool that has proven its effectiveness. Our efforts have yielded results. Yet, when we look at the overall numbers, with almost half a million persons ordered to leave the EU every year, only around 1 in 5 people who received a return decision are actually returned.

So we need to do more. Member States' national procedures on returns still have many loopholes and they diverge too much. We need to put in place a common European return system. For this, we need a renewed modern legal framework adapted to today's challenges. Six years ago, the Commission made a proposal to recast the 16-year-old Return Directive, but co-legislators could not agree on it. Last week, President von der Leyen announced to the European Council that we will be making a new legislative proposal early in the new mandate to make returns more effective and protect the fundamental rights and dignity of those that are under the return procedure. This time, the EU will need to get it right. We cannot afford another six years without any progress on this important issue.

An effective return policy will be essential for our comprehensive approach on migration, which must remain firm but fair, giving protection to those in need, preventing irregular and unsafe arrivals and returning those not in need. The pact's implementation remains our focus. At the same time, it is key to complete the picture with the essential missing piece: an effective return policy. During the upcoming weeks and months, the Commission will work closely with the co-legislators to make sure that we deliver together on this missing piece.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tomas TobÃ©, on behalf of the PPE Group. â MadamÂ President, Commissioner, the EPP Group strongly welcomes the clear and coherent position that was taken by the Commission President and the European Council last week.

We need to take back control of migration. We have common borders and therefore we need a common European response. With the adoption of the migration pact, we took the first major step that we, and not the criminal smugglers, should decide who can enter Europe.

However, it was always very clear to us that more would be needed to secure our borders and increase returns of those who do not have the right to be in Europe. The EPP want to be very clear with what we expect: a speedy and full implementation of the migration pact; a new proposal on returns presented within the first 100 days of the next Commission; increased cooperation with third countries, who should also explore new solutions like return hubs outside of Europe.Â We need a stronger Frontex, and on the instrumentalisation of migrants that we see from Russia and Belarus, we need a full stop.

The EPP will continue to lead the way forward, together with responsible political groups of this House. I can see that parts of the left and parts of the far right are united in their resistance to a common European policy. But EPP stands ready, together with the Commission and the Member States, to deliver.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Iratxe GarcÃ­a PÃ©rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, tras diez aÃ±os de desacuerdos, bloqueos y obstÃ¡culos, logramos alcanzar un acuerdo para dar soluciÃ³n al fenÃ³meno migratorio. Ahora que la justicia italiana ha sentenciado que el acuerdo del Gobierno de Meloni con Albania es ilegal, la seÃ±ora Von der Leyen debe abandonar su propuesta inhumana e ilegal para crear centros de deportaciÃ³n en terceros paÃ­ses. Porque es inaceptable arrodillarse ante la extrema derecha para bendecir un modelo migratorio que viola los derechos humanos.

SeÃ±orÃ­as, sabemos que la desinformaciÃ³n, los prejuicios y el odio al diferente son la Ãºnica razÃ³n de ser de la extrema derecha. Lo que no es menos tolerable es la bochornosa actitud de una derecha que rechaza un diagnÃ³stico de la inmigraciÃ³n basado en datos reales.

Y yo voy a hablar de esos datos reales. La mayorÃ­a de las proyecciones de la poblaciÃ³n de la UniÃ³n Europea alertan de que en 2025 alcanzaremos el pico de poblaciÃ³n y de que, de aquÃ­ a las prÃ³ximas dÃ©cadas, las personas jubiladas aumentarÃ¡n en un 35Â % en la poblaciÃ³n europea, que perderÃ¡ treintaÂ millones de personas en edad de trabajar.

Y ante el drama demogrÃ¡fico que se avecina, el Partido Popular Europeo y la extrema derecha nos dicen que los inmigrantes vienen a Europa a recibir subsidios, aunque la realidad sea que los inmigrantes usan los servicios pÃºblicos y las prestaciones sociales menos que los nacidos en la UniÃ³n. Nos dicen que los migrantes vienen a quitarnos el trabajo, aunque la realidad sea que sectores como la agricultura, la construcciÃ³n o la hostelerÃ­a, con un porcentaje de migrantes entre el 25 y el 50Â %, se hundirÃ­an sin su mano de obra. Y tambiÃ©n nos dicen que los migrantes ponen en peligro a nuestros seres queridos, aunque la mitad de las personas que cuidan a nuestros hijos, a nuestros padres y a nuestros abuelos son personas extranjeras.

SeÃ±orÃ­as del Partido Popular Europeo y de los falsos patriotas, ustedes saben que las entradas irregulares en la UniÃ³n Europea han caÃ­do un 42Â % en el Ãºltimo aÃ±o. Utilizan el dolor de seres humanos que huyen de la muerte y la miseria para generar miedo y alcanzar el poder. Frente a la crueldad de las medidas que ustedes esconden bajo el eufemismo de Â«innovadorasÂ», la Ãºnica soluciÃ³n posible es una polÃ­tica migratoria humanista, ordenada y segura. Una polÃ­tica migratoria que amplÃ­e los acuerdos de migraciÃ³n circular con terceros paÃ­ses para facilitar las contrataciones en origen. Una polÃ­tica migratoria que impulse el desarrollo econÃ³mico y la estabilidad polÃ­tica de los paÃ­ses de origen y que ayude con ayudas al desarrollo para combatir la inseguridad alimentaria y, al mismo tiempo, una polÃ­tica migratoria implacable en la lucha contra las mafias que trafican con seres humanos.

SeÃ±orÃ­as, las migraciones siempre han sido uno de los mayores motores del desarrollo de las naciones. Son el odio y la xenofobia los que destruyen las naciones. Acoger a quienes vienen de fuera buscando una vida mejor no solo es un deber al que nos obliga la historia de Europa, no solamente es defender los derechos y las libertades del mayor espacio democrÃ¡tico del mundo, es tambiÃ©n un camino sin vuelta atrÃ¡s para garantizar el mejor Estado del bienestar en el que vivimos.

Â Â Nicola Procaccini, a nome del gruppo ECR. â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, due messaggi sono arrivati forti e chiari da tutti i popoli europei cinque mesi fa: basta "follie verdi" eco-chic e basta immigrazione illegale.

Noi, che crediamo davvero nella parola democrazia, senza bisogno di doverlo scrivere nel nome del partito o del gruppo parlamentare, pensiamo che non si debba tradire la volontÃ  popolare. Fino ad oggi si Ã¨ seguita in Europa la linea no borders della sinistra: abbiamo avuto migliaia di migranti morti in mare, un milione di mancati rimpatri e abbiamo avuto le nostre cittÃ  consegnate al degrado e all'insicurezza.

Direi che Ã¨ giunto il momento di cambiare linea: Ã¨ tempo di governare l'immigrazione. Non entra chiunque ma solo chi Ã¨ davvero perseguitato, chi possiamo accogliere con dignitÃ  e possibilitÃ  di integrazione. Per fare questo dobbiamo trattare il fenomeno migratorio prima che arrivi sul territorio europeo.

Collega Perez, l'esternalizzazione Ã¨ giÃ  nel nuovo patto. Ã la ragione per cui Ã¨ stato istituito un fondo economico apposito ed Ã¨ la ragione per cui il nuovo patto puÃ² essere considerato come un primo piccolo passo nella giusta direzione.

Affrontare la questione migratoria al di fuori dei confini europei evita che i migranti paghino migliaia di dollari per consegnare la loro vita nelle mani degli scafisti; evita che, una volta arrivati, diventino manodopera per le mafie; ed evita che un cancelliere socialista decida di ripristinare le frontiere all'interno dell'Unione europea, mentre, all'insaputa dei cittadini tedeschi, con le loro tasse, finanzia le navi ONG che alimentano il traffico di esseri umani nel Mediterraneo.

Aiutare i Paesi di origine e transito dei migranti favorendone lo sviluppo economico e sociale, chiedere aiuto a Paesi terzi democratici, come l'Albania, non Ã¨ nÃ© di destra nÃ© di sinistra: Ã¨ semplicemente buon senso. Ed Ã¨ ciÃ² che ha iniziato a fare l'Italia, ottenendo risultati inimmaginabili fino a poco tempo fa. Finalmente lo hanno capito anche alcuni governi socialisti, come quello inglese o danese.

Onestamente, nessuno puÃ² sapere se e quanto funzionerÃ  una nuova politica europea dell'immigrazione. Di sicuro sappiamo che non ha funzionato quella praticata fino ad oggi, ovvero la vostra.

Â Â ValÃ©rie Hayer, au nom du groupe Renew. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, quarante-huit heures: voilÃ  le temps quâaura durÃ© le sÃ©jour des douzeÂ migrants dÃ©placÃ©s en Albanie. Comment peut-on vouloir sâinspirer dâun tel Ã©chec? Outre-Manche, les conservateurs britanniques avaient promis un effet dissuasif en signant un accord avec le Rwanda; rÃ©sultat: 18Â % de migrants en plus. En Italie, MmeÂ Meloni avait promis un blocus naval pour dÃ©courager les traversÃ©es; rÃ©sultat: les arrivÃ©es ont explosÃ©.

Aujourdâhui, les transferts vers lâAlbanie nâont tenu que quelques heures et sont une gabegie, une vraie, car, chers collÃ¨gues, Ã  la crise du coÃ»t de la vie sâajoute la crise du coÃ»t du populisme. EnÂ 2024,Â 12Â migrants dans un centre en Albanie, ce sont 65Â millionsÂ dâeuros: 5Â millionsÂ dâeuros par migrant! Une somme astronomique, aux frais du contribuable, pour une politique qui, non seulement, ne respecte en aucun cas nos valeurs, mais qui en plus ne fonctionne pas.

Chers collÃ¨gues, ces responsables politiques se leurrent. Avec leurs fausses solutions, câest aussi notre souverainetÃ© quâils bradent. En effet, oÃ¹ est la souverainetÃ© quand on confie sa politique rÃ©galienne Ã  un tiers? OÃ¹ sont la souverainetÃ© et lâindÃ©pendance quand on se met entre les mains dâun autre?

Oui Ã  une politique migratoire efficace. LâefficacitÃ©, câest de contrÃ´ler ces migrants dÃ¨s quâils arrivent sur notre sol. LâefficacitÃ©, câest de les enregistrer dans une base de donnÃ©es commune. LâefficacitÃ©, câest dâaccÃ©lÃ©rer le traitement des demandes, pour protÃ©ger celles et ceux qui peuvent lÃ©gitimement se voir accorder lâasile et pour renvoyer au plus vite les autres. Cette efficacitÃ©, chers collÃ¨gues, a un nom: câest le pacte sur la migration et lâasile, que nous avons votÃ© dans cet hÃ©micycle au printemps dernier.

Jâen appelle aux 27: transposez le pacte sur la migration et lâasile entiÃ¨rement et sans plus attendre. ComplÃ©tons ces mesures, rÃ©visons la directive retour et mettons fin Ã  lâinstrumentalisation des migrants par la Russie, mais rÃ©sistons Ã  la cacophonie des mesures nationales et Ã  la dÃ©magogie des fausses solutions miracles. LâefficacitÃ©, pas les effets de manche. RenforÃ§ons notre rÃ©ponse europÃ©enne Ã  ce dÃ©fi europÃ©en.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tineke Strik, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MadamÂ President, dear Commissioner, return policies have been the talk of the town for years now. Member States complain about the lack of cooperation by countries of origin, but they are silent about the very small number of readmission requests that they make. They insist on harsher rules to deport people, but they don't mention their bureaucratic failures to return people under the current rules.

Commissioner, it's your task to make sure that Member States do their homework and to propose laws when they are necessary, in a manner that is evidenceâbased and focused on solving the issue effectively. But in vonÂ derÂ Leyen's announcement of a new legislative proposal on returns, she allows Member States to shift out their responsibility. In these so-called return hubs, people will most probably be put in limbo, in indefinite detention, having no rights or perspective. That's not sustainable return. But that's a great way for Member States to circumvent their EU obligations.

The Commission should not facilitate this, but ensure that Member States use all the current possibilities and invest finally in effective and equal cooperation with countries of origin. I therefore urge you, Commissioner, not to be distracted by populist rhetoric, but to focus on actual solutions. Spurring tougher and tougher language on returns is the easy thing to do. Actually fixing the shortcomings and acting in line with human rights is the right thing to do.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Estrella GalÃ¡n, en nombre del Grupo The Left. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, la palabra mÃ¡s repetida en su discurso esta maÃ±ana ha sido retorno, retorno y retorno. Â¿De verdad es esa la miope mirada que queremos aplicar a la gestiÃ³n de las migraciones? Construir cÃ¡rceles para las personas refugiadas en Europa es una barbaridad: campos de concentraciÃ³n para seres humanos cuyo Ãºnico delito ha sido huir para poner su vida a salvo. Â¿Se imaginan que hubiÃ©ramos hecho esto con las personas ucranianas que huyeron de la invasiÃ³n rusa?

La seÃ±ora von der Leyen ha cruzado una lÃ­nea roja a cambio de un puÃ±ado de votos. Â¿Hasta dÃ³nde vamos a permitir que la seÃ±ora Meloni gobierne Europa? La propuesta de abrir guantÃ¡namos en paÃ­ses vecinos que no respetan los derechos humanos es ilegal, inmoral e inhumano. El acuerdo entre Italia y Albania viola el acceso al derecho de asilo y el Derecho internacional, vulnerando el principio de no devoluciÃ³n.

Estas fÃ³rmulas innovadoras que ahora nos inventamos para externalizar el asilo y las fronteras son, en realidad, una violaciÃ³n de los derechos humanos y un fracaso ya demostrado desde hace dÃ©cadas. Â¿De verdad quieren innovar? Apliquen el Derecho internacional, actÃºen con humanidad, abran vÃ­as legales y seguras para que las personas no se jueguen la vida en el mar: esa es la Ãºnica gestiÃ³n eficaz de las migraciones, coherente con los principios y valores europeos que la seÃ±ora Meloni desprecia, lo que parece que queremos comprarle.

Â Â Sarah Knafo, au nom du groupe ESN. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, on traverse Bruxelles et on se demande: Â«Suis-je encore en Belgique?Â» On arrive Ã  Strasbourg et, dans le tramway, on se surprend Ã  tenir son tÃ©lÃ©phone trÃ¨s fort, des deux mains. On longe la rue qui nous ramÃ¨ne chez nous et que lâon connaÃ®t par cÅur, mais on ne peut pas sâempÃªcher de regarder derriÃ¨re et de presser le pas. Vous avez beau Ãªtre de gauche, ces sentiments, vous les avez vÃ©cus. Si vous Ãªtes trop aveuglÃ©s pour vous lâavouer, vos enfants, eux, se lâavouent, et ils en souffrent. On se sent dÃ©possÃ©dÃ©. On nâest plus chez soi nulle part. Ce nâest pas nous qui avons quittÃ© notre pays, mais câest comme si notre pays nous avait quittÃ©.

Pourtant, ce nâest pas fini. Pourtant, ce nâest pas trop tard. Nous sommes atteints dâun mal dont le remÃ¨de existe. Câest lâimmigration qui a transformÃ© nos pays, et ce nâest pas une maladie incurable. Nous avons des frontiÃ¨res Ã  surveiller? Soit. Des dizaines de pays le font, nous lâavons fait pendant des siÃ¨cles, et il suffit de sây remettre.

Cinq cent mille nouveaux immigrÃ©s par an, ce serait impossible Ã  arrÃªter? Câest faux. Ce ne sont que des titres de sÃ©jour, de simples documents que nos fonctionnaires prennent le soin de tamponner et dâoffrir Ã  des Ã©trangers. Il suffit de leur demander dâarrÃªter. Câest aussi simple que cela. Si les Ã©trangers sous OQTF refusent de partir de leur plein grÃ©, ils nâauront plus le choix. Câest nous qui dÃ©cidons de qui nous accueillons et de qui nous expulsons.

Les politiciens dÃ©testent ce bon sens. Ils disent quâil y a des verrous qui nous empÃªchent, quâon ne connaÃ®t pas le dossier. Leurs solutions Ã  eux sont tellement plus Ã©laborÃ©es: ne rien changer. Si nous retrouvons le sens commun tout de suite, dans quelques annÃ©es, nous aurons du mal Ã  croire que nous avions pu nous laisser aller Ã  ce point et tomber aussi bas. Mais si nous ne nous ressaisissons pas, les historiens chercheront longtemps pourquoi de si grands peuples, si puissants, se sont laissÃ©s mourir sans rÃ©agir. Il est encore temps. RÃ©agissons.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jeroen Lenaers (PPE). â MadamÂ President, I am confused. I listened to the Commissioner from our socialist colleagues having a very constructive and balanced approach to migration, doing more to counter illegal migration, working harder to improve returns â an agenda that was embraced also by the European Council last week, including all the socialist leaders there. And then I listened to the leader of the socialists in this House, and I only hear, 'No, no, no. It's all extreme right.' Is MsÂ Dalli extreme right all of a sudden? Is Prime Minister SÃ¡nchez extreme right? Let's embrace the fact that leaders actually from leftâwing to rightâwing governments, from east to west and north to south, came together on an idea that more commitments are necessary.

Indeed, returns are down. Arrivals have been down in recent years because of the very agreements with third countries that you so fiercely opposed in this House.

So yes, this is not about burying the pact. It is about implementing the pact, but also realising that more is necessary. As President von derÂ Leyen wrote, we cannot be complacent. The pact is a huge achievement, but we need to do more, especially on returns. One in five people who were told to return actually go back to their country of origin. In 2023, this was 350Â 000 people.

Also, for the colleagues on the left, if we want to keep public support, if we want to keep resources to take care of real refugees, we need to improve this and we need to do it together in a spirit of compromise. So let's put new ideas on the table. Like the Council said, let's dare to think outside the box and let's do it quickly.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ana Catarina Mendes (S&D). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, Caros Colegas, eu julgo que Ã© preciso sermos mesmo muito claros sobre a proposta da ComissÃ£o e do Conselho para as migraÃ§Ãµes, porque esta medida Ã©, mesmo, contra os valores do projeto europeu. Ã um erro, Ã© desumano, Ã© a externalizaÃ§Ã£o das fronteiras. Caros Colegas, Ã© uma afronta aos direitos humanos. NÃ£o sou apenas eu que o digo. O sistema judicial italiano e o prÃ³prio Tribunal de JustiÃ§a da UniÃ£o tambÃ©m o dizem.

O facto de a presidente da ComissÃ£o Europeia sugerir que a UniÃ£o Europeia siga soluÃ§Ãµes inovadoras, como as que estÃ£o no Protocolo da AlbÃ¢niaâItÃ¡lia, deve nÃ£o sÃ³ preocuparânos, como tambÃ©m nÃ£o nos silenciar. Relembro, aqui, a decisÃ£o judicial italiana que ordenou o regresso a ItÃ¡lia dos imigrantes transferidos para o centro de retenÃ§Ã£o nos arredores de Tirana, por nÃ£o considerar os paÃ­ses de origem, Egito e Bangladexe, paÃ­ses seguros.

Relembro, Caros Colegas, a ConvenÃ§Ã£o de Genebra, a Carta Europeia dos Direitos Fundamentais e, mais recentemente, a decisÃ£o do Tribunal de JustiÃ§a da UniÃ£o Europeia sobre o caso relativo Ã  RepÃºblica Checa, que refere: Â«um paÃ­s fora do bloco nÃ£o pode ser declarado seguro, a menos que todo o seu territÃ³rio seja considerado livre de perigoÂ».

E, Meus Caros Colegas, nÃ³s nÃ£o estamos a falar de dinheiro, nÃ³s estamos a falar de vidas humanas. A polÃ­tica de externalizaÃ§Ã£o das fronteiras Ã© verdadeiramente um erro, porque destrÃ³i as vidas das pessoas. Cumpramos o projeto europeu.

(A oradora aceita responder a uma pergunta Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ»)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â JoÃ£o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ». â Senhora Presidente, Senhor Deputada Ana Catarina Mendes, os deputados do PS aprovaram o Pacto para as MigraÃ§Ãµes â que contÃ©m uma boa parte das medidas desumanas com que sÃ£o tratados, hoje, os migrantes â e o grupo dos socialistas e democratas, em que a senhora se inclui, preparaâse para aprovar o OrÃ§amento da UniÃ£o Europeia para 2025, que inclusivamente faz referÃªncias Ã  infraestrutura fÃ­sica para conter as migraÃ§Ãµes.

E o que lhe pergunto Ã©: como Ã© que Ã© possÃ­vel fazer esse discurso de defesa dos valores humanistas de uma polÃ­tica de migraÃ§Ãµes e, ao mesmo tempo, estar de acordo com polÃ­ticas e medidas desta natureza?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ana Catarina Mendes (S&D), Resposta segundo o procedimento Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ». â Caro Deputado JoÃ£o Oliveira, respondo com gosto. Sim, os socialistas e democratas, aqui no Parlamento Europeu, aprovaram o Pacto para as MigraÃ§Ãµes. O Governo portuguÃªs socialista contribuiu para que este Pacto das MigraÃ§Ãµes fosse o melhor compromisso possÃ­vel.

O que nÃ£o pode acontecer neste momento Ã©, a troco das perceÃ§Ãµes e do medo e do discurso do Ã³dio, quererâse revogar um pacto que ainda nÃ£o teve a sua implementaÃ§Ã£o. Mas, devo dizer, estarei sempre contra a externalizaÃ§Ã£o das fronteiras, porque considero um erro.

Os compromissos sÃ£o os compromissos. E, neste pacto, a externalizaÃ§Ã£o das fronteiras foi sempre um erro.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marieke Ehlers (PfE). â Voorzitter, commissaris, het migratiebeleid van de Europese Unie heeft te lang de rechten van migranten vooropgesteld, terwijl de veiligheid en de rechten van Europese burgers schaamteloos zijn verwaarloosd. Vorige week schreef Die Welt dat het veelgeprezen migratiepact verre van voldoende is. Het zal niet leiden tot een noemenswaardige vermindering van het aantal migranten in Europa. Een EUâtopdiplomaat zei treffend: âDat is voor ons allen duidelijk.â Alleen door de instroom te beperken en de terugkeer van illegale migranten te bevorderen, kunnen lidstaten voldoen aan hun grondwettelijke verplichtingen, namelijk het garanderen van huisvesting, gezondheidszorg, onderwijs en de veiligheid van hun burgers.

Het migratiedebat is sinds de Europese verkiezingen dankzij onze kiezers drastisch veranderd. We vertrouwen erop dat de Commissie deze winden van verandering zal omarmen voor een zo strikt mogelijk asielâÂ en migratiebeleid. Dit omvat onder andere het moderniseren van het Vluchtelingenverdrag, de mogelijkheid tot een optâout inzake asiel en migratie, een strenge terugkeerrichtlijn, het identificeren van veilige derde landen en het verminderen van de aanzuigende factoren voor migranten.

Nederland zal zich samen met andere Europese landen keihard inzetten voor het drastisch beperken van migratie naar Europa, Want er is een koerswijziging nodig. Het is nu of nooit.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

A to, jak wyglÄda niemiecka polityka powrotowa, najlepiej widaÄ na przykÅadzie odesÅania do Afganistanu groÅºnych przestÄpcÃ³w: gwaÅcicieli dzieci, mordercÃ³w, noÅ¼ownikÃ³w. A czego tak naprawdÄ potrzebujemy? Przede wszystkim uszczelnienia zewnÄtrznych granic unijnych, unijnego finansowania barier granicznych, silnego Fronteksu i skutecznej polityki powrotowej. Â Â

Â Â Malik Azmani (Renew). â Voorzitter, allereerst moet mij van het hart dat de Raad bij dit belangrijke debat vandaag kennelijk niet vertegenwoordigd is. Hoe blij ik ook ben dat commissaris Dalli hier aanwezig is, is het natuurlijk ook een beetje minachting dat de migratiecommissarissen, die er verantwoordelijk voor zijn, of de voorzitter van de Commissie hier vandaag niet zijn, want voor ons â Renew Europe â is dit een belangrijk debat. Het is niet voor niks een debat van prioritair belangÂ voor dit parlement.

We hebben grote stappen gezet, ook in dit parlement, als het gaat om het migratiepact. Volgens mij is het enige wat nog moet worden meegegeven: implementatie, implementatie en implementatie van het migratiepact. We hoeven daar geen twee jaar over te doen. Liever vandaag dan morgen.

Maar waar we nog stappen moeten zetten, is op het terugkeerbeleid. Want wanneer asielzoekers zijn afgewezen in een Europees land, wil ik niet dat ze het in een ander land opnieuw proberen. Door goede Europese samenwerking kunnen we voorkomen dat asielzoekers gaan rondzwerven door Europa. Voor mij is het heel logisch: het terugkeerbesluit dat in Ã©Ã©n land wordt uitgevaardigd, zal moeten gelden voor de hele Europese Unie. We hebben juist Europese maatregelen nodig om de asielinstroom, ook naar Nederland, te verlagen. We zien dat een dergelijke samenwerking haar vruchten afwerpt. Mede door het migratieakkoord met TunesiÃ« zijn de aankomsten via de Middellandse Zeeroute met meer dan zestig procent gedaald.

Daarom moeten we ook de politieke durf hebben om het terugkeerbeleid Europees te regelen door ook samen op te treden tegen derde landen die weigeren om mee te werken aan de terugname van afgewezen asielzoekers. Want als lidstaten dit op eigen houtje doen, dan gaat het ons niet lukken. Zolang lidstaten hun eigen belang blijven verkiezen boven Europese samenwerking, komen we nergens. Dus alleen samen kunnen we deze fundamentele oplossingen bieden.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Diana Riba i Giner (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, tropiezan una y otra vez con la misma piedra y no aprenden la lecciÃ³n: este Parlamento ha denunciado reiteradamente el chantaje que sufre la UniÃ³n Europea por parte de terceros paÃ­ses como, por ejemplo, TurquÃ­a o Marruecos, los cuales ustedes han decidido convertir en nuestros guardianes fronterizos; de hecho, hemos visto, de forma muy cruda, cÃ³mo el Gobierno marroquÃ­, por poner un ejemplo, convertÃ­a a miles de seres humanos en armas polÃ­ticas y los empujaba contra la frontera de Ceuta y Melilla, para presionar, en este caso, al Gobierno del Estado espaÃ±ol. Y, encima, consiguen cosas âseguimos enviando miles de millones de euros de fondos europeosâ y tambiÃ©n logros polÃ­ticos, como que, por ejemplo, hoy en dÃ­a EspaÃ±a reconozca la soberanÃ­a marroquÃ­ sobre el SÃ¡hara.

Nos preocupa sobre todo tambiÃ©n que hagamos caso a la carta de la presidenta Von der Leyen, que ha respaldado las ideas de Meloni en torno a Albania como una forma innovadora y creativa de pensar, cuando se trata de la misma receta de siempre.

SeÃ±orÃ­as, si son capaces de ponerse de acuerdo con MohamedÂ VI o ErdoÄan, podemos ponernos de acuerdo para establecer polÃ­ticas migratorias eficaces, justas...

(la presidenta retira la palabra a la oradora)

Â Â Ilaria Salis (The Left). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, con la vergognosa operazione in Albania di coloniale memoria, il governo italiano si Ã¨ fatto avanguardia di un attacco europeo contro le migrazioni e il diritto d'asilo.

Il tentativo, per ora fallito, di esternalizzare la detenzione in un campo di concentramento su territorio straniero e di accelerare le procedure di valutazione e rimpatrio dei migranti rappresenta: 1, una crudele sofferenza e umiliazione per le persone deportate in alto mare e trattate come umanitÃ  sacrificabile; 2, una prova di assoluta incompetenza, dato che l'illegittimitÃ  dell'operazione era evidente fin dall'inizio; 3, un'inquietante forzatura da destra del diritto internazionale che tutela i diritti delle persone in movimento.

Normalmente, un governo che ha esposto il proprio Paese a una simile figuraccia internazionale dovrebbe dimettersi per manifesta incapacitÃ  e mancanza di etica. Il Parlamento europeo dovrebbe prendere le distanze e condannarlo. L'operazione Albania dovrebbe essere immediatamente interrotta, mentre dobbiamo impegnarci per creare canali sicuri per la migrazione.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Mary Khan (ESN). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Frau Kommissarin! Es wurde ja so schÃ¶n Ã¼ber Zahlen gesprochen, aber allein bis September dieses Jahres sind 166Â 000 Menschen illegal in die EU gekommen, und die Dunkelziffer liegt deutlich hÃ¶her.

Seit 2015 kÃ¤mpfen wir mit diesem Problem, und erst jetzt wird in diesem Haus darÃ¼ber gesprochen. Diese Union trÃ¤gt vor allem maÃgeblich auch die Schuld daran, dass Millionen von illegalen Einwanderern auf unseren Kontinent gekommen sind und kaum abgeschoben werden kÃ¶nnen. Jetzt wird Ã¼ber eine Reform philosophiert, das Gemeinsame EuropÃ¤ische Asylsystem zu reformieren, das vor allem erst in zwei Jahren umgesetzt werden soll. Glauben Sie wirklich, dass in dieser Zeit nichts passiert? Glauben Sie, dass FÃ¤lle wie in Solingen und Mannheim auf Ihre Reform warten?

Wir brauchen keine Reform, wir brauchen auch keine weiteren Migrationsabkommen. Was wir brauchen, sind schnell gebaute, humane Einreise- und RÃ¼ckkehrzentren und die Streichung von Entwicklungshilfe fÃ¼r LÃ¤nder, die ihre illegalen StaatsbÃ¼rger nicht zurÃ¼cknehmen wollen. Â Â Lena DÃ¼pont (PPE). â MadamÂ President, colleagues, let us, for a second, remind ourselves what the starting point last term was. Member States were not able to finalise the previous pact. The House had been kept in numerous discussions about the pact and was also not able to conclude. It took us long negotiations for a complex package and finally last year we arrived with a solution.

Is it a silver bullet? How can it be? It was always clear that it's a baseline system for the inner dimension of the European Union. We always made clear that we cannot leave it there, that we need to improve and that we need to step up. And thanks to the EPP, the external dimension was already part of that.

Therefore, and we heard the numbers, only 20Â % of those who have no right to stay in the European Union are actually returned, which means we need to step up returns by various means. We need effective and meaningful third country cooperation with a European approach. We need to use the full leverage of the Visa Code, which has been at the hands of the Member States for quite some time, we need to fight trafficking and smuggling networks by all means, and we need to allow to take Frontex to take a greater role in assisting Member States in returns and in third country cooperation. And of course, we need to address instrumentalisation as a hybrid threat because it's highly sophisticated, highly cynical, but also highly beneficial for the organisers â well, it's nothing else than state-sponsored criminal networks here.

The EPP will always be the constructive force, and we will always be the ones who drive the asylum and migration policy forward.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AndrÃ¡s LÃ¡szlÃ³ (PfE), blue-card question. â MsÂ DÃ¼pont, you are from the EPP. We are witnessing a severe crisis now in Europe. Still, I have a very simple question to you. Does the EPP support still the relocation of illegal migrants in Europe?

(MsÂ DÃ¼pont asked MrÂ LÃ¡szlÃ³ to repeat the question.)

Does the EPP still support the relocation of illegal immigrants in the EU between Member States?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lena DÃ¼pont (PPE), blue-card answer. â If you had been part of the negotiating team of the pact, you would have well known that we found a solution for the question of relocation, for the question of solidarity and for the question of solidarity that Member States need to contribute, which is the answer to your question.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Cecilia Strada (S&D). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, in queste settimane si Ã¨ parlato molto dei centri in Albania inaugurati dal governo Meloni, come qualcosa a cui l'Europa potrebbe guardare con interesse, un modello per le nostre future azioni.

La scorsa settimana i primi 16 migranti richiedenti asilo sono stati portati nel centro. Ecco i risultati: due erano minorenni, due erano vulnerabili, non avrebbero mai dovuto essere portati in Albania e sono stati riportati in Italia. Gli altri 14 non provenivano da Paesi sicuri secondo i giudici. Quindi non si poteva applicare la procedura accelerata di frontiera: nemmeno loro avrebbero dovuto essere portati in Albania. Tutto questo, per inciso, Ã¨ costato molto di piÃ¹, per ogni singola persona, rispetto a quanto non sarebbe costato se le procedure fossero state svolte direttamente in Italia.

Il centro in Albania, perciÃ², Ã¨ un incubo logistico, Ã¨ un disastro giuridico, perchÃ© non rispetta le convenzioni internazionali e la stessa legislazione dell'Unione. Ã una grave minaccia per i diritti umani ed Ã¨ anche un danno economico piuttosto rilevante.

Il centro in Albania puÃ² essere un modello? SÃ¬, un modello di come non vanno fatte le cose. L'Europa stia invece dalla parte della legge e dei diritti delle persone.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jean-Paul Garraud (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, un changement radical de politique est indispensable en matiÃ¨re migratoire. La Commission europÃ©enne et plusieurs Ãtats tels que la France veulent une application rapide du pacte migratoire, en faisant croire que ainsi nos pays seraient protÃ©gÃ©s. Câest totalement faux. Le pacte migratoire ne fera quâaccÃ©lÃ©rer lâimmigration de masse. Quant au retour dans les pays dâorigine, si MmeÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen se dit Ã  prÃ©sent favorable Ã  une politique plus ferme, elle fait tout son possible pour favoriser les entrÃ©es. Avant de parler des retours, la premiÃ¨re des prioritÃ©s, câest de bloquer les entrÃ©es, mais la Commission ne le veut pas.

Notre politique doit passer par une gestion rigoureuse de nos frontiÃ¨res extÃ©rieures et par des contrÃ´les Ã  nos frontiÃ¨res intÃ©rieures, comme lâAllemagne vient de le dÃ©cider. Notre politique doit Ãªtre similaire Ã  celles de lâItalie et du Danemark pour externaliser les demandes dâasile. Le financement des pays tiers doit Ãªtre subordonnÃ© Ã  la mise en Åuvre des accords de retour. LâEurope doit garantir la sÃ©curitÃ© de ses citoyens et la souverainetÃ© de ses nations. Câest une question de justice, de sÃ©curitÃ© et de respect de la volontÃ© populaire.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Assita Kanko (ECR). â MadamÂ President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, 'They do nothing for our safety.' This was the heartfelt cry from German families about the many knife attacks in their country. They feel powerless and abandoned by us.

Citizens are deeply concerned about the EU's failing migration policy. Integration is non-committal and border control barely exists. Taboos about protecting external borders and the illusion that return policy is a solution prevail, while only 20Â % actually return.

Therefore, Germany, France and others are reintroducing internal border controls out of justified fear of attacks and to stop illegal migration â understandable, but this destroys our prosperity because we have internal borders popping up here and there. Europe loses a substance that guarantees the single market. Who will bear the brunt of this? The consumer, the traveller and our businesses.

If the EU still wants to be relevant, it's time to act more decisively. Throwing more money at Frontex will not help. Reviewing the agency's mandate and turning it into a real border protection agency will, and equipping, not hindering, the Member States to protect the EU external borders. I count on the new Commission to get serious about this.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Fabienne Keller (Renew). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la Commissaire, Â«les migrations sont un dÃ©fi europÃ©en exigeant une rÃ©ponse europÃ©enneÂ». Ces mots sont inscrits dans les conclusions de la rÃ©union des chefs dâÃtat du 17Â octobre dernier. VoilÃ  notre boussole pour gÃ©rer efficacement la migration, qui a conduit Ã  lâadoption dâun accord historique au printemps dernier: le pacte europÃ©en sur lâasile et la migration.

Ce pacte offre des outils nouveaux et puissants pour lutter contre les flux irrÃ©guliers et pour dÃ©courager le dÃ©part des personnes qui ne relÃ¨vent pas de lâasile grÃ¢ce Ã  la procÃ©dure dâasile Ã  la frontiÃ¨re, pour accÃ©lÃ©rer lâexamen des demandes dâasile et pour protÃ©ger rapidement ceux qui sont persÃ©cutÃ©s, mais aussi pour assurer, grÃ¢ce Ã  ce pacte et Ã  ces outils, un partage de responsabilitÃ©s Ã©quitable et une solidaritÃ© nÃ©cessaire entre les Ãtats membres. Ce pacte est une vÃ©ritable rÃ©volution, chers collÃ¨gues, et la prioritÃ© des prioritÃ©s, câest dâassurer sa bonne mise en Åuvre.

Je mâoppose fermement aux solutions dites innovantes dâexternalisation de la politique des retours dans desÂ hubs loin de chez nous. Il nous faut bien sÃ»r travailler sur une nouvelle directive sur les retours. Cependant, de tels centres seraient inefficaces, coÃ»tent une fortune aux contribuables et nous rendraient vulnÃ©rables au chantage de pays tiers, sans pour autant rÃ©gler le sujet dÃ©licat de lâobtention des laissez-passer. Les unes aprÃ¨s les autres, les tentatives des gouvernements de crÃ©er de tels systÃ¨mes se rÃ©vÃ¨lent Ãªtre des impasses. Lâabandon de lâaccord passÃ© par le Royaume-Uni avec le Rwanda en est un exemple. Restons souverains et maÃ®tres de notre politique dâimmigration.

(L'oratrice refuse une question carton bleu de Fabrice Leggeri)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Erik Marquardt (Verts/ALE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Frau Kommissarin! Von den konservativen Kollegen wurde ja gerade gesagt: âWir wollen die Kontrolle zurÃ¼ckgewinnen.â Aus meiner Sicht ist das richtig. Es ist nur aus meiner Sicht auch richtig, mal festzustellen, dass die Wahrheit ist, dass wir in den letzten Jahren die Kontrolle verlieren, weil wir die Regeln, weil wir die Gesetze, die wir uns selbst gegeben haben, nicht mehr einhalten, teilweise auch nicht mehr einhalten wollen.

In Italien haben wir es gerade gesehen, wenn es um Grundrechte geht: Man darf in Demokratien Minderheiten nicht einfach einsperren, nur weil Mehrheiten das wollen. Daran erinnern sie Gerichte, und dann versuchen sie nicht, die Regeln einzuhalten, sondern die Gerichte anzugreifen. Das finde ich unerhÃ¶rt, muss ich ehrlich sagen, und ich finde auch unerhÃ¶rt, dass wir es nicht schaffen, bei AbkommenÂ â und ich habe Ihnen genau zugehÃ¶rt, Frau DalliÂ â wie mit Tunesien und Libyen, wo man ja seine politischen Ziele haben kann, aber wo man auf der anderen Seite doch dafÃ¼r sorgen muss, dass Frauen dort nicht systematisch vergewaltigt werden, dass Menschen nicht systematisch irgendwo eingesperrt oder sogar in die WÃ¼ste geschickt werden. Das sind doch Dinge, die fÃ¼r das Zusammenleben, fÃ¼r die Zusammenarbeit in der Migrationspolitik wichtig sind.

Geben Sie mir bitte dazu noch zwei SÃ¤tze. Das Erste ist: Ich erwarte von der Kommission, dass sie diese Grausamkeiten bei der externen Zusammenarbeit endlich anerkennt. Und ich erwarte von uns gemeinsam und besonders den Konservativen, dass wir den Migrationspakt ordentlich umsetzen. Bitte schielen Sie nicht nach rechts, wir kriegen das zusammen hin.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Dolors Montserrat (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, la frontera sur de Europa âCanarias, Ceuta y Melillaâ estÃ¡ en grave riesgo de colapso ante la llegada constante de inmigrantes irregulares. Las mafias que trafican con personas no eligen al azar dÃ³nde envÃ­an sus pateras. Canarias, Ceuta y Melilla son las tres zonas que mÃ¡s inmigraciÃ³n irregular reciben hoy en toda Europa. Y no es por casualidad. Lo son porque el Gobierno de EspaÃ±a no defiende nuestras fronteras ni a nuestros cuerpos de seguridad, elude la cooperaciÃ³n internacional, desoye a Europa y desprecia la ayuda de Frontex.

SeÃ±ores socialistas, la polÃ­tica migratoria mÃ¡s inhumana es la que no existe, la que permite que las mafias campen a sus anchas y miles de personas mueran en el ocÃ©ano. La inacciÃ³n y la irresponsabilidad del Gobierno de SÃ¡nchez no solo estÃ¡n poniendo en peligro la seguridad de la UniÃ³n Europea, sino la de los miles de migrantes utilizados por las mafias.

Â¿Y saben por quÃ©? Porque al presidente SÃ¡nchez solo le preocupa aferrarse al poder y no dimitir ante los casos de corrupciÃ³n que le acechan en su Gobierno, en su partido y en su entorno personal. Es inadmisible que el mismo presidente SÃ¡nchez que tarda un minuto en entregar una amnistÃ­a a un prÃ³fugo de la Justicia, que no duda en excarcelar a presos etarras, que asalta cada dÃ­a las instituciones âcomo ayer la televisiÃ³n pÃºblica espaÃ±olaâ, tenga a Canarias, Ceuta y Melilla agonizando, sin ayuda para atender a los menores no acompaÃ±ados ni recursos para afrontar esta grave crisis migratoria.

El verdadero efecto llamada europeo se llama Pedro SÃ¡nchez. Europa debe actuar ya. Y obliguen a SÃ¡nchez a implementar el Pacto sobre MigraciÃ³n y Asilo.

Â Â MatjaÅ¾ Nemec (S&D). â Gospa predsednica! Ni problem politika Pedra Sancheza ali pa Å panije, ampak neuÄinkovita in celostna migracijska politika Evropske unije, ki Å¡e ne deluje.

Dandanes migracije poganjajo kompleksni vzroki in krizna Å¾ariÅ¡Äa po vsem svetu in naÅ¡ problem niso vraÄanja. Ampak nasprotno, naÅ¡a najveÄja teÅ¾ava je, da Evropa Å¡e naprej financira avtoritarce v severni Afriki, ki grobo zatirajo svoje lastne drÅ¾avljane. Kot tudi, da Evropa podpira obalne straÅ¾e, ki istoÄasno igrajo vlogo tihotapcev ljudi. In to tudi, da Evropa Å¡e naprej poÅ¡ilja oroÅ¾je na krizna Å¾ariÅ¡Äa kot vkljuÄno z Izraelom.

Kako se lahko Evropa tako kratkovidno pretvarja, da naÅ¡a politika in dejanja nimajo nobenih posledic? Seveda jih imajo.

SpoÅ¡tovani, po osmih letih smo letos pred volitvami potrdili pakt o migracijah in azilu. Äeprav ta ni idealen, ponuja odgovore na izzive migracij.

Sedaj pa se moramo osredotoÄi na izvajanje sprejetega sveÅ¾nja kot tudi na integracijo, uÄinkovito obravnavo ter seveda na sprejem. Ne pa na vÅ¡eÄne ukrepe in besede, ki v resnici ne bodo imele uÄinka, kot tudi priseljevanja v Albanijo ne. In s tem bomo le pospeÅ¡ili in ojaÄali tok po Zahodnem Balkanu in seveda njeno pot pospeÅ¡ili do severa.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Paolo Borchia (PfE). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, questo dibattito avviene a pochi giorni da un drammatico fatto di sangue accaduto a Verona, la mia cittÃ , bellissima e ospitale, che si trova in Veneto, storicamente terra di emigrazione e di integrazione: un ragazzo maliano ha perso la vita dopo aver aggredito con un coltello un agente di polizia che, per difendersi, ha dovuto aprire il fuoco.

Questo Ã¨ un episodio, uno dei tanti, che testimonia che, quando in un territorio arriva un numero spropositato di stranieri, la maggior parte dei quali non ha diritto alla protezione internazionale, poi diventa impossibile gestirne l'accoglienza.

Io avrei voluto, colleghi, che questo dibattito si svolgesse in maniera concreta e non ideologica, senza l'illusione che in Europa esistano opportunitÃ  e possibilitÃ  illimitate di accoglienza. Il titolo Ã¨ "Gestione ordinata ed efficacia della migrazione": perfetto, il contrario di tutto quello che abbiamo visto finora.

In conclusione, vorrei esprimere il mio sgomento per il rapporto della commissione contro il razzismo e l'intolleranza dei nostri vicini di casa del Consiglio d'Europa che "sputa veleno" sulle forze dell'ordine italiane che, ricordo, vanno applaudite, non diffamate.

(L'oratore accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Maria Grapini (S&D), Ã®ntrebare adresatÄ conform procedurii âcartonaÈului albastruâ. â Stimate coleg, migraÈia este o problemÄ, Ã®ntr-adevÄr, dar aÈi spus cÄ nu sunteÈi de acord cu ceea ce s-a dezbÄtut Èi aÈi fi vrut sÄ dezbatem concret cum sÄ facem ca migraÈia ilegalÄ sÄ nu mai fie Èi, Ã®n acelaÈi timp, sÄ-i ajutÄm pe cei care pleacÄ din motive economice. SpuneÈi-mi, care ar fi fost sau care sunt punctele dumneavoastrÄ de vedere? Ce mÄsuri concrete aveÈi dumneavoastrÄ pentru pactul pe migraÈie?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Paolo Borchia (PfE), risposta a una domanda "cartellino blu". â Grazie collega della domanda, che mi dÃ  l'opportunitÃ  di spiegare il nostro punto di vista. Chiaramente, le perturbazioni a livello geopolitico, a livello internazionale, ci dimostrano che adesso bisogna fare delle scelte, perchÃ© dovremo gestire un numero sempre crescente di persone che richiedono asilo.

Visto che le cifre ci dicono che la maggior parte delle richieste vengono rifiutate perchÃ© mancano i presupposti, noi non possiamo covare l'illusione che in Europa ci siano spazi, opportunitÃ  e modalitÃ  d'accoglienza per tutti quanti, per cui bisognerÃ  fare delle scelte.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Charlie Weimers (ECR). â MadamÂ President, colleagues, the right won the election. Securing the border â once rejected as heresy â has become mainstream. The Council, even the Commission, recognise the need to prevent illegal entry, host refugees outside the EU and swiftly deport illegal migrants. But will the European Parliament defend Europe or open the floodgates?

On Monday, this House rejected the left's attempt to control the debate on migration. Now they will use their superpower â their unrivalled ability to whine and cry. For the past decade, this House has indulged the left colleagues. We cannot afford to lose another decade to leftist tears. There is a rightâwing majority in this House to stop illegal migration, stop asylum abuse, and stop the weaponisation of migrants. It's time we use it.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew). â Fru talman! Det verkar rÃ¥da ett missfÃ¶rstÃ¥nd hÃ¤r i debatten om att huset Ã¤r delat vad gÃ¤ller Ã¥tervandring. Det vi inte kom Ã¶verens om hÃ¤r, det Ã¤r hur det ska gÃ¥ till.

Ãtervandring utan att ta hÃ¤nsyn till mÃ¶jligheterna fÃ¶r den som Ã¥tervÃ¤nder att reintegreras Ã¤r inte svaret. Ãtervandring utan att ta hÃ¤nsyn till fÃ¶rutsÃ¤ttningarna i mottagarlÃ¤nderna Ã¤r inte svaret. Ãtervandring utan fokus pÃ¥ behovet hos den enskilda mÃ¤nskliga situationen, mÃ¤nniskorna bakom statistiken, Ã¤r inte svaret. Att hela tiden svara âskicka tillbakaâ Ã¤r inte svaret.

Svaret dÃ¤remot Ã¤r migrationspakten. SÃ¤kra och lagliga vÃ¤gar in mÃ¥ste vara kÃ¤rnan i den europeiska migrationspolitiken, som alltid bygger pÃ¥ respekten fÃ¶r de mÃ¤nskliga rÃ¤ttigheterna och att garantera mÃ¤nniskors sÃ¤kerhet. VÃ¥rt enda svar kan inte vara att skicka tillbaka. ÃtervÃ¤ndande mÃ¥ste vara en del av ett ramverk. ÃtervÃ¤ndande mÃ¥ste vara en del, en pusselbit, i vÃ¥r migrations helhet.

(Talaren godtog en frÃ¥ga ("blÃ¥tt kort").)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rihards Kols (ECR), blue-card question. â I have a very simple question. We talk about returns. The definition of returns, according to the legal basis, is actually 'expulsion'. It's not about returning somebody who's wandering around Europe; we are returning those who are not eligible for international protection. And that is the question.

So do you see the equal sign between returns and expulsion? Expulsion is a legal decision by law enforcement institutions that is not a political decision and should be enforced.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew), blue-card answer. â I maybe do not see the conflict in your question or the big issue in your question. I do believe that what we are differing in is 'how do we do it?' Nobody here is defending the overstay of everyone here. No one is defending that.

What we are talking about is: how do we make people return? How do we enable them actually to have a life in dignity when they go back home? How do we make their countries of origin receive them? And how do we make sure that these people are not in a limbo where nobody wants them and their lives are put on hold?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ignazio Roberto Marino (Verts/ALE). â (inizio dell'intervento fuori microfono)... migrazione efficace e olistica, dice il titolo. Suggerisco di iniziare dall'onestÃ  intellettuale, assente in quella parte di quest'Aula due giorni fa, che bocciÃ² la discussione sulla deportazione e la detenzione in un lager al di fuori dei confini europei, di migranti che fuggono per sottrarsi alla violenza, alla fame, alla povertÃ .

L'accordo del governo Meloni con l'Albania ha un precedente: la base di GuantÃ¡namo, dove i prigionieri possono essere trattati al di fuori della legge. Ma nel caso di GuantÃ¡namo si tratta di sospetti di terrorismo. Qui si tratta del trattamento "olistico" di persone disperate.

Immagino che ognuno di voi ieri sera, compreso l'onorevole Procaccini, che ringrazio per l'ascolto, abbia avuto un'ottima cena e questa mattina una tonificante doccia calda. Ecco allora la visione olistica nelle parole del vicepresidente del Consiglio italiano, cito: "I confini sono sacri e cani e porci arrivano in Europa". Non so per i porci, ma i nostri amati cani domestici, in Europa, ricevono un trattamento ben piÃ¹ olistico che il governo Meloni...

(La Presidente toglie la parola all'oratore)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Siegfried MureÅan (PPE). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, Russia and Belarus are exploiting migrants in an attempt to destabilise the European Union. They are sending people illegally across the border into the European Union, challenging, threatening our security. This is a danger for the security of the European Union. We need to recognise this and we need to act on this. Anything illegal has to be tackled. The European Union needs to stand up for the rule of law. We need to defend the rule of law. Illegal migration is to be tackled. That is why a proposal on returns is to be welcomed.

We are now seeing an instrumentalisation of migration against the security of the European Union, with a clear attempt to divide us by the autocracies around the European Union. Russia is trying to instrumentalise migration against us through Belarus towards Poland. They will not hesitate to work with countries that are close to them in the Middle East and in Africa to continue to instrumentalise migration. We need to recognise this. We need to protect our borders properly. We need to protect ourselves from autocrats who are trying to destabilise our democracies, and we need to protect our external border as well, so that the freedom of movement inside the European Union can continue.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jorge BuxadÃ© Villalba (PfE). â SeÃ±ora presidente, contra la inmigraciÃ³n ilegal, deportaciÃ³n: contra las inmigraciones masivas, deportaciones masivas. Se fomenta el empleo, la iniciativa empresarial, la producciÃ³n industrial o la prÃ¡ctica del deporte, pero que los inmigrantes que entran ilegalmente en Europa se devuelvan a sus paÃ­ses no es una opciÃ³n que haya que fomentar, es cumplir la ley. Los europeos quieren deportaciones masivas y estÃ¡n hartos de la palabrerÃ­a cosmÃ©tica de Von der Leyen.

Hace seisÂ dÃ­as, sesentaÂ yÂ cincoÂ pakistanÃ­es entraron en Europa ilegalmente por las islas Canarias en un barco de las mafias. La ruta mÃ¡s corta desde PakistÃ¡n a EspaÃ±a es IrÃ¡n, Irak, Jordania, Israel, Egipto, Libia, Argelia, TÃºnez, Marruecos y Mauritania. Nueve estados musulmanes los han dejado pasar, pero Â¿por quÃ© no se quedan ahÃ­? Â¿Y cuÃ¡ntos de estos pakistanÃ­es forman parte del Estado IslÃ¡mico y vienen a cometer atentados terroristas a Europa? No tenemos ni una sola informaciÃ³n. Â¿QuÃ© impide meter a esos sesentaÂ yÂ cincoÂ pakistanÃ­es en un aviÃ³n y mandarlos a Islamabad? Solo su debilidad y su complicidad. AsÃ­ que basta de palabrerÃ­a: todo lo que no sea combatir las mafias y devolverlos a sus paÃ­ses no lo vamos a aceptar.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Elena Yoncheva (Renew). â MadamÂ President, if you want the European migration policy to be fair to those who need protection, it should be firm and effective to others who don't have the right to stay. Almost half of the people who arrive in Europe do not receive asylum status. Last year, less than 20Â % of those with return decisions were actually returned. Yes, Commissioner Dalli, we need to do more. Returns is the missing puzzle in the European Union migration policy. We need a new and effective return policy, but we also need the right approach.

Lessons must be learned from the ItalianâAlbanian deal. Such deals are expensive and ineffective, and open the door to legal challenges. What is most worrying, some Member States see this as a way to avoid the implementation of the migration pact. Instead, we should focus on working with countries of origin, establishing sustainable partnership and implementation of the migration pact without delay.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Tom Vandendriessche (PfE). â Voorzitter, tijdens dit debat komen er opnieuw honderddertig asielzoekers bij in Europa. Elk uur van elke dag van elke maand. Meer dan drieduizend asielzoekers per dag. De helft van hen zijn bovendien asielbedriegers. Ze misbruiken asielrecht om te migreren en vier van de vijf van deze gelukzoekers keren bovendien helemaal niet meer terug. Tijdens het debat komen er dus vijftig illegalen bij in Europa.

De Europese Unie observeert, debatteert, overweegt, palavert en delibereert, maar weigert tot actie over te gaan. Nochtans is de oplossing evident. Illegalen moeten we actief opsporen en terugsturen. Landen die hun illegalen niet willen terugnemen, mogen geen euro aan ontwikkelingshulp meer ontvangen. We kunnen hen economisch boycotten, visumsancties opleggen en financiÃ«le stromen stoppen, totdat ze meewerken. De Europese Unie kan hier haar meerwaarde bewijzen. Waarom gebeurt dat eigenlijk niet?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rasa JukneviÄienÄ (PPE). â Gerbiama Pirmininke! Gerbiami kolegos, tarptautinÄje teisÄje susiduria dvi konkuruojanÄios vertybÄs â ir abi labai svarbios - individualios migrantÅ³ teisÄs ir valstybÄs saugumas, teritorinis vientisumas. Antroji nÄra pakankamai Ä¯tvirtinta mÅ«sÅ³ teisÄje. SienÅ³ apsauga yra esminÄ prielaida valstybÄs saugumui. Mes privalome iÅ¡ esmÄs pakeisti savo iÅ¡orÄs sienÅ³ apsaugos politikÄ. Be iÅ¡oriniÅ³ sienÅ³ apsaugos, Ä¯skaitant fizines sienas, nebus veiksmingo Å engeno. KairiÅ³jÅ³ migracijos politika nelegalios migracijos atÅ¾vilgiu atvedÄ Ä¯ Å¡iÄ salÄ kraÅ¡tutinius radikalus. Nelegali instrumentalizuota migracija tampa diktatoriÅ³ ginklu prieÅ¡ demokratijas. Turime tai sustabdyti!

Â Â Harald Vilimsky (PfE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, meine sehr geehrten Damen und Herren! Die gesamte Irrwitzigkeit der sogenannten Migrations- und FlÃ¼chtlingsthematik zeigt sich an einem aktuellen Beispiel in meiner Heimatstadt Wien, wo eine syrische Familie die Summe von 4Â 600Â Euro netto Monat fÃ¼r Monat von der Ã¶ffentlichen Hand erhÃ¤lt. Und neben diesen 4Â 600Â Euro, die diese syrische Familie in Wien erhÃ¤lt, kommt noch ein Klimabonus dazu, der einmalig im vergangenen Monat im AusmaÃ von Ã¼ber 400Â Euro bezahlt wurde, und dazu noch eine sogenannte Mietzinsbeihilfe, sodass in Summe diese syrische Familie 5Â 500Â Euro von der Ã¶ffentlichen Hand erhÃ¤lt, und das ist mehr als das Doppelte, was ein normaler Arbeiter hier in meiner Heimatstadt verdient.

Das heiÃt also, dass hier eine ganz groÃe Diskussion darÃ¼ber entstanden ist, dass diejenigen, die Steuern zahlen, die zum allgemeinen Wohl beitragen, hier auf der anderen Seite Personen finanzieren, die unter dem Titel der SchutzwÃ¼rdigkeit zu uns gekommen sind und zu zwei DrittelnÂ â und das zeigen alle StatistikenÂ â nicht schutzwÃ¼rdig sind, weder Genfer Konventionsstatus erhalten, weder subsidiÃ¤r noch humanitÃ¤r entsprechenden Schutzstatus genieÃen.

Die einzige LÃ¶sung kann nur sein, diesen Menschen in ihrer Heimat zu helfen, sie auf ihrem Kontinent zu halten und sie nicht mehr nach Europa zu lassen.

Â Â FranÃ§ois-Xavier Bellamy (PPE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, nous voulons effectivement remettre sur la table la directive retour, et, parce que nous avons imposÃ© ce dÃ©bat, la gauche hurle au scandale. Jâentendais nos collÃ¨gues de Renew, comme ValÃ©rieÂ Hayer, immÃ©diatement crier au populisme. Mes chers collÃ¨gues, dans quel monde vivez-vous?

LâannÃ©e derniÃ¨re, sur les expulsions dÃ©cidÃ©es par les pays europÃ©ens, seules 20Â % ont Ã©tÃ© exÃ©cutÃ©es en moyenne âÂ moins de 10Â % en France. Pourquoi? Principalement Ã  cause de notre droit. Câest la loi contre la loi. Câest le droit, souvent le droit europÃ©en et les dÃ©cisions de justice, contre nos principes les plus fondamentaux, comme le droit dâasile, qui est en rÃ©alitÃ© dÃ©tournÃ© tous les jours par les mafias de passeurs, qui utilisent les vulnÃ©rabilitÃ©s de notre rÃ©ponse juridique.

Il est temps de sortir de lâimpuissance. Si nous ne le faisons pas aujourdâhui, câest lâidÃ©e mÃªme de la dÃ©mocratie qui sera contestÃ©e dans le cÅur et dans lâesprit des citoyens de nos pays. Â Â FranÃ§ois-Xavier Bellamy (PPE), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Câest bien sÃ»r totalement faux, et ceux qui pourront Ã©couter le propos de LenaÂ DÃ¼pont et suivre le travail que nous faisons avec nos collÃ¨gues du PPE le verront avec clartÃ©. Nous dÃ©fendons le fait de mettre fin Ã  lâimmigration illÃ©gale en Europe. Nous ne voulons pas rÃ©partir les migrants illÃ©gaux; nous voulons faire en sorte quâils repartent. Câest tout lâobjet de ce travail sur la directive retour, que nous mÃ¨nerons avec tous nos collÃ¨gues qui sont ici. Câest nous qui avons posÃ© ce dÃ©bat sur la table aujourdâhui, et nous sommes fiers de travailler pour faire en sorte que lâEurope construise ensemble une stratÃ©gie efficace. Câest peut Ãªtre une diffÃ©rence que nous avons: une stratÃ©gie collective pour faire en sorte de lutter contre lâimmigration illÃ©gale et pour que nos Ãtats gardent leur souverainetÃ© sur leur politique migratoire.

Â Â Paulo Cunha (PPE). â Senhora Presidente, Senhoras e Senhores Deputados, a crise migratÃ³ria Ã© um dos maiores desafios da nossa era. Ã imperativo que abordemos esta questÃ£o com humanidade e dignidade.

Em Portugal, sob a lideranÃ§a do Governo da AlianÃ§a DemocrÃ¡tica, temos implementado medidas exemplares que refletem estes valores fundamentais. O Plano de AÃ§Ã£o para as MigraÃ§Ãµes do nosso Governo promove um sistema eficaz para acolher os que querem entrar, mas simultaneamente intransigente na fiscalizaÃ§Ã£o e no combate aos abusos, ao exigir um vÃ­nculo a todos os migrantes que queiram tratar da sua regularizaÃ§Ã£o.Â Assim, facilitamos a integraÃ§Ã£o e reduzimos a imigraÃ§Ã£o irregular. Queremos garantir que cada migrante que chega ao nosso paÃ­s Ã© tratado com respeito e tem acesso a condiÃ§Ãµes de vida dignas enquanto aguarda a anÃ¡lise do seu pedido de asilo.

Temos que procurar soluÃ§Ãµes inovadoras que respeitem estes princÃ­pios. AlÃ©m das alteraÃ§Ãµes legais introduzidas, o primeiroâministro portuguÃªs anunciou recentemente a criaÃ§Ã£o de centros de acolhimento para imigrantes em territÃ³rio portuguÃªs como uma nova opÃ§Ã£o para uma gestÃ£o integrada.

Somos contra o recurso a fundos comunitÃ¡rios para a construÃ§Ã£o de muros nas nossas fronteiras externas. Defendemos o investimento no uso de tecnologia avanÃ§ada, na vigilÃ¢ncia e na cooperaÃ§Ã£o entre EstadosâMembros. A gestÃ£o eficaz e o fortalecimento das fronteiras externas da UniÃ£o Europeia sÃ£o elementosâchave para um espaÃ§o Schengen sem controlos das fronteiras internas. SÃ³ resolveremos a crise migratÃ³ria se combatermos as...

(a Presidente retira a palavra ao orador)

Spontane Wortmeldungen

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Paulius Saudargas (PPE). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, Europe is full. In parallel with a strategic and cautious integration policy â which is a longâterm solution â the only answer is fostering returns. We should be especially alert in the context of instrumentalised immigration. During Lukashenko's hybrid attack on Lithuania, Latvia and Poland, around 150Â 000 illegal crossings were registered in a shortâtime slot, and these crossings were accompanied by the special forces of Belarus, which in some cases forced the migrants to enter the European Union using their brutal armed force. Any kind of further escalation is possible in such a context. Our countries faced a need for an urgent solution.

We Lithuanians built a physical barrier on our own account in a record time, but what we really lacked was the common Europeanâwide approach to returns. The negotiations with third countries on admitting their nationals, for example Iraq, were complicated. Only together as the EU, we can do it.

To demolish Schengen and to build walls between ourselves is not a European solution. To strengthen the external borders and to foster the return process, this is the answer.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Fernando LÃ³pez Aguilar (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, un fantasma recorre Europa: el fantasma de la negaciÃ³n y el rechazo al hecho migratorio, que solo podemos gestionar si lo hacemos juntos y de acuerdo con el Derecho europeo. Hemos trabajado muy duro por un pacto legislativo, que ahora requiere una ejecuciÃ³n leal y rigurosa, incompatible con la externalizaciÃ³n de las fronteras y con la subcontrataciÃ³n de las solicitudes de asilo.

La carta de Von der Leyen Â«melonizandoÂ» la polÃ­tica europea es un grave error, una concesiÃ³n estratÃ©gica a la extrema derecha, contradictoria con los hechos y con los intereses de la UniÃ³n Europea.

Y a los que estÃ¡n obsesionados con los retornos les digo que la prioridad es la inclusiÃ³n, la prioridad es combatir las mafias y los trÃ¡ficos ilÃ­citos y, por supuesto, abrir vÃ­as legales y seguras.

Y, por tanto, le digo a la ComisiÃ³n que es la hora de exigir a los Estados miembros el cumplimiento leal de sus obligaciones en virtud del Pacto, con responsabilidad compartida y solidaridad vinculante.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Susanna Ceccardi (PfE). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, un milione di mancati rimpatri di immigrati irregolari che meritavano l'espulsione: parliamo di questo. Ma quali sono state le azioni dell'Unione europea per evitare questa enorme falla nel nostro sistema di sicurezza? Niente, l'Unione europea non fa niente â anzi â mette i bastoni tra le ruote a qualsiasi iniziativa degli Stati membri per bloccare l'immigrazione irregolare.

In Italia abbiamo stipulato un patto con l'Albania per costruire lÃ  dei centri per il rimpatrio. Ma i giudici italiani stanno boicottando questa scelta, solo per opposizione politica. Sempre i giudici hanno mandato a processo Matteo Salvini per aver fermato l'immigrazione clandestina nel 2019 e a processo â pensate un po' â per sequestro di persona, come se fosse un criminale comune. Ha dell'incredibile, ma Ã¨ questo che si sono inventati pur di bloccare chi Ã¨ riuscito a dare risposte efficaci.

Secondo voi a chi delinque nei nostri paesi e non vuole integrarsi con la cultura europea e non rispetta i principi di uguaglianza tra uomo e donna, che cosa dovremmo fare? Dovremmo tenerceli tutti? Fermiamo l'ipocrisia e rispettiamo il mandato dei cittadini europei, che vogliono sicurezza e meno immigrazione.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). â Arvoisa puhemies, ilolla katson, miten Eurooppa, Euroopan suunta maahanmuuttopolitiikan suhteen on muuttumassa. Puola torjuu vÃ¤lineellistettyÃ¤ maahanmuuttoa Suomen esimerkkiÃ¤ seuraten. Saksa palautti sisÃ¤rajavalvontansa, ja nyt 17 EU-maata vaatii kiristyksiÃ¤ EU:n palautuspolitiikkaan. NÃ¤mÃ¤ 17 EU-maata eivÃ¤t kuitenkaan ryhtyneet toimeen turhanpÃ¤iten, vaan nimenomaan siksi, ettÃ¤ tehtÃ¤vÃ¤Ã¤ todella riittÃ¤Ã¤. Kielteisen turvapaikkapÃ¤Ã¤tÃ¶ksen saaneet muodostavat suuren turvallisuusriskin Euroopalle, joten tÃ¤mÃ¤n suunnanmuutoksen tapahtuminen oli vain ajan kysymys.

EU:n nykyinenkin lÃ¶ysÃ¤Ã¤kin lÃ¶ysempi maahanmuuttopolitiikka on tehnyt Euroopasta entistÃ¤kin saavutettavamman VÃ¤limeren etelÃ¤puoliselle elintasosiirtolaisuudelle, siis nimenomaan siirtolaisuudelle. Vai onko joku vielÃ¤kin sitÃ¤ mieltÃ¤, ettÃ¤ hÃ¤dÃ¤nalainen ihminen ei kulje ensimmÃ¤isenkÃ¤Ã¤n turvallisen maan lÃ¤pi matkallaan LÃ¤hi-idÃ¤stÃ¤ Saksaan, Ruotsiin ja Suomeen?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hilde Vautmans (Renew). â Voorzitter, een correcte aanpak van migratie is cruciaal voor Europa. Wij pleiten voor een streng maar rechtvaardig migratiebeleid. Wij moeten bepalen wie Europa binnenkomt, niet de mensensmokkelaars. We weten ook wat we daarvoor moeten doen: versterk de buitengrenzen â die zijn nu een kaas met gaten â, voer het migratiepact uit en werk een Europees terugkeerbeleid uit, menselijk en humaan. Dat is wat er moet gebeuren.

Dan hebben we geen interne grenscontroles nodig. Dan hebben we vrij verkeer van goederen en personen, waaraan wij Europeanen zo gehecht zijn. Dat is cruciaal voor onze economie. Want, collega's, jullie weten toch: met die interne controles vangen we geen enkele illegaal, maar dwarsbomen we onze economie. Dan hebben we ook geen onzalige ideeÃ«n van surrogaatgevangenissen nodig.

De rechterzijde wil ik Ã©Ã©n ding heel graag zeggen: vergeet niet, collegaâs, dat jullie het geluk hebben geboren te zijn in een land in vrede.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â JoÃ£o Oliveira (The Left). â Senhora Presidente, vias seguras e legais para as migraÃ§Ãµes, investimento em polÃ­ticas de integraÃ§Ã£o social, respeito pelos direitos sociais e laborais como direitos universais, rejeiÃ§Ã£o das discriminaÃ§Ãµes e combate Ã  instrumentalizaÃ§Ã£o das migraÃ§Ãµes pelos grandes interesses econÃ³micos â nada disto norteia as polÃ­ticas da UniÃ£o Europeia.

Do pacto para as MigraÃ§Ãµes ao OrÃ§amento para 2025, passando pelas mais recentes conclusÃµes do Conselho Europeu, o que a UE tem para apresentar Ã© a preferÃªncia pelo fecho e retenÃ§Ã£o nas fronteiras de migrantes e refugiados, sÃ£o as detenÃ§Ãµes e as dificuldades dos procedimentos de asilo, Ã© uma polÃ­tica migratÃ³ria tÃ£o seletiva, que se torna intransponÃ­vel atÃ© para quem procura refÃºgio da guerra, da fome ou da morte. Escondendo a indecÃªncia das suas polÃ­ticas atrÃ¡s do sarcasmo, a UniÃ£o Europeia designa, agora, de promoÃ§Ã£o dos regressos a polÃ­tica de expulsÃµes de migrantes e refugiados.

Faz falta outra polÃ­tica, uma polÃ­tica de migraÃ§Ã£o inclusiva, de respeito pelos direitos humanos e da dignidade de todos os indivÃ­duos, independentemente da sua origem. Ã disso que os povos precisam.

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Liebe Menschen Europas, wir schaffen das! Ein Satz, der einige der Abgeordneten hier offensichtlich mehr traumatisiert hat alsÂ â ich weiÃ nichtÂ â eine Ãberquerung des Mittelmeers auf einem Schlauchboot. Ich mÃ¶chte Ihnen allen mal eine unbequeme Wahrheit erÃ¶ffnen: Fachleute sagen uns, dass durch den Klimawandel Migrationsbewegungen von hunderten Millionen Menschen nach Europa zu erwarten sind. Diese Menschen werden kommen.

Eine Kollegin hat gesagt: Jetzt wird hier Ã¼ber eine Reform philosophiert. Es wird nicht Ã¼ber eine Reform philosophiert; es wurde eine Reform beschlossen im FrÃ¼hjahr dieses JahresÂ â das EU-Migrations- und Asylpaket. NatÃ¼rlich hilft illegale Migration niemandem und schafft Probleme. NatÃ¼rlich kommt auch legale Migration mit Herausforderungen. Aber Migration ist auch unsere Chance, Probleme zu bekÃ¤mpfenÂ â den FachkrÃ¤ftemangel, das kollabierende Rentensystem oder z.Â B. auch die Landflucht.

Dieses Thema ist nicht einfach, aber wir schaffen das!

Â Â Matej Tonin (PPE). â Gospa predsednica! Äe Å¾elimo ilegalne migracije spraviti v neke obvladljive okvirje, potem je jasno, da moramo temeljno spremeniti naÅ¡e sporoÄilo, da kdorkoli bo v Evropsko unijo priÅ¡el ilegalno, bo vrnjen nazaj v svojo lastno domovino. Å ele na takÅ¡en naÄin se bo nezaustavljiv tok zaustavil.

Äe bomo resniÄno vzpostavili mehanizme, da bomo vse tiste, ki prihajajo v Evropsko unijo ilegalno, tudi uÄinkovito vraÄali nazaj v njihove domovine. Brez tega se ilegalnih migracij ne da urediti. Ker, karkoli bomo naredili, vedno se bo naÅ¡la pot, da ljudje pridejo sem, iÅ¡Äejo boljÅ¡e Å¾ivljenje in na nek naÄin pridejo do tega, da reÄejo, bomo Å¾e nekako stvari uredili.

Zato moram priznat, da sem bil vesel zadnjega pisma predsednice Evropske komisije, da se konÄno tudi v Evropi stvari postavljajo na pravo pot in v pravo smer, da uredimo ilegalne migracije.

Reikia dar kartÄ pasakyti, kad Migracijos pakto vykdymas yra solidarus visÅ³ 27 Å¡aliÅ³ nariÅ³ vyriausybiÅ³ atsakomybÄs reikalas. Ir visa tai, kÄ siÅ«lo Komisija, reikia taip pat stengtis Ä¯gyvendinti. Ir dÄl to mes turime dar kartÄ pasakyti, kad mes giname Å¾mogaus teises.

Â Â 

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, dear honourable Members, migration has always existed, albeit it's a changing phenomenon driven by new opportunities for movement, but also by a changing geopolitical context.

With the pact, we took several steps in the right direction, and making it work is a top priority. We must be ready to adapt our management of migration to new realities. Cooperation with third countries is an important element of our comprehensive approach. We need to further strengthen this aspect if we are to prevent irregular migration and fight smugglers successfully.

The Return Directive was adopted 16 years ago. During those 16 years, our migration situation has obviously changed. Our practices and operational reality have evolved considerably. Some Member States are calling for exploring possible ways forward as regards solutions, like the idea of developing return hubs outside the EU. The Commission will continue to explore possible ways forward as regards the idea of developing return hubs outside the EU, especially in view of a new legislative proposal on return.

But let me stress: important attention will be paid to the respect of fundamental rights when considering measures such as return hubs. Exchanges with international organisations such as UNHCR are taking place to make sure international law and the principle of non-refoulement is always respected in all cases.

The Commission will build on the achievements of recent years to ensure that we stand fair and firm on migration, addressing with European solutions what we all agree to be a European challenge. This is what the European citizens expect, and we will deliver.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â Thank you very much. The debate is now closed.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Nikola BartÅ¯Å¡ek (PfE), in writing. â The European Commission has repeatedly claimed that the Migration and Asylum Pact equips the EU with better tools to secure its external borders and respond effectively to migration challenges. However, these are largely hollow promises. The current Returns Directive remains ineffective, and although President von der Leyen has spoken about reviewing the framework, years have been wasted.

Since 2018, hundreds of thousands of irregular migrants have not been returned, despite orders. The reality is stark: only about 20Â % of third-country nationals ordered to leave the EU have actually returned. In 2022 alone, 515Â 000 irregular migrants were issued return decisions by EU Member States, but only 86Â 000 were effectively returned. This indicates a clear failure of the system.

A key issue is the over-reliance on voluntary returns, which is not reflective of the true challenges. We must strengthen our capacity to return individuals who do not wish to leave, as this is the predominant reality. Moreover, returns should be prioritised for those posing security threats. While there is much discussion about this, little tangible action has been taken. The lack of effective returns continues to act as a pull factor for illegal migration into the EU, exacerbating the problem.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â
