Ãsszesen 33%-kal volt tÃ¶bb a problÃ©mÃ¡s, szabÃ¡lytalan forrÃ¡sfelhasznÃ¡lÃ¡s az elÅzÅ Ã©vhez kÃ©pest. SajnÃ¡latos mÃ³don az idei jelentÃ©s tÃ¶bb magyar kÃ¶zbeszerzÃ©si Ã¼gyet is kiemel, amelyek szabÃ¡lytalansÃ¡gokat mutatnak. Ezek az esetek is vilÃ¡gosan jelzik a magyar kormÃ¡nyzat gyenge ellenÅrzÃ©si mechanizmusait Ã©s az aggasztÃ³an magas korrupciÃ³s szintjÃ©t. Az EurÃ³pai UniÃ³ jÃ¶vÅje szempontjÃ¡bÃ³l elengedhetetlen a kÃ¶ltsÃ©gvetÃ©si ellenÅrzÃ©si mechanizmusok Ã©s Ã¡tlÃ¡thatÃ³sÃ¡g uniÃ³s szintÅ± megerÅsÃ­tÃ©se, kÃ¼lÃ¶nÃ¶sen azon tagÃ¡llamok esetÃ©ben, ahol a korrupciÃ³, a csalÃ¡s, az elszÃ¡moltathatÃ³sÃ¡g Ã©s a jogÃ¡llamisÃ¡g hiÃ¡nya megfigyelhetÅ. Csak egy erÅsebb uniÃ³s szintÅ± ellenÅrzÃ©si mechanizmussal biztosÃ­thatjuk az uniÃ³s polgÃ¡rok bizalmÃ¡t.

Â 

12. Findings of the Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women on Poland's abortion law (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Le PrÃ©sident. â Lâordre du jour appelle le dÃ©bat sur la dÃ©claration de la Commission sur les conclusions du ComitÃ© pour lâÃ©limination de la discrimination Ã  lâÃ©gard des femmes concernant la loi polonaise sur lâavortement (2024/2867(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, I am grateful for the opportunity to address the importance of sexual and reproductive rights in this House. Sexual and reproductive rights are an essential part of women's rights, and Member States are competent to legislate in this area.

This summer, the UN Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination Against Women found the situation in Poland in relation to abortion to be of concern.

To recall the background, in 2020 the Polish Constitutional Tribunal limited the right to abortion in cases of foetal malformations. This amounts to a near-total ban, since most abortions in Poland were performed on this basis. It is also a fact that many doctors refused to perform abortion for fear of prosecution. Access to the procedure is hindered by complicated bureaucracy and actions by anti-abortion groups. After the decision of the tribunal, many people in Poland took to the streets to express their anger at the restrictions on their fundamental rights and freedoms.

At the same time, the government has recently taken steps to ease the situation pending a legislative reform. The government issued guidelines for hospitals and prosecutors regarding the use of legal abortion to ensure that women qualifying for an abortion under the current rules have effective access to the procedure. With these guidelines, the government aims to clarify that abortion is permissible if the pregnancy poses a risk to the mother's mental health and that the opinion of one doctor is sufficient to ascertain such a diagnosis.

Moreover, the government made it easier for women to access emergency contraception. Despite these new guidelines, there is a risk that thousands of women in Poland are forced to travel abroad to receive access to health services. This option is not accessible for women with lower socioeconomic status or women from marginalised communities. When women are left without other choices, they sometimes resort to unsafe clandestine procedures with all the risk that this entails. And of course, such situations should not be tolerated.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ewa Kopacz, w imieniu grupy PPE. â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Polki to dumne, Åwiadome i odwaÅ¼ne kobiety, ktÃ³re doskonale znajÄ swoje prawa. Â Â

Â Â Abir Al-Sahlani, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Mr President, Madam Commissioner, esteemed colleagues, state control over her body, state control over her health, state control over her own choices. The ban on abortion in Poland has led to a lethal outcome for many women in Poland. One year has passed since the government change, a change that was led by the young people of Poland, by the women in Poland, and nothing has happened yet.

Thousands of women are forced to flee the country over the Polish border so they can perform abortion. Others are forced to undergo illegal, unsafe and in many aspects also lethal abortions. In Poland, thanks to the anti-gender movement and political parties, together with the religious establishment, it is the state that is exercising violence against women.

The Polish abortion ban is nothing else but a ruthless, cruel violence against women. It is time for change. It is time for women in Poland to own their own choices. They have the right to their own bodies.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Manon Aubry, au nom du groupe The Left. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, si jâavais dÃ» avorter en Pologne, jâaurais pu y laisser la vie. Comme Dorota, que les mÃ©decins ont Ã©galement laissÃ©e mourir, alors que son fÅtus nâÃ©tait pas viable.

Et encore, je parle ici dâavortements thÃ©rapeutiques. MÃªme ceux-lÃ  sont restreints en Pologne. MÃªme quand la vie de la femme est en danger. MÃªme quand les mÃ©decins peuvent le pratiquer, ils laissent mourir les femmes au lieu de les sauver, par peur de poursuites. Alors que dire du droit plus large, encore interdit en Pologne, de choisir dâavoir un enfant ou pas, de choisir dâavorter ou non? Comment tous ces hommes peuvent-ils dÃ©cider pour des millions de femmes ce quâelles peuvent faire de leur corps? Que savent-ils de lâimpact dâune grossesse sur nos vies, sur nos corps?

Depuis lâarrivÃ©e au pouvoir de DonaldÂ Tusk, qui avait promis de lÃ©galiser lâavortement, presque rien nâa changÃ©. Les promesses sont restÃ©es des mots, et la souffrance des Polonaises une rÃ©alitÃ©. Le rapport du ComitÃ© pour lâÃ©limination de la discrimination Ã  lâÃ©gard des femmes est formel: lâinterdiction de lâavortement en Pologne met en danger la santÃ© et la vie des femmes, cause des souffrances mentales et physiques et constitue une violation de leurs droits.

Cette conclusion, qui est vraie pour la Pologne, elle lâest aussi, chers collÃ¨gues, pour de nombreux autres pays, Ã  lâheure oÃ¹ lâextrÃªme droite menace chaque jour un peu plus nos droits. En Hongrie, les femmes doivent Ã©couter le cÅur du fÅtus avant dâavorter. En Italie, les mÃ©decins objecteurs de conscience sont si nombreux que lâaccÃ¨s Ã  lâavortement est un vÃ©ritable parcours de la combattante. Ã Malte, le droit Ã  lâavortement est tout simplement interdit. Câest sans parler de tous les Ãtats oÃ¹ les mouvements antichoix sont en embuscade, oÃ¹ ils sâorganisent pour dÃ©sinformer ou attaquer les plannings familiaux, quand ils ne siÃ¨gent pas directement sur les bancs de lâextrÃªme droite de cet hÃ©micycle ou nâorchestrent ici, au Parlement europÃ©en, des manifestations.

Chers collÃ¨gues, vous avez entendu la rhÃ©torique de lâextrÃªme droite: un avortement serait un meurtre, un gÃ©nocide. Que sont-ils? Que font-ils aujourdâhui? Ils portent une lourde responsabilitÃ©: celle de sâattaquer Ã  nos droits fondamentaux. Leur visage, ici, au Parlement europÃ©en, est trÃ¨s clair.

Alors, chers camarades, chers collÃ¨gues, je vous propose, ensemble, un engagement commun: mener ici le combat pied Ã  pied pour Ã©tendre le droit Ã  lâavortement pour toutes, pour lâinscrire dans la charte des droits fondamentaux, pour faire aboutir lâinitiative europÃ©enne facilitant lâaccÃ¨s Ã  lâIVG partout en Europe.Â Parce que, nâen dÃ©plaise Ã  tous les rÃ©actionnaires prÃ©sents âÂ en trop grand nombreÂ â ici, notre corps nâest pas assignÃ© Ã  une mission. Charge Ã  chacune et Ã  chacun dâentre nous dâen choisir sa destinÃ©e. Câest la condition mÃªme de notre Ã©mancipation.

Alors, pour Izabela, pour Agnieszka, pour Dorota, pour toutes celles et tous ceux qui nâont pas eu la chance avant nous de bÃ©nÃ©ficier du droit Ã  lâavortement, menons ensemble ce combat et chassons de ce Parlement europÃ©en lâextrÃªme droite, qui nâa quâune obsession: sâattaquer Ã  nos droits.

Â Â Arba Kokalari (PPE). â Herr talman! AbortrÃ¤tten kan aldrig tas fÃ¶r given. NÃ¤r USA nu gÃ¥r till val stÃ¥r kvinnors rÃ¤tt till sin kropp i centrum. I Texas, dÃ¤r abort har fÃ¶rbjudits, har mÃ¶dradÃ¶dligheten Ã¶kat med Ã¶ver 50Â % pÃ¥ nÃ¥gra Ã¥r.

Â Â Ana Catarina Mendes (S&D). â Senhor Presidente, ironia do destino Ã© que Simone Veil foi a primeira mulher presidente deste Parlamento Europeu. Eu faÃ§o parte de um paÃ­s que, em 1982, iniciou, logo apÃ³s a revoluÃ§Ã£o, a discussÃ£o. Foram precisos 35 anos para que nÃ³s pudÃ©ssemos ter a despenalizaÃ§Ã£o da interrupÃ§Ã£o voluntÃ¡ria da gravidez, por uma questÃ£o de justiÃ§a social, por uma questÃ£o de acesso Ã  saÃºde pÃºblica, mas sobretudo por respeito por todos aqueles que nÃ£o tÃªm oportunidades, mas que precisam de recorrer aos acessos mÃ©dicos.

Por isso mesmo, uma Europa que respeita os direitos humanos Ã© uma Europa que respeita o direito ao aborto, e a PolÃ³nia tem que recuar jÃ¡ na sua decisÃ£o.

Â 

Â Â Margarita de la Pisa CarriÃ³n (PfE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±ora comisaria, seÃ±orÃ­as, las conclusiones de las Naciones Unidas respecto a Polonia y su legislaciÃ³n sobre el aborto son muy preocupantes. Suponen una clara intromisiÃ³n en la soberanÃ­a de un paÃ­s para imponer una agenda que atenta contra el derecho mÃ¡s fundamental: el derecho a la vida.

No existe un derecho al aborto. Invocan una convenciÃ³n sobre la discriminaciÃ³n de la mujer del aÃ±o 1979 que no menciona en absoluto este tema. Es una vergÃ¼enza que las Naciones Unidas crean que pueden exigir a los Estados cambios legislativos contrarios a sus propias Constituciones, que se inventen referencias, que intenten manipular con casos donde corre peligro la vida de la mujer. No son estas mujeres las que les estÃ¡n preocupando. Sean claros: pretenden el aborto libre, sin supuestos o plazos.

La ley polaca es un ejemplo de compromiso con los mÃ¡s vulnerables: el niÃ±o por nacer y su madre. No pretendan enfrentarlos. Las legislaciones en otros Estados, cada vez mÃ¡s permisivas con el aborto, promueven el desamparo de la mujer embarazada, a la que no se le ofrecen alternativas y se la presiona en esta irreversible y dramÃ¡tica decisiÃ³n.

El aborto es un fracaso. Aunque lo quieran disfrazar de servicio sanitario, es contrario a la vocaciÃ³n de los mÃ©dicos de sanar y cuidar. Es incomprensible que se les pretenda empujar a actuar en contra de este principio. Â¿DÃ³nde quedarÃ¡ la confianza del mÃ©dico con el paciente?

Pido a las Naciones Unidas que recuperen el sentido original de los derechos humanos, donde el derecho a la vida y la dignidad de la persona son incuestionables.

(La oradora acepta responder a varias preguntas formuladas con arreglo al procedimiento de la Â«tarjeta azulÂ»)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Bruno GonÃ§alves (S&D), Pergunta segundo o procedimento Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ». â Senhora Deputada, se calhar nÃ£o foi informada, mas este nÃ£o Ã© um debate sobre existir ou nÃ£o existir aborto. Este Ã© um debate sobre a criminalizaÃ§Ã£o do aborto e lamento informÃ¡âla de que, independentemente daquilo que seja a sua posiÃ§Ã£o...

(O Presidente interrompe o orador)

Lamento informÃ¡âla, mas isto nÃ£o Ã© um debate sobre a existÃªncia ou inexistÃªncia do aborto. O aborto existe hÃ¡ muitos anos e continuarÃ¡ a existir. O debate aqui, hoje, presente Ã© sobre a criminalizaÃ§Ã£o do aborto. E aquilo que nÃ³s hoje sabemos Ã© que sempre que avanÃ§amos â seja na PolÃ³nia, seja em Portugal, seja em Espanha â com a criminalizaÃ§Ã£o do aborto, quem sofre mais sÃ£o as mulheres, psicologicamente, mentalmente, fisicamente.

E, portanto, a minha pergunta Ã  senhora deputada Ã© muito simples: concorda ou nÃ£o concorda com o direito das mulheres a decidirem sobre a sua prÃ³pria saÃºde e a decidirem sobre os seus prÃ³prios corpos?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Margarita de la Pisa CarriÃ³n (PfE), respuesta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â Creo que el derecho que tiene una mujer es a ser protegida en su embarazo, a poder dar la bienvenida a su bebÃ©. Sabemos que donde hay legislaciones permisivas, donde existe la simple propuesta del aborto, la mujer se siente empujada a abortar.

No les veo preocupados a ustedes por esta situaciÃ³n de las mujeres, que eso sÃ­ que es una verdadera discriminaciÃ³n: el que una mujer no pueda acoger a sus hijos por una situaciÃ³n econÃ³mica complicada o por falta de apoyo emocional, muchas veces de un hombre, o porque tenga que evitar que se vea un embarazo despuÃ©s de una agresiÃ³n. No les veo preocupados por esas situaciones donde el aborto sÃ­ que estÃ¡ siendo de alguna manera impuesto.

La simple proposiciÃ³n del aborto supone una presiÃ³n para la mujer. Las mujeres quieren ser amparadas, quieren ser acogidas, quieren poder decir Â«bienvenidoÂ» a su bebÃ©.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Raquel GarcÃ­a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew), pregunta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â SeÃ±ora De la Pisa CarriÃ³n, usted ha dicho que todos tenemos derecho a nuestra vida y nuestra dignidad. En su opiniÃ³n, Â¿no tenÃ­a Izabela Sajbor el mismo derecho a su vida y a su dignidad? TenÃ­a treinta aÃ±os.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Margarita de la Pisa CarriÃ³n (PfE), respuesta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â Cuando la vida de la mujer estÃ¡ en juego, nadie cuestiona que haya una decisiÃ³n en ese sentido. No conozco exactamente todos los detalles de lo ocurrido allÃ­. Pero, cuando hay riesgo para la vida de la mujer, la ley polaca contempla esa situaciÃ³n.

Si ustedes quisieran colaborar con los profesionales mÃ©dicos, a los que se les intenta dejar de lado, a los que ni siquiera se les da la libertad de conciencia... En los hospitales, normalmente se hace un consejo mÃ©dico y en ese consejo mÃ©dico se exige la deliberaciÃ³n de mÃ¡s de un profesional sanitario y entre esos profesionales...

En Polonia, el hecho de que el aborto estÃ© amparado cuando corre riesgo la vida de la mujer depende de una decisiÃ³n mÃ©dica que se toma en un hospital. Con mis compaÃ±eros aquÃ­ en el Parlamento Europeo he tenido la oportunidad de visitar Polonia y comprobar lo que digo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Irene Montero (The Left), pregunta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â SeÃ±ora De la Pisa, yo creo que usted es plenamente consciente de que mienten, y mienten porque lo que hacen sus legislaciones prohibiendo el aborto es poner en riesgo la vida de las mujeres.

Ustedes saben perfectamente que cuando se prohÃ­be el aborto no hay menos aborto: lo que hay es mÃ¡s mujeres jugÃ¡ndose la vida y mÃ¡s mujeres que mueren intentando decidir sobre su propio cuerpo.

Â¿QuÃ© tipo de sociedad quieren ustedes? Â¿QuÃ© tipo de crueldad los acompaÃ±a para querer paÃ­ses y legislaciones en los que estÃ¡n, ustedes, poniendo en riesgo la vida de mÃ¡s de la mitad de la poblaciÃ³n, es decir, de las mujeres?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Margarita de la Pisa CarriÃ³n (PfE), respuesta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â Empiezo dando las gracias a mis compaÃ±eros, porque me estÃ¡is dando una oportunidad de intercambiar impresiones con vosotros en un tema que sabÃ©is que me preocupa muchÃ­simo: gracias por esta oportunidad.

Â¿Te das cuenta, Irene, del impacto social que tiene una legislaciÃ³n del aborto como la que existe en EspaÃ±a? Â¿QuÃ© mensaje estamos transmitiendo a la sociedad, a los niÃ±os, a las mujeres, cuando seÃ±alamos la fertilidad como un problema, cuando seÃ±alamos el embarazo como un problema? Es cerrarle los brazos, es decirle: Â«No eres bienvenidoÂ».

Yo quiero una sociedad que acoja la vida, que celebre la vida, que celebre la fertilidad de las mujeres, no que estÃ© contra la mujer.

Â Â MÃ©lissa Camara (Verts/ALE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, Madame la Commissaire, ces derniÃ¨res annÃ©es, les Polonaises se sont massivement mobilisÃ©es, elles se sont levÃ©es contre la dÃ©cision du Tribunal constitutionnel de rÃ©duire lâaccÃ¨s Ã  lâavortement. Elles se sont levÃ©es contre les dÃ©cÃ¨s et contre les emprisonnements de femmes qui ne souhaitaient quâune chose: pouvoir jouir pleinement de leurs droits sexuels et reproductifs, en toute sÃ©curitÃ©.

Ce quâil sâest passÃ© en Pologne avec les manifestations noires et les grÃ¨ves des femmes doit nous inspirer. Elles ont dÃ©montrÃ© quâÃ  force de mobilisation il Ã©tait possible de renverser la vapeur. En allant massivement aux urnes, les Polonaises ont permis de renverser un rÃ©gime illibÃ©ral en place depuis huit ans. Comment se fait-il quâil ait fallu en arriver lÃ ? Comment se fait-il quâun des pays les plus ouverts et avant-gardistes sur lâavortement depuis sa loi deÂ 1956 ait pu basculer ainsi pour devenir lâun des plus restrictifs?

Cela sâexplique par lâarrivÃ©e au pouvoir des artisans dâun rÃ©gime illibÃ©ral. Quand des dirigeants dâextrÃªme droite arrivent au pouvoir, ce sont dâabord aux droits des femmes et des minoritÃ©s quâils sâen prennent. Nous en avons eu la preuve en Pologne, et nous en avons eu la preuve dans dâautres pays europÃ©ens sur dâautres sujets relatifs aux droits des femmes, des minoritÃ©s, des personnes LGBT, tels que les thÃ©rapies de conversion, lâÃ©ducation Ã  la vie sexuelle et affective, les droits sexuels et reproductifs.

Ã cet Ã©gard, Simone de Beauvoir a dit: Â«Rien nâest jamais dÃ©finitivement acquis. Il suffira dâune crise politique, Ã©conomique ou religieuse pour que les droits des femmes soient remis en question. Votre vie durant, vous devrez rester vigilantes.Â» Nous serons vigilantes.

Â Â Irene Montero (The Left). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±ora comisaria, bien saben, seÃ±orÃ­as, que impedir el derecho al aborto no hace que haya menos aborto: hace que haya mÃ¡s mujeres que mueren abortando. Es exactamente lo que estÃ¡ ocurriendo en Polonia.

Ustedes no defienden la vida, ponen en riesgo la vida de mÃ¡s de la mitad de la poblaciÃ³n, es decir, de las mujeres, y las instituciones de Polonia estÃ¡n ejerciendo violencia contra las mujeres y son responsables de esas muertes.

El informe de Naciones Unidas es muy claro: la prohibiciÃ³n y persecuciÃ³n del derecho al aborto es una violaciÃ³n de derechos fundamentales y es tortura. Las mujeres tenemos derecho a decidir sobre nuestros cuerpos y garantizar el derecho al aborto no es una opciÃ³n, es una obligaciÃ³n para todos los Estados miembros.

Europa debe asegurar tambiÃ©n, de forma urgente, que Polonia deja de perseguir a las personas, fundamentalmente mujeres, que asisten a las mujeres que abortan, y creo que la pregunta que tiene que responder la ComisiÃ³n es si Von der Leyen es capaz de garantizar los derechos de las mujeres teniendo a la extrema derecha de Meloni dentro.

Socialistas, verdes, liberales, forman parte de ese pacto con la extrema derecha. Â¿Puede esa gran coaliciÃ³n de guerra garantizar el derecho al aborto legal, seguro y gratuito? Â¿O nos van a decir a las mujeres que guardemos silencio y que esperemos a otro momento porque ahora los derechos feministas generan ruido y no generan consenso?

Si la gran coaliciÃ³n de guerra le abre las puertas al machismo y a la vulneraciÃ³n de derechos, las mujeres de Europa podrÃ¡n contar con un movimiento feminista que va a conseguir educaciÃ³n sexual para decidir, anticonceptivos para no abortar y aborto legal y seguro para no morir.

(La oradora acepta responder a una pregunta formulada con arreglo al procedimiento de la Â«tarjeta azulÂ»)

Â Â Alvise PÃ©rez (NI), pregunta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â Querida Irene, gracias por tu intervenciÃ³n. En EspaÃ±a se aborta a mÃ¡s de cienÂ milÂ infantes al aÃ±o, y hay que tener en cuenta ya no la parte moral, que la compaÃ±era de VOX ha definido perfectamente, sino, sobre todo, la parte econÃ³mica: tenemos un sistema de pensiones absolutamente quebrado y necesitamos un cierto nivel de maternidad para poder financiar este sistema.

Dicho lo cual, aparte de preguntarle cÃ³mo considera usted, entonces, que podemos evitar que el sistema de pensiones en EspaÃ±a quiebre si no tenemos unos apoyos a la maternidad y todo en lo que basamos las polÃ­ticas es, bÃ¡sicamente, en el aborto, Â¿considera usted que matamos a mÃ¡s mujeres las personas que defendemos la vida o aquellas que legislan para liberar a los que las asesinan y las violan?

Â 

Â Â Irene Montero (The Left), respuesta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â Diputado, soy diputada, no querida: quÃ­tese un poquito el machismo de la cara.

Y, lo segundo, si queremos que las mujeres en Europa tengan hijos, hijas o hijes, tenemos que garantizar el derecho a la vivienda y no regalarles la vivienda de los europeos y las europeas a los fondos de inversiÃ³n; si queremos que las familias en Europa tengan hijos, hijas e hijes, necesitamos garantizar salarios dignos, necesitamos garantizar servicios pÃºblicos y no privatizarlos, y necesitamos echar a los racistas como ustedes que estÃ¡n vulnerando los derechos fundamentales de las personas que migran.

Necesitamos avanzar en derechos sociales, en derechos feministas y en polÃ­ticas antirracistas, justo lo contrario de lo que representan ustedes, los fascistas, a los que Europa va a volver a ganar, como ya ha hecho otras muchas veces en la historia.

Â Â Laurence Trochu (ECR). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, mes chers collÃ¨gues, une nouvelle fois, ce Parlement dÃ©bat dâun sujet qui ne le concerne pas. La loi polonaise sur lâavortement est lÃ©gitime dans la mesure oÃ¹ elle a Ã©tÃ© votÃ©e par une reprÃ©sentation nationale qui tient son mandat du peuple polonais et qui ne doit rendre des comptes quâÃ  lui seul.

Oui, la Pologne a le droit de choisir ses propres lois. Oui, les Polonais ont le droit de considÃ©rer que la vie de lâenfant Ã  naÃ®tre doit Ãªtre protÃ©gÃ©e, et particuliÃ¨rement celle dâun enfant handicapÃ©. Oui, la Pologne a le droit de protÃ©ger sa dÃ©mographie en limitant le recours Ã  lâavortement. Oui, la Pologne a le droit dâestimer que les avortements sont une souffrance pour les femmes et quâil faut les en prÃ©server. Et non, lâUnion europÃ©enne ne peut imposer son idÃ©ologie sans respecter les rÃ¨gles du Tribunal constitutionnel polonais, pas plus quâun rapport des NationsÂ unies fondÃ© sur lâÅuvre dâassociations militantes ne peut dicter sa loi Ã  une nation souveraine au choix souverain.

Mes chers collÃ¨gues, si la dÃ©mocratie est une valeur de lâUnion europÃ©enne, commenÃ§ons par respecter les lois des Ãtats membres.

(L'oratrice accepte une question carton bleu)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Manon Aubry (The Left), question Â«carton bleuÂ». â Vous dites, Madame Trochu, que lâavortement est une souffrance. Mais comment osez-vous? Allez dire Ã§a aux femmes polonaises et aux familles des femmes polonaises qui ont perdu la vie prÃ©cisÃ©ment parce quâelles nâont pas pu avorter alors que leur vie Ã©tait en danger.

Vous dites aussi que la vie dâun enfant Ã  naÃ®tre doit Ãªtre protÃ©gÃ©e. Et alors, que faire de la vie des femmes? Ces vies que vous menacez depuis ce Parlement europÃ©en, depuis le confort de ce Parlement europÃ©en? Voyez-vous, graver ce droit dans le marbre nâest pas un aveu de faiblesse, contrairement Ã  ce que vous dites. Câest prÃ©cisÃ©ment ce qui permet dâÃ©viter que des gens comme vous reviennent en arriÃ¨re sur nos droits fondamentaux. Et on sera toujours sur votre route.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Laurence Trochu (ECR), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Vous nâavez pas compris le sens de mon intervention, Madame Aubry. Mon intervention visait Ã  rappeler que la Pologne est un Ãtat souverain et que, en tant que tel, câest au peuple polonais de dÃ©cider des lois quâil estime bonnes pour lui.

Nous nâavons pas dâingÃ©rence Ã  faire dans les dÃ©cisions des Ãtats membres. Vous prÃ©tendez, avec vos bons sentiments, savoir mieux que tout le monde ce qui est permis, ce qui est bien, ce qui est mal, eh bien nous devrions au contraire rappeler ici quâun fonctionnement dÃ©mocratique nÃ©cessite, dans ces temps troublÃ©s par vos interventions, que les peuples souverains aient toujours le dernier mot.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Raquel GarcÃ­a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). â MadamÂ President, Commissioner, MsÂ de la Pisa CarriÃ³n, two years ago, IzabelaÂ Sajbor, aged 30, a European citizen, died because of the Polish abortion laws, because doctors would not help her when she went into premature labour and got sepsis, because they risked going to jail.

Two months ago, a United Nations expert established that the Polish abortion laws discriminate against women, constitute unjustifiable, state-sanctioned coercion, causing intense physical and mental suffering and in some cases could amount to torture, cruel, inhuman or degrading treatment. The UN experts put into writing what most of us in this House know and feel is wrong, because we cannot let women suffer and be criminalised for their reproductive choices.

Abortion bans do not lead to fewer abortions. How many more women need to suffer and die in Europe? Because before we enshrine access to safe and legal abortion in our Treaties, we owe it to Izabela, we owe it to our daughters, and we owe it to all of us.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Margarita de la Pisa CarriÃ³n (PfE), pregunta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â A mÃ­, por interpelaciones, me gustarÃ­a saber cuÃ¡l es su postura ante el aborto. Â¿Lo limitarÃ­a usted a los casos donde corre riesgo la vida de la mujer o usted habla de un aborto en otras situaciones? Â¿En quÃ© casos, por ejemplo? Â¿QuÃ© estadios? Â¿QuÃ© supuestos?

Porque creo que se manipula aquÃ­ al hablar de situaciones donde la vida de la mujer podÃ­a estar en riesgo, lo que, como hemos dicho, es un caso completamente diferente del que muchas de ustedes pretenden defender o en esta CÃ¡mara se pretende defender. TraerÂ a colaciÃ³n estos casos donde corre peligro la vida de la mujer es una manipulaciÃ³n.

Yo le pregunto: Â¿cuÃ¡l es su posiciÃ³n ante el aborto? Â¿Que se limite a los casos donde corre riesgo la vida de la mujer o un aborto libre, sin lÃ­mite de plazos, sin supuestos? Â¿Hasta cuÃ¡ndo? Â¿Hasta cuando el bebÃ© vaya a nacer? Me gustarÃ­a saber su opiniÃ³n en ese sentido.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Raquel GarcÃ­a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew), respuesta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â Mi opiniÃ³n es muy simple: mi opiniÃ³n en cuanto al aborto sigue las guÃ­as de la OrganizaciÃ³n Mundial de la Salud. Y, si no se ha leÃ­do esos informes elaborados por los mejores expertos en temas de salud reproductiva y sexual de las mujeres, le invito a que lo haga. En esos informes podrÃ¡ ver mi postura.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Benedetta Scuderi (Verts/ALE). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, la Commissione speciale delle Nazioni Unite ha detto chiaramente che la legge contro l'aborto in Polonia infligge sofferenze fisiche e psicologiche alle donne, mettendo a rischio la loro salute e le loro vite.

Ã una grave violazione dei diritti fondamentali. PerÃ², purtroppo, non Ã¨ garantito l'accesso all'aborto illimitato anche quando la legge non lo vieta. In Italia, ad esempio, l'aborto libero non Ã¨ sicuro, non Ã¨ un diritto e, troppo spesso, Ã¨ negato: liste di attesa infinite, decine di ospedali con il 100% di obiettori di coscienza, antiabortisti pagati anche da fondi europei nei luoghi della salute pubblica, che costringono a torture psicologiche chi vuole accedere all'aborto.

Troppe donne costrette a fare l'impossibile per accedere a un servizio sanitario fondamentale. Ã di nuovo l'estrema destra che prova a minacciare le nostre libertÃ . E per questo che chiediamo alla Commissione di vigilare sull'adozione di una nuova legge in Polonia, ma anche di fare quanto in suo potere perchÃ© questo diritto sia garantito in tutti gli Stati membri e di assicurarsi che fondi europei non vengano utilizzati per ostacolarlo.

PerciÃ² Ã¨ necessario inserire il diritto all'aborto nella Carta dei diritti fondamentali dell'UE: smettete di fare politica sui nostri corpi! Chi si oppone all'aborto sicuro non Ã¨ per la vita ma contro le nostre vite.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hanna Gedin (The Left). â Fru talman! Denna FN-rapport Ã¤r av stor betydelse â inte bara fÃ¶r polska kvinnor utan fÃ¶r alla vÃ¤rldens kvinnor. Den sÃ¤ger att kvinnors rÃ¤ttigheter Ã¤r mÃ¤nskliga rÃ¤ttigheter, att abortrÃ¤tten Ã¤r en mÃ¤nsklig rÃ¤ttighet.

Det borde vara en sjÃ¤lvklarhet â det Ã¤r Ã¤ndÃ¥ Ã¥r 2024 â men det Ã¤r det inte. Fortfarande tar sig mÃ¤n vÃ¤rlden Ã¶ver rÃ¤tten att bestÃ¤mma Ã¶ver vÃ¥ra kroppar, att begrÃ¤nsa vÃ¥ra liv, att utsÃ¤tta oss kvinnor fÃ¶r livsfara.

Nu Ã¤r det vÃ¥r uppgift i detta parlament att agera. RÃ¤tten till abort mÃ¥ste skrivas in i den europeiska rÃ¤ttighetsstadgan. Inga lÃ¤nder ska kunna fortsÃ¤tta att bruka vÃ¥ld mot vÃ¥ra kroppar.

Till er som sÃ¤ger att det Ã¤r komplicerat, att ni har andra invÃ¤ndningar sÃ¥ sÃ¤ger jag: SkÃ¤rp er! RÃ¤tten att starta ett fÃ¶retag Ã¤r iÂ dag inskriven i stadgan och det Ã¤r fine. Men hur kan det vara sÃ¥ att det Ã¤r viktigare Ã¤n rÃ¤tten till vÃ¥ra liv?

FÃ¶lj FN-rekommendationerna! Besluta om att abort Ã¤r en mÃ¤nsklig rÃ¤ttighet.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Maria Walsh (PPE). â Madam President, I come from a country that has endured the harsh consequences of restricting women's reproductive healthcare choices. In Ireland, there were times when pregnant women were institutionalised, deprived of their rights and subjected to deep suffering due to state-imposed restrictions on their reproductive decisions. While Ireland has made significant progress, the scars of the past still linger.

The debate today centres around Poland, where women are currently being denied access to abortion care. State involvement in women's bodies is not only unjust, it undermines their dignity. A policy designed for women should not disregard female autonomy. It ultimately invades their own choices. We know the cost of silencing female autonomy, for prioritising politics over well-being or dignity. We hear colleagues here this evening doing the same.

I urge Poland to listen, to learn from our history and to honour its commitments under the Committee on Elimination of Discrimination Against Women. Access to safe and legal abortion is not just a matter of health. It's a matter of justice and equality. And ultimately, folks, dear colleagues, dear Commission, dear Member States, we cannot fail our women and we must not fail our women. I stand here with all the women who have had to make difficult choices for themselves â and that's what it is: for themselves.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Paolo Inselvini (ECR). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, nascere, crescere, giocare, ridere, piangere, litigare, gioire, avere paura, coraggio, lottare, vincere, fallire, avere una famiglia, invecchiare, semplicemente vivere. A qualcuno tutto questo Ã¨ stato negato e viene negato ogni giorno.

PerchÃ©? PerchÃ© in modo ipocrita ed egoista vengono eliminati i piÃ¹ deboli, scartati gli indifesi e i piÃ¹ fragili. A differenza di ciÃ² che viene propagandato dalla sinistra, la legge vigente in materia di aborto in Polonia in questo momento rappresenta una speranza per tutti noi europei. La Santa Madre Teresa di Calcutta sosteneva che il piÃ¹ grande distruttore di pace oggi Ã¨ l'ingiustizia commessa contro l'innocente bimbo non nato.

PerciÃ², ricordiamoci che c'Ã¨ sempre un'alternativa e spetta a noi qui trovarla per il bene dei bambini e delle donne, non solo di chi Ã¨ qui, ma di chi verrÃ . Accogliamo la vita anche qui al Parlamento europeo con amore e con il coraggio richiamato da Papa Francesco di esempi come Re Baldovino. E se davvero vogliamo la pace, come ci diciamo spesso in quest'Aula, allora diamo voce a tutti coloro che una voce non l'hanno potuta avere.

(L'oratore accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

Â Â

Â Â Hilde Vautmans (Renew), âblauwe kaartâ-vraag. â Collega, ik heb uw betoog hier gehoord en vind het eigenlijk een echte schande. Het is duidelijk dat u een man bent. U zegt hier dat het beleid in Polen een voorbeeld is voor heel Europa. Daar ben ik het niet mee eens. Ik zou u Ã©Ã©n ding willen zeggen: stel dat u een vrouw bent, u wordt brutaal verkracht en u moet dan negen maanden een kind dragen, baren en in liefde opvoeden. Hoe gaat u dat doen? Ik vind uw betoog hier een echte schande voor Europa.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Paolo Inselvini (ECR), risposta a una domanda "cartellino blu". â Io credo, onorevole, che le sue affermazioni, come tante delle affermazioni fatte dalla sinistra, confermino la vostra visione ideologica. PerchÃ© ci si dimentica che ciÃ² che sta nove mesi nella pancia delle madri Ã¨ una persona!

Bisognerebbe semplicemente riconoscere la realtÃ  e la veritÃ , non la mia opinione o quella dei partiti di centro-destra, ma la veritÃ  di questo mondo.

Per cui, grazie per avermi fatto sottolineare ancora una volta la vostra ideologia e la vostra malafede.

Â Â Robert BiedroÅ (S&D), blue-card question. â I fully support your statement, but your colleague from Renew just proposed that there should be a referendum organised on abortion. I never found any referendum in our political culture, you come from Slovakia, I come from Poland. We never organised referendums on human rights, whether it's on abortion or death penalty.

I fully agree with your group to put abortion in the Charter of Fundamental Rights. I want to know then, in your opinion, whether you support organising referendums on human rights or whether putting it as a right, which should be not questionable even in referendum?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lucia Yar (Renew), blue-card answer. â Thank you for this question and I will speak for myself in this case. I don't think that fundamental rights should be put into referendum. I don't think that women or men should be deciding on what's happening in such a way. And definitely I know that in Poland, at least 50% of inhabitants are women, so I'm sure that another half cannot be deciding on them. So, personally speaking, I don't think the referendum is a good idea.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Lina GÃ¡lvez (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, el ComitÃ© de las Naciones Unidas para la EliminaciÃ³n de la DiscriminaciÃ³n contra la Mujer es contundente en su informe sobre el aborto en Polonia: con la actual ley del aborto âque el Gobierno de Tusk no ha cambiadoâ Polonia ha discriminado a las mujeres, a sus derechos, ha dado lugar a traumas, muertes, miedos y amenazas para los especialistas y las activistas.

Las conclusiones de este informe son claras: Polonia debe legalizar y despenalizar totalmente el aborto. En tanto no se haga realidad, debe introducir una moratoria y poner fin a todas las detenciones, investigaciones y procesos penales relacionados con los profesionales de la salud âparticularmente mujeresâ que asisten a las mujeres que necesitan abortar.

La salud y los derechos sexuales y reproductivos son parte integral de los derechos humanos, bÃ¡sicos para la igualdad de gÃ©nero, para la democracia y para el proyecto europeo. Por ello, las y los socialistas seguimos pidiendo que los derechos sexuales y reproductivos âinsisto, derechos, porque Ãºltimamente veo que muchos grupos en esta CÃ¡mara quieren quitar la palabra Â«derechosÂ» de los derechos sexuales y reproductivosâ se incluyan en la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la UniÃ³n Europea.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Birgit Sippel (S&D). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Vor gut einem Jahr verhalf eine sehr hohe Wahlbeteiligung in Polen Donald Tusk zum MachtwechselÂ â insbesondere durch die Beteiligung junger Menschen und vieler Frauen. Diese Frauen gingen fÃ¼r das Ende der PiS-Herrschaft an die Wahlurne, aber auch fÃ¼r das Wahlversprechen, SchwangerschaftsabbrÃ¼che innerhalb von 100Â Tagen sicher und legal zugÃ¤nglich zu machen.

Nach beinahe 400Â Tagen hat sich quasi nichts geÃ¤ndert. Schlimmer: Tusk hat das Thema fÃ¼r diese Amtszeit ad acta gelegt, und somit herrscht in Polen weiterhin ein Abtreibungsunrecht. Autonome Entscheidungen Ã¼ber ihren eigenen KÃ¶rper werden Frauen verwehrt undÂ â meist mÃ¤nnlichenÂ â Ãrzten und StaatsanwÃ¤lten Ã¼berlassen.

Das alles ist respektlos. Es entmÃ¼ndigt Frauen, und es zwingt Frauen dazu, heimlich abzutreiben und dabei ihr Leben zu riskieren. Diese Frauenleben und die vielen enttÃ¤uschten Hoffnungen auf VerÃ¤nderungen haben Sie, Herr Tusk, zu verantworten. Noch kÃ¶nnten Sie das Ã¤ndern.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Elisabeth Grossmann (S&D). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Frau Kommissarin, meine sehr geehrten Damen und Herren! Das Recht auf Selbstbestimmung Ã¼ber den eigenen KÃ¶rper ist ein Menschenrecht, Ã¼ber das jede Frau in Europa frei verfÃ¼gen soll, und deshalb ist das EuropÃ¤ische Parlament sehr wohl der richtige Ort, das zu debattieren. Ein faktisches Abtreibungsverbot stellt eine groÃe Gefahr fÃ¼r die psychische und die physische Gesundheit von Frauen dar. Und nicht nur in Polen, sondern in allen europÃ¤ischen LÃ¤ndern brauchen wir einen sicheren Zugang zu legalen Abtreibungen, wohnortnah und leistbar.

Keine Frau setzt diesen Schritt leichtfertig. Und diese Frauen in dieser schwierigen Situation mit MÃ¶rderinnen gleichzusetzen, wie das heute hier geschehen ist durch einige RednerinnenÂ â das ist eine skandalÃ¶se Entgleisung!

Es geht auch darum, ungewollte Schwangerschaften zu vermeiden. Deshalb braucht es auch in ganz Europa kostenlose VerhÃ¼tungsmittel, zumindest fÃ¼r Jugendliche und Einkommensschwache, und auch flÃ¤chendeckend eine hochwertige SexualaufklÃ¤rung.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Evin Incir (S&D). â Madam President, colleagues, it's outrageous that we continue to face backlash regarding our rights, women's rights within the EU. What we choose to do with our bodies should not be dictated by men or women that hate women.

No woman has ever sought to control what men do with their bodies. So why do we still encounter this outdated mentality and obsession with regulating women's autonomy? The report on Poland's abortion laws highlights the severe discrimination faced by women in Poland and the immense suffering and challenges imposed by the laws in the country.

To all the women haters here who believe they know better than us women what we need: how are you any different from the mullahs in Iran when you seek to imprison and punish women simply for wanting independence and the right to make decisions about our own bodies? These laws in Poland target women specifically, stripping us of our bodily autonomy. Enough is enough.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Margarita de la Pisa CarriÃ³n (PfE), pregunta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â Has comentado que los hombres pueden decidir hacer lo que quieran. Yo creo que eso no es del todo cierto, Â¿no? Si una mujer toma la decisiÃ³n de seguir adelante con su embarazo y tener ese bebÃ© âalgo que ocurre y yo estoy de acuerdo con elloâ, el padre tiene la obligaciÃ³n de hacerse cargo del bebÃ©.

Entonces, si asumimos la obligaciÃ³n del padre de hacerse cargo de ese bebÃ©, Â¿por quÃ© piensas que no tiene que opinar, en este caso, del aborto y que es una cosa solamente de mujeres? Perdonad, es una lecciÃ³n bÃ¡sica de biologÃ­a, pero los niÃ±os tienen un padre y una madre.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Evin Incir (S&D), blue-card answer. â Madam President, the last time I checked, it's not the man who carries the child. It is the woman who carries the child. So the person who carries the child has the right to decide what the person does with their body.

Nobody else â you or any other person â has the right to choose for that person what to do. And don't forget, there is really a special place for women who don't support other women.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alessandra Moretti (S&D). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, sono giÃ  sette le donne morte in Polonia a causa del divieto di accedere all'interruzione di gravidanza.

La legge polacca Ã¨ una delle piÃ¹ restrittive d'Europa, una legge che non solo limita la libertÃ  delle donne, ma condiziona pesantemente il dovere dei medici di prendersi cura delle persone.

Criminalizzare l'aborto non elimina l'aborto, ma lo rende clandestino e pericoloso per le donne. Un'intrusione illegittima della politica che ricorda un dibattito di questi giorni in cui una ministra italiana arriva addirittura a chiedere ai medici di comportarsi come spie.

Abbiamo tutti sperato che con il cambio di governo qualcosa in Polonia cambiasse: non Ã¨ stato cosÃ¬. La destra ha deluso ancora donne e ragazze, che sono scese in piazza in Polonia e in tutta Europa, per ricordare che ad ognuna deve essere garantito il diritto fondamentale di autodeterminarsi.

Non si tratta solo di diritti sanitari, di servizi sanitari, si tratta di diritti fondamentali difesi nello Stato di diritto. Sappiate che noi donne non vi permetteremo piÃ¹ di decidere sui nostri corpi.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Catch-the-eye procedure

Â Â Juan Fernando LÃ³pez Aguilar (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, Â¿por quÃ© estamos discutiendo en el Parlamento Europeo de las leyes restrictivas de la interrupciÃ³n del embarazo en Polonia?

Es cierto que hay aspectos de la legislaciÃ³n penal y del Derecho de familia que continÃºan siendo competencia de los Estados miembros, pero tambiÃ©n lo es que en el Parlamento Europeo pusimos en marcha un Marco del Estado de Derecho de la UE para que por la regresiÃ³n que tuvo lugar en Polonia de la mano de un Gobierno ultra que restringiÃ³ ese derecho de las mujeres se le activara el procedimiento del artÃ­culo 7 del Tratado de la UniÃ³n Europea por violaciÃ³n grave de los valores fundacionales del Estado de Derecho, y es cierto que en el Marco del Estado de Derecho de la UE se consagra el principio de no regresiÃ³n, es decir, que no se pueden producir legislaciones que restrinjan derechos que estuvieran previamente establecidos.

Yo estoy convencido de que la interrupciÃ³n voluntaria del embarazo es un derecho humano, porque los derechos sexuales y reproductivos de las mujeres son derechos fundamentales y humanos. SÃ© que no es fÃ¡cil cambiar una ConstituciÃ³n ârequiere mayorÃ­a cualificadaâ ni es fÃ¡cil cambiar la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la UniÃ³n Europea âque requiere nada menos que la unanimidad de los Estados miembrosâ, pero sÃ­, este Parlamento Europeo puede expresar con claridad que esa regresiÃ³n, este retroceso de derechos fundamentales, es completamente contraria a los valores europeos y el Derecho europeo.

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Hohes Haus! Vorab mÃ¶chte ich die Frage in den Raum werfen, ob wir Ã¼berhaupt noch eine Debatte Ã¼ber Abtreibung fÃ¼hren mÃ¼ssten, wenn in den letzten Jahrhunderten die Positionen der Macht mehrheitlich von Frauen besetzt gewesen wÃ¤ren.

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members, as I mentioned earlier, I stress again that this is an area for Member States to legislate in. Member States are also responsible for defining their health policies and for the organisation and delivery of health services and medical care. When doing so, they must respect the rights enshrined in their national constitutions and their commitments under international law, such as the European Convention on Human Rights.

The European Court of Human Rights has repeatedly recognised that the lack of access to abortion services can violate the right to family and private life. In addition, Article 35 of the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights provides that everyone in the EU has the right to access to preventive health care and the right to benefit from medical treatment under the conditions established by national laws and practices.

As the European Parliament stressed in its 2024 resolution, restricting sexual and reproductive health and rights, obstructs the fulfilment of people's rights, hinders countries' development and undermines democracy and European values.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â Thank you very much, Commissioner Dalli, for this. And with that, we close the debate.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Marc Angel (S&D), in writing. â Following the last parliamentary elections, Poland has woken up with a glimmer of hope for change â a Poland that believes in gender equality and in women's right to self-determination over their own body. This hope has materialised through progressive politicians who dared to assert and defend that gender equality is a human right that cannot be sold off. Today, this same mantra must guide these politicians.

Dear colleagues, do not disappoint your citizens or they will lose trust in politics! Restricting abortion rights for political, macho and outdated reasons is, above all, mistreating entire families without taking into account the distress of these women, girls, mothers and wives. It will only serve the church by ignoring the fact that thousands of women will resort to it in precarious sanitary conditions, thus increasing the risk of death.

Dear colleagues, keep the hope alive and deliver the right to abortion for all â now!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â

13. Seven years from the assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia: lack of progress in restoring the rule of law in Malta (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is the debate on the Commission statement entitled 'Seven years from the assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia: lack of progress in restoring the rule of law in Malta' (2024/2868(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, membre de la Commission. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Mesdames et Messieurs les dÃ©putÃ©s, je vous remercie tout dâabord dâavoir inscrit Ã  lâordre du jour de la plÃ©niÃ¨re ce dÃ©bat sur la situation de lâÃ©tat de droit Ã  Malte, sept ans aprÃ¨s lâassassinat de Daphne Caruana Galizia. Permettez-moi de rÃ©affirmer que la Commission a condamnÃ© Ã  plusieurs reprises lâassassinat de Daphne Caruana Galizia, qui reprÃ©sente une attaque contre la libertÃ© de la presse et souligne la nÃ©cessitÃ© dâun renforcement de la protection des journalistes et, plus gÃ©nÃ©ralement, de lâÃ©tat de droit. La Commission salue le courage et lâengagement de la famille de Daphne Caruana Galizia et de la sociÃ©tÃ© civile maltaise.Â Nous attendons des autoritÃ©s et des tribunaux maltais quâils fassent tout le nÃ©cessaire pour queÂ tous les responsables soient poursuivis en justice.

De maniÃ¨re plus gÃ©nÃ©rale, sâagissant de la situation de lâÃ©tat de droit Ã  Malte, la Commission entretient une bonne coopÃ©ration avec les autoritÃ©s maltaises dans le cadre du cycle annuel relatif Ã  lâÃ©tat de droit.

The 2024 Rule of Law Report contains an in-depth analysis of the main developments concerning the rule of law in Malta.Â The report underlines that Malta has taken steps to solve certain issues, but efforts are still needed to address several recommendations.

Regarding the area of justice, Malta is taking up one of the first recommendations that the Commission made in the Rule of Law Report by starting an important reform process to involve the judiciary in the appointment procedure of the Chief Justice. This is a positive development on which we encourage Malta too.

At the same time, efficiency of justice is crucial to ensure that the rule of law is upheld and to strengthen the trust of citizens in justice systems. The Commission has recommended to Malta to take steps to improve the efficiency of justice; specifically, further steps are needed to improve the level of human resources. The length of proceedings remain far too long to ensure the efficiency of justice.

As regards anti-corruption, the report notes that measures have been taken to improve the capacity of certain investigative authorities, the Attorney General and the Permanent Commission Against Corruption. Although there was some progress, further efforts are needed to reduce the length of investigations of high-level corruption cases and to establish a robust track record of final judgements, as the Commission recommended.

Moreover, the anti-corruption recommendations issued following the public inquiry into the assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia have not been implemented yet.

As to media freedom, while some progress has been made in the consultation process in relation to safeguards to improve the working conditions of journalists, no legislative measures have been adopted, and there has been no progress on reforming the regime regulating access to official documents.

Furthermore, there have been no developments regarding the measures needed to strengthen the rules and mechanisms to enhance the independent governance and editorial independence of public service media. We cannot enjoy freedom and democracy without an independent media landscape and an adequate protection of journalists. The Commission made recommendations to several Member States, including Malta, to strengthen media freedom.

The European Union has also taken action â a directive and a recommendation â to protect journalists and civil society from abusive litigation, the so-called SLAPPs. This initiative was largely inspired by the fact that, at the time of her assassination, Daphne Caruana Galizia was facing more than 40 SLAPPs. For this reason, many stakeholders refer to this directive as Daphne's Law. Malta has already transposed the directive.

As to other institutional issues, reforms to establish a national human rights institution and to introduce a formal framework for public participation in the legislative process are pending. The report reiterates recommendations in this regard for Malta.

Pour conclure, Malte a avancÃ© sur certains dossiers, et la Commission lâencourage Ã  suivre cette voie Ã©galement pour les recommandations sur lesquelles des progrÃ¨s sont encore Ã  rÃ©aliser. Nous sommes prÃªts Ã  soutenir ces efforts, je lâai dit en commenÃ§ant. Nous souhaitons aussi, dans le cas concret de lâassassinat de Daphne Caruana Galizia, que tous les responsables soient poursuivis en justice. Nous sommes toujours Ã  la disposition des autoritÃ©s maltaises pour coopÃ©rer avec elles, comme lâa fait Europol dans le courant de la procÃ©dure.

Je vous remercie Ã©videmment Ã  nouveau pour ce dÃ©bat et suis maintenant Ã  lâÃ©coute de vos interventions.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â David Casa, f'isem il-grupp PPE. â Sinjura President, gÄ§addew seba' snin minn meta assassinaw lil Daphne. Qtil illi skont inkjesta pubblika, kien possibbli minÄ§abba kultura ta' impunitÃ  li teÅ¼isti fil-pajjiÅ¼. Serq u korruzzjoni. TÄ§anÅ¼ir sfrenat minn fuq dahar il-poplu bla konsegwenza. Hija impunitÃ  li l-kriminali tant gÄ§amlithom arroganti illi assassinaw lill-Ä¡urnalista l-aktar segwita f'Malta, fid-dawl tax-xemx, u sfortunatament dik l-impunitÃ  gÄ§adha magÄ§na illum. GÄ§andna Gvern illi jikkorrompi kulma jmiss b'idejh, li pereÅ¼empju, Ä§alla lil min seraq minn fuq dahar il-pazjenti tal-kanÄer, illi Ä§alla lil min jiffroda pagamenti minn persuni b'diÅ¼abilitÃ , u li biegÄ§ l-identitÃ  Maltija u Ewropea lil nies ta' pajjiÅ¼i terzi. U meta l-Ä¡ustizzja tiÄÄaqlaq, tiÄÄaqlaq minÄ§abba xogÄ§ol ta' persuni privati, tiÄÄaqlaq minÄ§abba l-Ä¡urnalisti u l-Ä¡udikatura li qiegÄ§da tagÄ§mel id-dover tagÄ§ha. Il-Ä¡ustizzja jekk tiÄÄaqlaq, tiÄÄaqlaq minkejja l-Ä§akma fuq il-pulizija u l-istituzzjonijiet ta' pajjiÅ¼na.

U r-risposta x'inhi, meta l-Ä¡ustizzja toqrob lejn il-korrotti, bÄ§alma huwa Dottor Joseph Muscat, l-eks Prim Ministru ta' Malta? Attakk oxxen fuq il-Ä¡udikatura. Attakk fuq il-Ä¡urnalisti. Attakk fuq is-soÄjetÃ  Äivili u dawn l-attakki jsiru mill-Gvern preÅ¼enti.Â Il-Maltin jixirqilhom aÄ§jar, u l-Ewropej jixirqilhom aÄ§jar. GÄ§aliex il-Gvern Malti qed ifalli bl-ikrah, kemm biex jimplimenta r-riÅ¼ultati tal-inkjesta pubblika u anke biex ikun hemm Ä¡ustizzja gÄ§all-korruzzjoni illi kixfet Daphne.

Le Sur Kummissarju, tgÄ§idilniex iktar fuq is-saltna tad-dritt u r-rapport li qed titkellem dwarhom. Issa jmiss azzjoni u mhux iktar rapporti, gÄ§aliex ir-restawr tas-saltna tad-dritt, tal-istituzzjonijiet illi jipproteÄ¡u lin-nies u mhux lill-korrotti huwa l-unika legat xieraq li jista' jonora lil Daphne Caruana Galizia. Issa ddejjaqna mill-Kummissjoni illi titkellem biss, issa hemm bÅ¼onn naraw li l-Kummissjoni taÄ¡ixxi u mhux iktar titkellem.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alex Agius Saliba, f'isem il-grupp S&D. â Sinjura President, filwaqt illi nixtieq nibda billi nsellem il-memorja tal-Ä¡urnalista Daphne Caruana Galizia, illum ma nistax ma nikkummentax fuq l-ipokrezija u d-double standards ta' dan il-Parlament.

Filwaqt illi llum qegÄ§din intellgÄ§u l-Ä§mistax-il dibattitu fuq il-qtil tal-Ä¡urnalista Daphne Caruana Galizia, gÄ§ax gÄ§all-Grupp tal-Popolari dawn iÄ§ossu illi meta jkunu qegÄ§din jagÄ§mlu hekk, kif rajna minn Malti bÄ§alna, David Casa, li meta tattakka lill-Gvern SoÄjalista tkun qed tirbaÄ§ xi ftit punti politiÄi, dan il-Parlament, dan l-istess Parlament gÄ§adu sieket, gÄ§adu mutu u gÄ§adu trux quddiem il-massakru ta' mija u erbgÄ§in Ä¡urnalist f'GaÅ¼a u l-Libanu matul din l-aÄ§Ä§ar sena biss. Ma rajna l-ebda dibattitu. Ma rajna l-ebda diskussjoni. U l-messaÄ¡Ä¡ illi qed jintbagÄ§at minn dan il-Parlament huwa li gÄ§alina hemm Ä¡urnalisti tal-ewwel u tat-tieni klassi.

Fil-kaÅ¼ ta' Daphne Caruana Galizia, il-mandant u l-kriminali kollha wara l-qtil brutali tagÄ§ha jinsabu kollha kemm huma arrestati, u l-maÄ¡Ä¡oranza tagÄ§hom diÄ¡Ã  qed jaffaÄÄjaw pieni twal ta' priÄ¡unerija. Il-Gvern, kif qalet ukoll il-Kummissjoni, diÄ¡Ã  wettaq riformi waÄ§da wara l-oÄ§ra sabiex isaÄ§Ä§aÄ§ l-istituzzjonijiet, is-saltna tad-dritt. Riformi, illi anke l-Kummissjoni Ewropea, illum stess, faÄ§Ä§ret li qegÄ§din nimbuttaw 'il quddiem. U allura nappella lil dan il-Parlament sabiex jiffoka fuq il-vera problemi ta' Ä¡urnalisti illi qegÄ§din jaffaÄÄjaw madwarna. Mija u erbgÄ§in Ä¡urnalist maqtula ...

(Il-President irtirat il-kelma lill-kelliem)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Fabrice Leggeri, au nom du groupe PfE. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, il y a sept ans, Daphne Caruana Galizia Ã©tait assassinÃ©e. Pourquoi? Parce quâelle avait osÃ© dÃ©voiler les dessous de la corruption Ã  Malte. Corruption qui âÂ surprise, surprise!Â â impliquerait des responsables du gouvernement socialiste. Oui, voilÃ  ce que lâon tente dâÃ©touffer. Pendant ce temps, lâenquÃªte nâavance pas. Sept ans dâomerta. Sept ans de silence coupable.

Rendons hommage Ã  cette journaliste, comme lâa fait RobertaÂ Metsola. Mais soyons honnÃªtes: câest un cauchemar dÃ©mocratique que lâUnion europÃ©enne laisse assassiner des journalistes sur son propre sol. Quelle honte! On nous parle sans cesse dâÃ©tat de droit et de valeurs europÃ©ennes, mais quand il sâagit de protÃ©ger les petits copains du systÃ¨me, soudainement Bruxelles se tait. Et que dire de cette enquÃªte, qui nâaboutit Ã  rien? Pourquoi? Parce que des figures du pouvoir socialiste maltais seraient peut-Ãªtre impliquÃ©es? Alors on prÃ©fÃ¨re fermer les yeux, Ã©touffer lâaffaire et laisser pourrir la justice. Câest cela lâÃ©tat de droit version Union europÃ©enne. Deux poids, deux mesures.

Quand la Commission europÃ©enne sâamuse Ã  faire la police chez des gouvernements souverains, comme en Pologne ou en Hongrie, elle oublie de regarder ses alliÃ©s socialistes. Quand on est complaisant avec la corruption qui tue, on nâa pas de leÃ§ons Ã  donner. La Commission ferait bien de commencer par balayer devant sa porte. Assez de ce cirque hypocrite! On sâacharne contre des gouvernements lÃ©gitimes Ã©lus par leur peuple alors que lâon passe sous silence les turpitudes dâautres gouvernements corrompus.

Le vrai scandale, il est lÃ : des Ã©lites protÃ©gÃ©es, une justice Ã  deux vitesses. Pendant ce temps, les peuples europÃ©ens perdent confiance, car ils voient bien que ceux qui gouvernent ne sont lÃ  que pour servir leurs propres intÃ©rÃªts, pas ceux de leurs citoyens. Alors Ã  la Commission europÃ©enne je dis: Â«ArrÃªtez de vous mÃªler des affaires des nations souveraines et commencez par mettre de lâordre dans vos propres rangs.Â»

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alessandro Ciriani, a nome del gruppo ECR. â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, se un uomo non Ã¨ disposto a correre dei rischi per le sue idee, o le sue idee non valgono nulla o non vale niente lui. Questa frase di Pound tratteggia perfettamente lo spirito di Daphne Caruana Galizia, giornalista coraggiosa che fece della vita e della ricerca di veritÃ  una cosa sola, al punto di anteporre la vita stessa alla lotta alla corruzione del suo Paese.

Una battaglia difficilissima per rompere il vaso di Pandora delle commistioni tra governo e malaffare, che prima le costÃ² dichiarazioni di odio, poi una bomba, un ordigno con il quale si voleva che illecite corruzioni restassero nascoste, continuando a schiacciare il corpo di Malta.

Dopo sette anni, sulla vicenda di Daphne ancora molte domande sono senza risposta e occorre chiedere con forza risultati significativi del governo maltese di miglioramento del sistema giudiziario e delle libertÃ  sostanziali.

Onorare la memoria, comunque, significa tenere viva la fiamma che arde nei cuori di giornalisti immuni da ogni condizionamento, testimoni della funzione sociale e civile e dell'informazione che smaschera mafie, prepotenze, sistemi criminali. L'informazione Ã¨ libertÃ , soprattutto laddove questa Ã¨ fragile o in ostaggio.

Parafrasando JÃ¼nger ricordando tutti coloro che hanno dato la vita per amore di veritÃ , costoro hanno vissuto un tempo in cui bisognava essere coraggiosi e possedere coraggio significa essere all'altezza di qualsiasi destino.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Moritz KÃ¶rner, on behalf of the Renew Group. â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, today we remember Daphne Caruana Galizia, a brave and tireless journalist whose voice was silenced because she dared to expose the truth. Daphne wasn't just reporting stories. She was fighting for justice, for accountability, for the very soul of our democracy. And for that, she paid the ultimate price.

Her brutal assassination is a chilling reminder for Europe. When Daphne was murdered, that blow wasn't just against her. It was a blow to media freedom across our continent. It was a message meant to instill fear, to silence those who hold power to account. But we cannot allow that. Media freedom is not just another value. It is a cornerstone of our democracies. Without free and independent media, the foundations of justice and transparency begin to crumble, corruption thrives, and the powerful can act unchecked in darkness.

We must unite to protect media freedom, not just for the sake of journalists, but for the sake of democracy itself. Let us honour Daphne's legacy by safeguarding what she fought for: truth, justice and the unyielding right to speak freely.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Daniel Freund, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â Madam President, dear colleagues, when Daphne Caruana Galizia was killed with a car bomb seven years ago, it was a shock. A journalist murdered for her work and her investigations into corruption and organised crime right in the European Union. And while I'm still deeply disturbed by that, there was always a little bit of hope that at least the international outcry, the attention, would bring about change in Malta and that the murderers would all be convicted.

But I have to say, seven years on, on both fronts, we have seen too little progress. The mastermind behind the murder of Daphne has still not been convicted today and the Maltese Government still fails to implement all the international recommendations from GRECO, from the Venice Commission and the European Commission, but also from Malta's own public inquiry.

And since the transposition of the SLAPP legislation was mentioned, the way that the Maltese Government implemented it wouldn't have helped Daphne because national SLAPPs are not covered by the legislation. How is this possible? We need to keep up the pressure so that there is real change. I think that's the way to honour Daphne â that things change in Malta.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Î ÏÏÏÎ¸ÎµÏÎ· ÏÎ·Ï Î´Î¿Î»Î¿ÏÎ¿Î½Î¯Î±Ï ÏÎ·Ï Dafni Caruana Galizia ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ Î­Î½Î± Î¼Î½Î·Î¼ÏÏÏÎ½Î¿ Î® ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ ÎµÏÎ±Î»ÏÎ®ÏÎ¹Î¿ Î½Î± Î±Î»Î»Î¬Î¾Î¿ÏÎ¼Îµ ÏÎ± ÏÏÎ¬Î³Î¼Î±ÏÎ± ÏÏÎ·Î½ ÎÎ½ÏÏÎ® Î¼Î±Ï;

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ana Miguel Pedro (PPE). â Madam President, today we remember Daphne Galizia, who was brutally assassinated for exposing corruption and defending the truth. Her legacy lives on, but in Malta the culture of impunity persists.

Malta's recent history reminds us of the massive power of citizens and journalists in the fight for justice. Their efforts led to the courts declaring the EURÂ 4Â billion hospital privatisation deal fraudulent. Because of them, former Prime Minister Muscat and his associates are facing criminal charges linked to this web of deceit. Their courage has exposed schemes from the abuse of disability payments to fraudulent ID cards that aim to exploit the most vulnerable.

Yet despite these victories, the Maltese State has failed to implement a single recommendation from the public inquiry into Daphne's assassination. Not one conviction has been secured for the corruption she exposed. When institutions are taken captive, justice stumbles and journalists become the last line of defence. This is the reality we face in Malta today and we cannot turn a blind eye.

It's on those who believe in justice, fairness and the rule of law to stand up and call for change. We must support the fight for reform and insist that no journalist, no citizen, ever faces the threat that Daphne did. We honour her memory â yes â but more than that, we owe it to every European who believes in truth, accountability and democracy.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Fernando LÃ³pez Aguilar (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±or comisario, una vez mÃ¡s este Parlamento rinde tributo a la periodista de investigaciÃ³n Daphne Caruana, en el sÃ©ptimoÂ aniversario de su asesinato, y rinde igualmente tributo a la media docena de periodistas que han sido asesinados en otros Estados miembros de la UniÃ³n Europea, en la RepÃºblica Checa, los PaÃ­ses Bajos o Grecia, porque de lo que aquÃ­ se trata es de la protecciÃ³n de la libertad informativa, del pluralismo informativo, y no de atacar a Malta, como obsesivamente se escucha desde los escaÃ±os de la derecha simplemente porque en Malta hay un Gobierno que no es de su color ni de su complacencia.

De lo que aquÃ­ se trata es de extraer lecciones de la experiencia, y asÃ­ ha hecho este Parlamento Europeo, legislando nada menos que con la Directiva sobre demandas estratÃ©gicas contra la participaciÃ³n pÃºblica, al objeto de proteger a los periodistas de acciones intimidatorias, y con el Reglamento Europeo sobre la Libertad de los Medios de ComunicaciÃ³n, que garantiza transparencia y daciÃ³n de cuentas en la publicidad institucional, en la financiaciÃ³n y en la titularidad de los medios de comunicaciÃ³n.

Y, por tanto, sirva este recordatorio para seÃ±alar que el Parlamento tiene el deber de proteger la libertad de informaciÃ³n y el pluralismo informativo, que son derechos fundamentales protegidos por la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la UniÃ³n Europea. Y de eso es de lo que se trata, de nada mÃ¡s que de eso.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sophie WilmÃ¨s (Renew). â Madam President, almost every year since 2017 â the tragic assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia â the European Parliament has adopted a resolution calling for more efficient and faster justice, especially in the most serious cases of corruption. The climate of impunity cannot be allowed.

I met this morning with the son of Daphne Caruana Galizia, who, in addition to his grief, has to live with this sense of injustice, especially since the alleged mastermind behind the assassination of his mother has still not been tried after seven years and as the pre-trial detention could come to an end.

Since 2017, the European Parliament 'laments', 'regrets', 'reminds', 'expresses concerns about' the length of proceedings. But the real question to me is: when will the Rule of Law Report have more teeth?

CommissionerÂ Reynders, you have done so much for the rule of law, and thank you for that. But it is clear to me that the Rule of Law Report should include binding recommendations with a clear deadline for action, with a systematic link with European funds and programmes as well. We owe Daphne Caruana Galizia this much at least.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gaetano Pedulla' (The Left). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, a sette anni dall'omicidio della giornalista Daphne Caruana Galizia, pure l'ultima relazione della Commissione europea sullo Stato di diritto riconosce il perdurare di forti violazioni alla libertÃ  di informazione a Malta, cosÃ¬ come in altri Paesi dell'Unione.

In questo emiciclo facciamo bene a parlarne, a riconoscere premi ai cronisti piÃ¹ coraggiosi, ma questo non basta a cambiare le cose. Un nuovo passo necessario sta nel superare la rassegnazione all'assassinio dei giornalisti d'inchiesta, al pari di quelli che operano nelle aree di guerra, rinunciando a considerare queste morti un effetto collaterale della loro professione.

In un'iniziativa promossa di recente proprio in questo Parlamento da Movimento Cinque Stelle, The Left e S&D giornalisti ucraini, palestinesi, le associazioni internazionali di categoria hanno posto il tema del sostegno economico ai cronisti feriti, alle famiglie dei colleghi uccisi. Sono quasi 150, ricordo solo a Gaza in un anno.

Per questo mi faccio portavoce della richiesta alla Commissione di istituire uno strumento â anche all'interno del bilancio comunitario â per dare assistenza economica, medica e legale agli operatori dell'informazione europei, o che lavorano comunque con testate e agenzie di stampa in Europa. Diamo una mano, serve a tutti noi.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Judita LaÅ¡Å¡Ã¡kovÃ¡ (NI). â VÃ¡Å¾enÃ¡ pani predsedajÃºca, vÃ¡Å¾enÃ­ kolegovia, v prÃ­pade atentÃ¡tu na novinÃ¡rku sa bavÃ­me o treste smrti za vÃ½kon Äinnosti. NepozerÃ¡me sa na korupciu na Malte Äi na korupciu v Holandsku, kde boli za bieleho dÅa popravenÃ­ novinÃ¡ri. PÃ¡n Reynders, pozrite sa do zrkadla! Iba PlatÃ³nova starostlivosÅ¥ o duÅ¡u, oddanosÅ¥ pravde a tÃºÅ¾ba po spravodlivosti mÃ´Å¾e zachrÃ¡niÅ¥ naÅ¡u EurÃ³pu.

Â Â Peter Agius (PPE). â Sinjura President, Daphne gÄ§alija kienet Ä§abiba. Kienet Ä§abiba taâ Malta. XogÄ§olha kien servizz gÄ§ad-demokrazija.

Il-qtil taâ Daphne fil-fatt huwa ferita gÄ§al Malta. Id-domanda li rridu nsaqsu llum hija kif ser infejqu dik il-ferita, gÄ§ax il-ferita gÄ§adha miftuÄ§a beraÄ§!

Kellna inkjesta pubblika li qaltilna li hemm bÅ¼onn reati Ä¡odda biex niddiljaw mal-korruzzjoni Ä¡o Malta, fosthom reat dwar l-unexplained wealth. Taf x'gÄ§amel il-Gvern? EÅ¼empju ieÄ§or - l-inkjesta qaltilna li hemm bÅ¼onn nipproteÄ¡u l-Ä¡urnalisti. X'gÄ§amel il-Gvern? Tafu li qed jikkunsidra li jdaÄ§Ä§al il-libell kriminali? X'ÄuÄ huma l-iscoreboards? Libell kriminali! Mela sewwa, mela lill-Ä¡urnalisti jrid jitfagÄ§hom il-Ä§abs, u lill-politiÄi korrotti jara kif jipproteÄ¡ihom.

NagÄ§laq bâsejÄ§a lill-Kummissjoni Ewropea, bÄ§alma gÄ§amel il-Ä§abib tiegÄ§i David Casa. InÅ¼gÄ§u l-ingwanti tal-bellus, u rridu azzjoni konkreta fuq l-istat tad-dritt u libertÃ  tal-istampa Ä¡o Malta.

Â Â Daniel Attard (S&D). â Sinjura President, il-mod brutali li bih inqatlet Daphne Caruana Galizia u Ä¡urnalisti oÄ§rajn, bÄ§al JÃ¡n Kuciak u de Vries, huwa kundannabbli fâkull sens. Minn dakinhar, apparti l-arresti, Malta bidlet is-sistema Ä¡udizzjarja, saÄ§Ä§et l-indipendenza tal-pulizija, u daÄ§Ä§let riformi li frankament jistgÄ§u jkunu ta' eÅ¼empju gÄ§al Ä§addieÄ§or. Imma naturalment il-fatti ma jfissru xejn gÄ§al min gÄ§andu l-istorja tiegÄ§u diÄ¡Ã  miktuba.

Li ma nistax naÄÄetta hi l-ipokrezija, gÄ§ax filwaqt li din il-Kamra ma titlifx okkaÅ¼joni biex tispezzjona lil Malta, tibqaâ siekta fuq il-mija u tletin Ä¡urnalist illi nqatlu fâGaÅ¼a. Tkellmu fuq Malta, mela le, forsi gÄ§ax aÄ§na Å¼gÄ§ar. Ma nistgÄ§ux ngÄ§idu li qed tiddefendu l-istampa Ä§ielsa filwaqt illi nibqgÄ§u siekta, jekk mhux niddiskriminaw kontra Ä¡urnalisti oÄ§rajn illi nqatlu f'pajjiÅ¼i oÄ§rajn, fl-Ewropa u lil hinn. Â Â Veronika CifrovÃ¡ OstrihoÅovÃ¡ (Renew). â Madam President, dear colleagues, dear Commissioner, I was not here seven years ago when Daphne Caruana Galizia was assassinated. But I am here today, and I'm not afraid to say that it is in great part to Daphne and to JÃ¡n Kuciak.

It is also no coincidence that we are discussing the state of law today, because the state of the rule of law is reflected in how journalists are treated, When they are free to investigate, to report, to speak up, it may be uncomfortable, but it shows that democracy is strong. However, when they are threatened and attacked, we see that democracy is faltering and there's failures in governments. We have seen this in Malta and we have seen this beyond.

Let me express my gratitude to the sons of Daphne for continuing her legacy. I really admire your work, wherever you are, because intimidation, SLAPP lawsuits, and similar malicious tactics have no place in a democratic society. Thank you Daphne, thank you, JÃ¡n, for your courage. We will not forget and we will not stop.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Isabel Wiseler-Lima (PPE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, je vous remercie pour lâengagement dont vous avez fait preuve au cours des cinq derniÃ¨res annÃ©es en faveur de lâÃ©tat de droit. Sept ans que cela dure, sept ans que la justice bafouille, sept ans que les responsables de lâassassinat de Daphne Caruana Galizia jouissent de lâimpunitÃ©. Sa famille mÃ¨ne un immense combat, et leur courage, lâexemple quâils donnent, fait aussi que nous ne doutons pas et que nous continuons encore et encore Ã  demander des comptes, Ã  demander justice.

Ils nâabandonnent pas; nous nâavons certainement pas le droit dâabandonner. Ce serait dâailleurs faire le jeu de la corruption et des criminels que dâoublier. Mais sept ansâ¦ Quel exemple donnons-nous Ã  nos jeunes? Comment les faire croire en la dÃ©mocratie quand nous permettons que la dÃ©mocratie laisse de tels crimes impunis? Nous devons rendre justice pour le meurtre dâune femme, dâune Ã©pouse, dâune mÃ¨re, mais aussi dâune journaliste qui luttait pour la vÃ©ritÃ©.

Elle savait les risques quâelle prenait. Elle a acceptÃ© tous les dangers pour faire triompher la vÃ©ritÃ©, pour dÃ©fendre un monde oÃ¹ la vÃ©ritÃ© prÃ©vaut. Son engagement ne peut avoir Ã©tÃ© vain. Nous avons le devoir de faire plus que dâexiger la vÃ©ritÃ©.Â Tous les coupables, tous, notamment les commanditaires, doivent finir en prison. Sinon, notre monde ne vaut pas la peine que nous le dÃ©fendions.

Je crois en nos dÃ©mocraties, mais il faut que lâimpunitÃ© cesse. Il faut que ce monde ressemble Ã  celui pour lequel nous nous battons. Il faut faire vaincre une dÃ©mocratie oÃ¹ la vÃ©ritÃ© et la justice prÃ©valent.

(L'oratrice accepte une question carton bleu)

Â Â Alex Agius Saliba (S&D), mistoqsija karta blu. â Il-kollega tal-Grupp tal-Popolari gÄ§adha kemm insinwat illi f'Malta teÅ¼isti impunitÃ  gÄ§aliex dawk kollha involuti fil-qtil ta' Daphne Caruana Galizia - il-mandant u anke dawk illi wettqu d-delitt - gÄ§adhom jiÄ¡ru 'l barra b'impunitÃ  u mhux qed jaffaÄÄjaw Ä¡ustizzja. Jiena nistaqsik: inti uÅ¼ajt il-kelma "impunitÃ ". Taf illi l-mandant u l-persuni kollha illi kienu involuti f'dan il-qtil ilhom snin twal arrestati u l-maÄ¡Ä¡oranza assoluta minn tagÄ§hom diÄ¡Ã  Ä¡ew issentenzjati gÄ§al dan il-qtil brutali? Â Â Isabel Wiseler-Lima (PPE), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Monsieur, il me semble quâil y a tout de mÃªme un rÃ©el consensus autour du fait que les commanditaires nâont sÃ»rement pas Ã©tÃ© arrÃªtÃ©s.

Â Â Evin Incir (S&D). â Madam President, Hungary ranks 31st, Bulgaria 30th, Greece 29th, Croatia 28th, Romania 27th, Poland 26th, Slovakia 25th, Italy 24th, Cyprus 23rd. Following this, Malta ranks 22nd among the EU, EFTA and North American countries according to the World Justice Project's Rule of Law Index.

Now, let's discuss corruption and the rule of law in the EU as a whole, even though the EPP and its farâright allies seem disinterested in addressing these issues, likely to avoid drawing attention to the many countries that they govern that rank lower than Malta in this index. Instead, they attempt to exploit our democratic platform in here, in the European Parliament, to score political points.

The murder of Daphne Caruana Galizia should not be politicised for gain. Such behaviour is disgraceful. The EU Rule of Law Report for 2024 clearly states that Malta faces challenges and concerns regarding the rule of law, and no one disputes this. Much more must be done to guarantee media freedom and the rule of law. The same must apply in all countries with all different governments, regardless of colour.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â SunÄana Glavak (PPE). â PoÅ¡tovana predsjedavajuÄa, poÅ¡tovani povjereniÄe, kolegice i kolege, za poÄetak, Å¾eljela bih reÄi da nije zaÅ¡tita novinara ekskluzivno pravo kolega s ljevice.

Kako bi zaÅ¡titila ove principe, ali politiÄke, ekonomske i druÅ¡tvene sile koje Äesto podrivaju naÅ¡e napore, Europska unija poduzima niz mjera. MeÄutim, svjesni smo toga da to nije dovoljno. Ubojstvo Dafne izazvalo je globalnu osudu i otvorilo ozbiljna pitanja o vladavini prava, ne samo na Malti, veÄ i Å¡ire u Europskoj uniji i ovaj je sluÄaj postao simbol prijetnji neovisnosti novinarstva u demokratskim druÅ¡tvima, ali takoÄer ukazao na strukturalne slabosti u pravosudnom i politiÄkom sustavu.

Â Â Sandro Ruotolo (S&D). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, sÃ¬, la ferita Ã¨ ancora aperta. Nel mio Paese, che vive in tempi di pace come tutti gli altri Paesi europei, sono stati uccisi undici giornalisti: nove dalle mafie e due dal terrorismo.

Io sono un esponente del Partito democratico e noi abbiamo un grande problema, per esempio, nel nostro Paese, che sono i consigli comunali â la politica â che ha dei rapporti con la mafia e con la corruzione: pensate che nella mia regione, Napoli e la Campania, ci sono 21 consigli comunali sciolti per infiltrazioni mafiose: dieci di centrosinistra e quattro erano sindaci del Partito democratico.

Allora, io ho visto lo scontro tra il Partito Popolare e i colleghi socialisti di Malta; qui dobbiamo dire delle cose semplici, chiare: siamo tutti contro la mafia, la corruzione e la criminalitÃ  organizzata.

C'Ã¨ un punto per Daphne Caruana Galizia: ha bisogno della veritÃ , di conoscere tutta la veritÃ . E fino a quando non conoscerÃ  tutta la veritÃ  quella parte di Malta, non ci sarÃ  la pacificazione e per avere la pacificazione dobbiamo rendere ...

(La Presidente toglie la parola all'oratore)

TakÅ¾e prosÃ­m vÃ¡s, nezneuÅ¾Ã­vajme pamiatku takÃ½chto Ä¾udÃ­, ktorÃ­ zomreli takÃ½mto spÃ´sobom, aby sme tu vytÄºkali z toho politickÃ½ kapitÃ¡l. Ja verÃ­m tomu, Å¾e Malta je prÃ¡vny Å¡tÃ¡t, Å¾e maltskÃ© orgÃ¡ny a inÅ¡titÃºcie si vedia vyrieÅ¡iÅ¥ takÃ½to problÃ©m a vedia, Äo majÃº robiÅ¥, a Å¾e vyÅ¡etria vÅ¡etko spravodlivo, aby bol naozaj ten vinnÃ­k, ktorÃ½ toto spÃ´sobil, potrestanÃ½, ale nehovorme, Å¾e to je chyba vlÃ¡dy, Å¾e to je chyba maltskÃ½ch politikov a Å¾e to je chyba maltskÃ½ch Ä¾aviÄiarov.

Â Â

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Madam President, dear colleagues, whenever a journalist is murdered it's a big problem for democracy, as many of our colleagues have said. I just want to add something to what Mr Saliba said, also what you said here, my dear colleague.

Here in Europe, we are a family. So when there is a problem and we call out the problems of the other family members, it's nothing personal. You shouldn't take it personally. You shouldn't take it as something against your country or your people. We should be able to openly discuss when there is a problem without our feelings getting hurt. So whenever there is a nation mentioned in this title, we see the same thing. We see the people coming from this nation being hurt emotionally. They are angry. They feel like their country's being made fun of or something like that. But that's not how it is.

So I understand that sometimes the debates here are heated and sometimes people do not use the words that they should use, but keep an open mind to criticism from your colleagues, to criticism from your fellow Europeans, from your family.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, membre de la Commission. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Mesdames et Messieurs les dÃ©putÃ©s, je voudrais dâabord vous remercier pour vos interventions et prÃ©ciser que, depuis lâassassinat de Daphne Caruana Galizia, nous avons entamÃ©, il y a cinq ans, un travail sur lâÃ©tat de droit sur la base du rapport sur lâÃ©tat de droit publiÃ© chaque annÃ©e. La derniÃ¨re Ã©dition date de juillet dernier, et nous y introduisons des recommandations chaque annÃ©e depuisÂ 2022. Jâen ai Ã©voquÃ© quelques-unes tout Ã  lâheure, et nous demandons Ã  chacun des Ãtats membres, y compris Ã  Malte, de les mettre en Åuvre.
