Depuis lâassassinat, nous avons aussi proposÃ© aux autoritÃ©s maltaises dâaider Ã  la conduite des enquÃªtes, quâEuropol âÂ je lâai rappelÃ© tout Ã  lâheureÂ â a contribuÃ© Ã  faire avancer. Mais nous ne sommes pas au bout du processus: il faut que tous les responsables puissent Ãªtre traduits en justice. Des actions ont Ã©tÃ© entreprises Ã  lâÃ©chelon de lâUnion europÃ©enne, qui reposent aussi, malheureusement, sur les leÃ§ons tirÃ©es de cet Ã©vÃ©nement tragique. Jâai rappelÃ© les dispositions que nous avons introduites concernant les procÃ©dures-bÃ¢illons. Il est vrai que Malte a Ã©tÃ© le premier Ãtat Ã  transposer la directive âÂ le dÃ©lai de transposition court jusquâau 7Â maiÂ 2026. Toutefois, nous sommes en train dâexaminer, bien entendu, la maniÃ¨re dont la transposition a eu lieu âÂ je sais quâil y a des critiques de la part de la sociÃ©tÃ© civile, et la Commission rÃ©agira pour voir sâil y a lieu de demander des amÃ©nagements Ã  Malte. Jâai entendu le commentaire tout Ã  lâheure sur lâabsence de possibilitÃ© dâaction dans le cadre des procÃ©dures nationales; ce sont des choses qui seront Ã©valuÃ©es.

Depuis, la Commission a aussi proposÃ© et fait adopter le rÃ¨glement europÃ©en sur la libertÃ© des mÃ©dias, qui aura des rÃ©percussions. Lorsque des rÃ¨gles europÃ©ennes sont violÃ©es, nous nâhÃ©sitons pas non plus Ã  introduire des procÃ©dures dâinfraction. Vous savez que nous sommes devant la Cour de justice pour les Â«passeports dorÃ©sÂ», comme on les appelle, ces programmes dâinvestissement qui permettent de se voir octroyer la citoyennetÃ© nationale. Le dÃ©bat a lieu devant la Cour de justice. Mais pour cela, il faut Ã©videmment quâil y ait eu violation Ã  nos yeux du droit europÃ©en. Pour le reste, ce sont des recommandations que nous formulons, comme Ã  tous les Ãtats membres, en tentant de les faire progresser.

Je voudrais vous dire que la Commission encourage bien entendu les autoritÃ©s maltaises Ã  poursuivre les rÃ©formes engagÃ©es. Jâai citÃ© un certain nombre de rÃ©formes qui ont eu lieu, mais aussi un certain nombre de rÃ©formes qui attendent toujours dâÃªtre mises en Åuvre. Nous sommes prÃªts Ã  poursuivre notre dialogue avec les autoritÃ©s maltaises pour avancer sur ce plan. Je voudrais aussi saluer, comme beaucoup lâont fait, lâengagement de la sociÃ©tÃ© civile maltaise, qui a Ã©tÃ© fondamental dans ce processus. Jâai eu moi-mÃªme lâoccasion de rencontrer beaucoup dâacteurs de la sociÃ©tÃ© civile sur place, Ã  Malte, comme dâailleurs de dÃ©battre avec le fils de Daphne. Je crois quâil y a une action concrÃ¨te que nous pouvons mener ensemble, sur le terrain.

Je voudrais encore prÃ©ciser que lâÃ©volution de la situation de lâÃ©tat de droit Ã  Malte et dans tous les Ãtats membres sera Ã  nouveau analysÃ©e dans le cycle annuel de lâÃ©tat de droit dans le rapport qui sera publiÃ© lâannÃ©e prochaine, le sixiÃ¨me rapport, publiÃ© enÂ 2025. Je voudrais Ã  nouveauÂ demander, parce que jâai entendu beaucoup de commentaires Ã  ce sujet, de pouvoir dÃ©battre avec le Parlement europÃ©en de la situation dans tous les Ãtats membres de lâUnion europÃ©enne, y compris Malte, et dây consacrer du temps, peut-Ãªtre Ã  travers des commissions. En effet, je constate que nous arrivons maintenant Ã  le faire avec le Conseil, dans le cadre du Conseil des affaires gÃ©nÃ©rales. Nous ne consacrons pas seulement un dÃ©bat au rapport sur lâÃ©tat de droit, mais nous organisons un dÃ©bat sur la situation dans chaque Ãtat membre, individuellement, par groupe.

Jâai proposÃ© Ã  plusieurs reprises que lâon puisse Ã©galement examiner la situation dans chacun des Ãtats membres ici, au Parlement. Je relance Ã  nouveau cet appel. Je comprends quâil y ait une attention particuliÃ¨re sur lâune ou lâautre situation, mais ce sera important, y compris dans le cas de Malte, dâexaminer âÂ peut-Ãªtre dâailleurs avec les autoritÃ©s maltaisesÂ â oÃ¹ des manquements sont encore prÃ©sents et oÃ¹ des recommandations doivent Ãªtre appliquÃ©es.

VoilÃ , je relance une nouvelle fois cet appel. Ce nâest pas la premiÃ¨re fois dans le cadre de la discussion sur lâÃ©tat de droit, et jâespÃ¨re quâil y aura une Ã©volution en la matiÃ¨re dans les annÃ©es Ã  venir.

Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members, I am standing before you replacing Commissioner Ferreira. Cities and regions are key in implementing the EU's ambitious green and digital agenda, whilst ensuring inclusive growth and shared prosperity.

During these two last decades, the European Union regions have achieved remarkable convergence. Europeans who joined the EU since 2004, have seen on average, their GDP per capital increase from 52 % of the EU average in 2004 to nearly 80 % in 2023, which also translates into an impressive improvement of their living standards.

This convergence of EU regions was enabled, among other factors, by the direct support to investment from cohesion policy as well as their integration into the single market. Thanks to the cumulative impact of Cohesion Policy funding in the 2014-2020 and 2021-2027 programmes, the EU GDP is estimated to be 0.9 % higher by 2030. The impact is much stronger in cohesion countries where support is concentrated.

In 2030, GDP will be 8 % higher in Croatia, 6 % higher in Poland and Slovakia, and 5 % higher in Lithuania. This is not just an accomplishment of the EU institutions and cohesion policy. It is also the result of the work that has been carried out by the regions themselves, by the Member States, by parliaments pushing for future-oriented policies, by project groups and by stakeholders.

Cohesion policy provides a framework that enables the regions to deliver for themselves. Cities have been important actors in this transformation. Nowadays, about 75 % of the EU population live in urban areas. Cities are the economic motors of Europe, generating about 85 % of GDP and about 75 % of employment.

It should also be noted that local and regional authorities play an important role in the delivery of the EU's initiatives, as 70 % of European legislation needs their intervention to be implemented. Yet despite the aforementioned positive economic advancements, multiple challenges remain in several sectors, such as post-crisis economic recovery, social exclusion, housing, climate change mitigation and adaptation, environmental degradation, health risks, mobility and demographic change, as well as challenges that pertain to the twin green and digital transitions.

National convergence at EU level is not always accompanied by internal convergence between capital regions and the rest of the territory in some countries. This is due to economic dynamism and national public investment in infrastructure, as well as human capital research and innovation centres being often centralised in the more developed regions, while other regions, especially rural and remote areas, struggle to boost economic activity and face demographic challenges.

Furthermore, some regions and territories have fallen into development traps. Their economic growth, employment and productivity have been stagnating for the better part of the last two decades. Trapped territories exist at all levels of development, richer and poorer, each of them facing different structural challenges, impairing their development.

Given internal territorial imbalances, even though cities with the highest GDP often face acute congestion, concentrated pollution, pressure on public services, housing problems and pockets of poverty and social exclusion that are difficult to address. Regions and municipalities share many of the key challenges obstructing innovation and implementation of the digital transformation, whether on infrastructure, smart governance, smart mobility, start-up ecosystems, open data or digital sustainability. This makes them critical actors and interlocutors for accelerating the dissemination and takeup of digital technologies across Europe, as well as fostering digital ecosystems.

Lastly, the effects of climate change are exacerbating regional inequalities, notably in rural, coastal, Mediterranean and south eastern EU regions, risking over 1 % of GDP annually.

Women, children, young and ageing populations in these areas face higher vulnerability to climate related extreme weather events. Many EU cities are also particularly vulnerable as they act as heat islands. Some suffer from floodings and forest fires.Â Place-based policies, such as cohesion policy, can help cities and regions to reduce the economic cost of climate-change mitigation.

Given all these challenges, it is clear that cities and regions need EU support and tailored assistance, for example, true cohesion policy.T o succeed with the green and digital change on the ground, the transition has to be fair and engaging for all. This involves putting the quality of life of the citizens first.

As presented in the ninth Cohesion Report, investments are a necessary but not sufficient condition to ensure economic development. Some regions and cities, despite receiving cohesion policy support for many years, still experience weak economic performance. Therefore, to further stimulate regional growth and convergence, there is a need to explore how the link between investments and reforms could be further strengthened to maximise the impact of cohesion policy.

Thank you very much for your attention, but now I'm looking forward, of course, to the discussions.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Andrey Novakov, on behalf of the PPE Group. â MrÂ President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, let me get straight to the point: every problem is local, every solution we vote here is implemented locally. This is why, no matter what happens with cohesion policy in the future, no matter what kind of reforms we are going to conduct, this policy should remain local. It should be based on the shared management principle.

But we have a bigger problem than the cuts. We have bigger problems than the language we are using here not being understandable to local authorities, because after all, the absorption rate is hopelessly low, which means that there is no interest to this policy. We have to be better at explaining why this is important and what we are doing here â and by saying we, I am putting myself in the first place â because if the mayors of the regional authorities are not interested in this policy, it's already dead. We don't have to wait for any reforms to come. If nobody is interested in using this policy, it's already gone.

So help me promote this policy back at home, because when this plenary session is over, then we fly back not just to the Member State, but to our constituency, to our village, to our city, to our local community and then we have to explain why this mayor should apply for more EU funds and apply it better in order to implement this project in the service of its people and with the support of the Union.

Finally, I welcome the initiative of the Commission of disbursing 10Â billion in support of the Member States who suffer from the floods. This is the way we should support part of our family when they suffer.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Mohammed Chahim, namens de S&D-Fractie. â Voorzitter, in de regio en in de stad maak je vaak voor het eerst kennis met de EU, met Europa. Meestal ongemerkt. Want wat doet de Europese samenwerking nou voor jou? Zoals Johan Cruijff ooit zei: âJe gaat het pas zien, als je het doorhebt.â

Budgetten, fondsen en subsidiepotjes zeggen mensen niet zoveel. Maar wat er met het geld wordt gedaan natuurlijk wel. Ik moet dan denken aan het fietspad tussen Tilburg en Turnhout, waarop veel mensen dagelijks de grens oversteken voor werk, studie of familiebezoek. Of aan de jobcoaches in Rotterdam die leerlingen aan een vaste baan helpenÂ en daarom cruciaal zijn voor die stad. Maar ik denk ook aan een windmolenpark in Flevoland dat vierhonderdduizend huishoudens voorziet van groene stroom. Dit zijn allemaal voorbeelden van projecten die mede mogelijk gemaakt zijn door Europa, door de EU. Projecten in de stad, in de regio, dicht bij jouw huis. Daar merk je wat van. Daar zie je wat Europa voor jou doet.

Terwijl sommigen in dit parlement de geldkraan willen dichtdraaien, willen wij juist meer aandacht voor de stad en de regio. De steden weten wat nodig is in de wijken, in de buurten en in onze straten. De regio zorgt ervoor dat mensen in de dorpen ook worden gehoord. Zij weten wat nodig is om onze plannen, die we hier maken, tot een succes te maken. En ze zijn onmisbaar voor de uitvoering. Daarom hebben we juist meer budget nodig en niet minder. Ik snap echt de bewegingen van sommigen in dit parlement niet. De steden en regio's geven Europa een gezicht en je gaat het pas zien als je het doorhebt.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Denis Nesci, a nome del gruppo ECR. â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, la politica di coesione Ã¨ la principale fonte di investimento europeo per lo sviluppo dei territori e per ridurre le disparitÃ  nelle nostre regioni.

Mettere al centro politiche attive del lavoro e la creazione di un ambiente favorevole alla crescita Ã¨ essenziale, anche sanando quelle distorsioni burocratiche che creano inefficienza e lentezza per imprese e consumatori.

In questi giorni di difficoltÃ  permettetemi di ringraziare gli operatori delle forze dell'ordine, i vigili del fuoco e i volontari tutti che stanno affrontando situazioni drammatiche per salvare vite umane in tutta Italia e rivolgo un'attenzione particolare alla Calabria, la mia terra, che Ã¨ stata messa a dura prova da forti alluvioni. Si tratta di fenomeni che purtroppo viviamo quotidianamente in tutta Europa.

Per questo motivo Ã¨ fondamentale promuovere azioni per la tutela dei nostri territori attraverso un aumento e un miglioramento dei finanziamenti diretti. Tali finanziamenti dovrebbero essere orientati sia a una piÃ¹ efficace prevenzione delle calamitÃ  naturali, sia a una ricostruzione piÃ¹ resiliente dopo un evento calamitoso.

Â Â Valentina Palmisano, a nome del gruppo The Left. â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, tutti noi siamo qui all'interno del Parlamento europeo perchÃ© crediamo fermamente nei valori fondanti dell'Unione europea.

Ma Ã¨ importante affermare che il cuore pulsante del progetto europeo non si trova dentro questi palazzi, ma nei territori di appartenenza di ognuno di noi. Sto parlando delle regioni e della cittÃ  e oggi vi parlo non soltanto come parlamentare europea ma come componente del consiglio comunale della mia cittÃ , Ostuni.

E vi dico con certezza che le sfide ambientali, sociali ed economiche di cui qui dentro si parla tanto, giustamente, vengono affrontate innanzitutto dai Comuni, che sono in prima linea nella lotta contro l'esclusione sociale, nell'attuazione delle misure relative alla transizione verde, industriale, digitale, nella lotta al cambiamento climatico.

L'Europa cosa puÃ² fare? Deve supportare le regioni e le cittÃ , coinvolgendole in tutte le fasi decisionali, soprattutto nelle politiche di coesione, e deve migliorare il loro accesso ai finanziamenti. Io concludo approfittando di questo spazio per ringraziare tutti gli amministratori locali per il grande lavoro che svolgono tutti i giorni.

Â Â Arno Bausemer, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, meine sehr verehrten Damen und Herren! Allein fÃ¼r Deutschland werden zur FÃ¶rderung der StÃ¤dte und Regionen von 2021 bis 2027 elf Milliarden Euro bereitgestellt; so weit, so gut.

Die ideologische BÃ¼rokratie beginnt allerdings zum Nachteil der Regionen zu wirken, weil ein GroÃteil dieser Gelder zweckgebunden fÃ¼r ein grÃ¼neres und CO2-freies Europa eingesetzt werden mussÂ â ânachhaltige und innovative stÃ¤dtische MobilitÃ¤tâ heiÃt das auf dem EU-Papier.

GefÃ¶rdert werden tatsÃ¤chlich aber immer mehr Radwege, was eine VerdrÃ¤ngung der Autofahrer aus den InnenstÃ¤dten zur Folge hat. VergieÃen Sie bei den Sozialisten und GrÃ¼nen doch bitte keine KrokodilstrÃ¤nen, wenn wir im EuropÃ¤ischen Parlament Ã¼ber die Krise der Autohersteller debattieren, denn es ist doch Ihr GrÃ¼ner Deal, der Autoherstellern und Verbrauchern das Leben schwer macht.

StÃ¤dte sind ohne die Regionen nicht denkbar: Pendler kommen zur Arbeit, Kunden zum Einzelhandel, Touristen zu Besuch. Die allermeisten dieser Menschen kommen aufgrund der Entfernungen nunmal nicht mit dem Rad, sondern mit dem Auto, oder sie kommen eben nicht. Mehr Radwege und weniger ParkplÃ¤tze verringern vielleicht den CO2-AusstoÃ, aber vor allem verringert diese Entwicklung die MobilitÃ¤t der Menschen.

Lassen Sie uns Wege finden, um die MobilitÃ¤t der Menschen zu stÃ¤rken! Wir brauchen weniger grÃ¼ne Umerziehung, wir brauchen mehr Freiheit fÃ¼r die Menschen in Europa.

Â Â Elena Nevado del Campo (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, como dijo Robert Schuman: Â«Soy europeo; deseo, sin embargo, seguir siendo francÃ©s, y, en Francia, Lorena es mi patria chicaÂ».

Las ciudades y las regiones estuvieron siempre en la mente de las personas que iniciaron el proyecto europeo. He sido alcaldesa de mi ciudad, de CÃ¡ceres, y puedo dar cuenta de la enorme repercusiÃ³n que los fondos europeos han tenido en el desarrollo local y de la forma en que la gobernanza multinivel y la asociaciÃ³n entre administraciones han sido claves para respetar las particularidades de cada comunidad desde el nivel mÃ¡s bÃ¡sico.

Como extremeÃ±a, como espaÃ±ola, como europea, creo firmemente en el rol de las ciudades y de las regiones: es esencial. El principio de subsidiariedad se introdujo para definir el papel que cada instituciÃ³n debe ejercer al servicio del bien comÃºn en un marco de comÃºn respeto. La mera eficiencia no puede justificar cualquier traspaso de aquello que las comunidades locales y regionales pueden hacer por sÃ­ mismas: romper con este principio es romper con el principio mismo de la UniÃ³n, con la cohesiÃ³n social y territorial que persiguen los Tratados.

Europa es el resultado de una comunidad cultural e histÃ³rica cuyas raÃ­ces se hunden en cada ciudad, en cada pueblo, en cada comarca. Sin las ciudades no hay ciudadanos y, por ello, cualquier polÃ­tica europea debe realizarse siempre con respeto hacia la perspectiva local y regional: que no lo olvide la ComisiÃ³n.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jean-Marc Germain (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, mes chers collÃ¨gues, la politique de cohÃ©sion âÂ voulue par Jacques Delors, qui aimait plus que tout la solidaritÃ© qui unitÂ â, câest cette belle idÃ©e quâaucune rÃ©gion, aucune ville, aucun village ne doit rester sur le bord du chemin. Et jamais, non, jamais nous nâaccepterons de conditionner lâattribution des fonds rÃ©gionaux Ã  la rÃ©alisation de rÃ©formes structurelles dont on imagine sans peine quâelles puiseraient dans les pires recettes Ã©culÃ©es du billet vert nÃ©olibÃ©ral: toujours moins de droits pour les salariÃ©s, toujours plus dâargent pour les actionnaires et jamais aucune considÃ©ration pour les limites planÃ©taires.

Je vous lâai dit: non, non, jamais nous nâaccepterons de dire au Secours populaire dans le Cantal que lâEurope souhaite le soutien vital qui lui est apportÃ© pour lâaide alimentaire des plus dÃ©munis, car la France aurait refusÃ© de porter lâÃ¢ge de la retraite Ã  67, Ã  69 ou Ã  71 ans âÂ car ils ne sâarrÃªtent jamais.

Mes chers collÃ¨gues, unissons-nous, unissons-nous pour que lâEurope ne se transforme pas en ce que le FMI a eu de pire, un remÃ¨de pire que le mal: des rÃ©formes imposÃ©es, au mieux inefficaces, et souvent mortifÃ¨res.

Â Â Jorge BuxadÃ© Villalba (PfE). â SeÃ±or presidente, casi todos los espaÃ±oles nacimos en hogares en propiedad, disfrutÃ¡bamos de estabilidad, ahorro, autonomÃ­a, libertad, sentido de pertenencia y seguridad.

En las Ãºltimas dÃ©cadas, una alianza de la izquierda europea, que odia la propiedad ajena, con los megarricos del Foro de Davos y el Club de Roma ha arrasado nuestras ciudades: sus zonas de bajas emisiones han convertido las ciudades en fortalezas que impiden la libre circulaciÃ³n a los que no pueden pagar un coche elÃ©ctrico; sus delirios fanÃ¡ticos imponen casas climÃ¡ticamente neutras, pero carÃ­simas, que solo los privilegiados pueden adquirir, condenando a las familias a vivir a cuarentaÂ kilÃ³metros de su trabajo y, a los jÃ³venes, en pisos compartidos con extraÃ±os.

Y ahora nos vienen con el cuento de las ciudades de quinceÂ minutos, Â¡pero si ya lo tenÃ­amos todo a quinceÂ minutos: el comercio local, el colegio, un parque donde jugar...! Â Â Åerban Dimitrie Sturdza (ECR). â Domnule preÈedinte, stimaÈi colegi, politica de coeziune rÄmÃ¢ne piatra de temelie a dezvoltÄrii durabile Èi echitabile Ã®n Uniunea EuropeanÄ pentru reducerea disparitÄÈilor regionale. Â 
Â Â

Â Â Ciaran Mullooly (Renew). â Mr President, as an MEP representing a largely rural constituency, I regularly see the vital role that our city plays within the region. For many, that journey to a greener, social and more sustainable future can often bring significant challenges. Cohesion funding is a lifeline for some of these communities, offering targeted support to address regional inequalities, and it must be increased if we are serious about fostering balanced regional development.

I know where this is working well: the Cities at Heart network, a European programme, brings together 10Â different local governments working to create tools for the improvement of city centres. I've been to such a growing municipal area: Sligo, which brings valuable bestâpractice examples of how they've implemented innovative measures for property management, tourism policy and much more. Furthermore, Sligo has won the Purple Flag Award for their night-time economy.

Public policy in Ireland is often centralised in Dublin. We must change this â we need a territorial approach. Europe depends on the success of all its regions. We must ensure both urban and rural communities have the resources they need to build a sustainable and a prosperous future.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Vladimir PrebiliÄ (Verts/ALE). â Gospod predsednik! SpoÅ¡tovane kolegice in kolegi! KakÅ¡na si Å¾elite, da bi bila evropska mesta leta 2030? Bodo Å¡e vedno na vrhu seznama najbolj zaÅ¾eljenih mest za bivanje na svetu?

Da bi to ohranili, moramo zagotoviti trajnostni promet, cenovno dostopne javne storitve, javno Å¡olstvo in zdravstvo ter reÅ¡eno stanovanjsko vpraÅ¡anje. To je kljuÄni izziv naÅ¡e generacije in tega mandata. In da bi to lahko dosegli, moramo prisluhniti naÅ¡im Å¾upanjam in Å¾upanom, saj so ti v neposrednem stiku z drÅ¾avljankami in drÅ¾avljani in najbolje poznajo njihove potrebe.

Kot nekdanji Å¾upan KoÄevja sem se prepriÄal, da je treba socialne in zelene programe uresniÄevati v jedru demokracije, to je v lokalnih skupnostih. To je tudi razlog, zakaj moramo mestom omogoÄiti bolj neposreden dostop do evropskih sredstev, in to takoj.

Nacionalne vlade ne smejo odloÄati o vsem. Svoj glas morajo imeti lokalne skupnosti. Le tako bo Evropa ostala vodilna svetovna sila na podroÄju trajnostnega razvoja in kakovosti Å¾ivljenja.

Â

Â Â Younous Omarjee (The Left). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, nous ne sommes pas rÃ©tifs Ã  une rÃ©forme en profondeur de la politique de cohÃ©sion. Mais je le dis sans dÃ©tour: la politique de cohÃ©sion est une politique rÃ©gionale. Ainsi reconcentrer, renationaliser les fonds europÃ©ens de la cohÃ©sion serait une profonde erreur.

Devant notre Parlement europÃ©en, la prÃ©sidente vonÂ derÂ Leyen a pris un engagement, elle a mÃªme pris un double engagement: dâabord pour une politique de cohÃ©sion renforcÃ©e, ensuite pour une politique de cohÃ©sion qui place les rÃ©gions au centre âÂ et je ne doute pas que cet engagement pris devant la plÃ©niÃ¨re sera respectÃ©. Ce qui veut dire, pour nous, le maintien de programmes rÃ©gionaux nÃ©gociÃ©s directement entre la Commission europÃ©enne et les autoritÃ©s rÃ©gionales, et mis en Åuvre par les rÃ©gions.

Reconcentrer serait, Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, avec la montÃ©e des partis dâextrÃªme droite partout en Europe, un risque immense pour la dÃ©mocratie, un risque immense pour lâEurope. Les rÃ©gions dâEurope, vous le savez, sont le cÅur et le souffle de la dÃ©mocratie europÃ©enne.

Â Â Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE), question Â«carton bleuÂ». â Monsieur Omarjee, je veux vous remercier parce que, sous la prÃ©cÃ©dente lÃ©gislature, vous avez, en qualitÃ© de prÃ©sident de la commission du dÃ©veloppement rÃ©gional, luttÃ© Ã¢prement contre la centralisation des fonds.

Â Â Younous Omarjee (The Left), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Merci beaucoup, chÃ¨re collÃ¨gue, pour votre question, qui montre bien que ce dÃ©bat arrive au bon moment.

Nous aurons dans quelques semaines les auditions des commissaires et, aussi bien dans la commission des budgets que dans celle du dÃ©veloppement rÃ©gional, il nous faudra affirmer la position âÂ qui semble unanime, quasiÂ unanime ici, au Parlement europÃ©enÂ â selon laquelle la politique de cohÃ©sion devra Ãªtre renforcÃ©e, quâelle doit demeurer une politique rÃ©gionale et que nous refusons les propositions qui semblent provenir de la DGÂ BUDG. Mais nous attendons Ã©videmment des garanties sur ce plan.

Â Â Nora Junco GarcÃ­a (NI). â SeÃ±or presidente, Â¿cÃ³mo vamos a tener una vivienda asequible en EspaÃ±a si los ayuntamientos, las comunidades autÃ³nomas y el Estado son los principales fondos buitres de la vivienda, los que la encarecen de verdad, y no la actividad privada?

La vivienda tiene que dejar de ser la fuente de financiaciÃ³n de las administraciones. En EspaÃ±a, el 32Â % del precio de la vivienda es el suelo y el 25Â % son impuestos: tasas municipales, tasas autonÃ³micas, licencias, impuestos estatalesâ¦ Es mÃ¡s de la mitad del precio lo que va directamente a las administraciones pÃºblicas, que necesitan toda esta financiaciÃ³n para soportar su estructura; eso sin contar con la especulaciÃ³n de suelos municipales, que degenera en corrupciÃ³n polÃ­tica en la mayorÃ­a de los casos.

Y es que, en EspaÃ±a, al contrario que en el resto de Europa, encabezamos el ranking de paÃ­ses que mÃ¡s municipios tienen y, lejos de fusionar y reducir ayuntamientos, los hemos ampliado. Las administraciones son mÃ¡s eficientes cuando hay cohesiÃ³n. Tenemos que abordar todos estos problemas de manera urgente fomentando las iniciativas privadas en el sector con estrategias efectivas. La fusiÃ³n obligatoria de municipios en EspaÃ±a es la asignatura pendiente.

Â Â Marcos Ros Sempere (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, nos perciben alejados y tenemos que cambiarlo. Â Â Raquel GarcÃ­a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). â Voorzitter, hier staat vandaag een Spaanse van geboorte, een Nederlandse en Friezin door keuze, en een Europeaan uit overtuiging. Want dankzij de Europese Unie mocht ik de liefde volgen, werken, studeren en mijn gezin stichten. Van de hoofdstad Madrid tot het Friese dorp Gorredijk: ik heb alle kansen kunnen pakken die Europa te bieden heeft.

Mijn dochters gun ik diezelfde vrijheid. De vrijheid om te kunnen kiezen om weg te vliegen, maar vooral ook om te kiezen om dicht bij de plek te blijven waar ze zich thuis voelen. Dat ze dan kunnen genieten van een schone Waddenzee en een bloeiende, kansrijke regio, die door middel van de Lelylijn verbonden is met de rest van Europa. En van laagdrempelige toegang tot Europese programma's, waaronder ter bescherming van hun taal, het Fries. En dat ze op de vraag âIs Europa er voor ons?âÂ nuchter kunnen antwoorden: âJa, sa is it, en net oars.â

Â

Â Â Isabelle Le Callennec (PPE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, 70Â % de la lÃ©gislation europÃ©enne a des rÃ©percussions directes sur nos municipalitÃ©s. En tant que maire dâune ville de Bretagne, jâen ai fait le constat. Le rÃ´le des villes et des territoires est donc absolument central dans la mise en Åuvre des politiques de lâUnion. Pour Ãªtre efficaces et lisibles, les mesures qui concernent la vie quotidienne des citoyens doivent donc Ãªtre Ã©laborÃ©es avec les institutions locales, y compris les territoires ruraux et ultrapÃ©riphÃ©riques.

La simplification des rÃ¨gles dâaccÃ¨s aux projets europÃ©ens et de leur instruction est une demande de longue date des Ã©lus locaux, garants dâune dÃ©mocratie de proximitÃ©. LâÃ©chelon territorial devrait donc pouvoir Ãªtre reconnu comme un Ã©chelon de confiance dans la gestion administrative et financiÃ¨re des fonds structurels âÂ des fonds quâil convient de prÃ©server, de conforter, de pluriannualiser, et qui pourraient faire lâobjet dâacomptes de versement.

Pour rÃ©pondre concrÃ¨tement aux attentes de nos concitoyens, le dialogue avec le ComitÃ© des rÃ©gions et les associations dâÃ©lus des 27Â Ãtats membres doit donc Ãªtre permanent et sincÃ¨re. Je ne doute pas que ce sera lâun des objectifs prioritaires de la future Commission.

Â Â Nora Mebarek (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, les rÃ©gions sont aujourdâhui menacÃ©es de devenir les variables dâajustement des politiques budgÃ©taires de la France et de lâEurope. Ã travers elles ce sont pourtant des projets concrets, indispensables Ã  la transition Ã©cologique, aux transports ou encore au dÃ©veloppement Ã©conomique qui prennent vie.

Et pourtant, en France comme Ã  Bruxelles, la tentation est grande de sacrifier ces territoires sur lâautel de lâaustÃ©ritÃ©. Dâun gouvernement Ã  lâautre, EmmanuelÂ Macron impose chaque jour aux rÃ©gions de trouver de nouvelles Ã©conomies pour combler un dÃ©ficit gonflÃ© par des cadeaux fiscaux aux plus riches. Par ailleurs, la Commission europÃ©enne, sous lâimpulsion dâUrsulaÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen, prÃ©parerait une rÃ©forme qui centraliserait ou recentraliserait les fonds et marginaliserait les rÃ©gions dans leur gestion.

Cette rÃ©forme est une menace pour lâavenir de lâEurope, car elle retire aux rÃ©gions et aux villes leur rÃ´le dans la gestion des fonds de cohÃ©sion. Câest lâessence mÃªme du projet europÃ©en que lâon met en pÃ©ril. En liant la distribution des fonds Ã  la mise en Åuvre des rÃ©formes Ã©conomiques dÃ©cidÃ©es Ã  Bruxelles, la Commission donne encore plus de pouvoir aux gouvernements centraux au dÃ©triment des territoires, victimes dâune double peine.

DerriÃ¨re ces rÃ©formes se cachent des coupes sombres, qui mettraient en danger des projets essentiels. Ce projet de rÃ©forme est un coup de force dÃ©guisÃ©. Centraliser toujours plus tout en excluant les acteurs locaux, câest trahir lâesprit de la construction europÃ©enne et le principe de subsidiaritÃ©, câest affaiblir la dÃ©mocratie de proximitÃ© et dÃ©vitaliser nos territoires.

Ceux qui soutiennent cette vision technocratique ne font que promouvoir une libÃ©ralisation dÃ©passÃ©e, loin et trÃ¨s loin des prÃ©occupations de nos concitoyens.

Â Â Raffaele Stancanelli (PfE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, le cittÃ  e le regioni hanno ricoperto nella storia un ruolo di primaria importanza nella nascita e formazione della nostra Europa.

La vera forza delle comunitÃ  locali risiede nella valorizzazione dell'autonomia e della responsabilitÃ . L'Unione europea, per essere realisticamente efficiente, deve rispettare le peculiaritÃ  regionali, ponendo al centro politiche orientate all'efficienza economica, alla sicurezza e all'innovazione. Le cittÃ  devono operare come motore di crescita sostenibile, agevolando i comparti produttivi e favorendo l'occupazione.

Ã imperativo promuovere soluzioni calibrate sulle tipicitÃ  locali, rigettando una centralizzazione eccessiva delle competenze. In un contesto di prosperitÃ  e coesione sociale, con l'autonomia nella gestione delle risorse, si puÃ² provvedere al sostentamento delle piccole e medie imprese, che rappresentano il pilastro dell'economia delle nostre cittÃ .

Ã quindi essenziale evidenziare che, con il rispetto della storia, delle tradizioni e dell'autonomia regionale, si puÃ² avere un'Europa forte, sicura e prospera per le generazioni future.

Â Â

Â Â Ruggero Razza (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, la centralitÃ  delle regioni e dei comuni nell'ambito delle politiche di coesione Ã¨ stata ribadita da tutti i gruppi e sono certo che nessuno in quest'Aula ritiene che si possa fare a meno dei comuni e delle regioni nella pianificazione dei prossimi cicli di programmazione.

Io ho avuto il privilegio di fare parte di un governo regionale che ha saputo investire le risorse nelle politiche di coesione, che ha certificato miliardi di euro di spese effettivamente fatte e che ha programmato l'attuale ciclo di programmazione, mettendo insieme risorse per oltre 15 miliardi di euro.

E quindi nessuno piÃ¹ di chi ha avuto queste esperienze â e in tutti i gruppi siamo in tanti â puÃ² essere assolutamente consapevole della necessitÃ  di vedere le regioni come protagoniste, al pari dei comuni, della nuova politica di coesione.

PerÃ² le regioni vanno ascoltate e quando â come Ã¨ capitato nell'ultima seduta del Comitato europeo delle regioni â ci hanno raccontato che la transizione verde non puÃ² compromettere la competitivitÃ  dell'Europa, anche in questo vanno ascoltate.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Oihane Agirregoitia MartÃ­nez (Renew). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, a principios del mes de septiembre lanzamos una pregunta a la ComisiÃ³n Europea trasladÃ¡ndole nuestra preocupaciÃ³n con respecto a ciertos rumores que habÃ­a de, en el nuevo marco financiero plurianual, agrupar programas que, a dÃ­a de hoy, son de gestiÃ³n compartida con las regiones, de cara a hacer un Ãºnico programa a nivel nacional: la respuesta no fue nada clara.

Hoy me alegraba escucharle hablar del importante rol que tienen las ciudades y las regiones para el desarrollo de una Europa que sea competitiva, verde y social, pero tambiÃ©n me ha preocupado escucharle hablar de la necesidad de estimular una convergencia y explorar vÃ­nculos entre inversiones y reformas.

De manera que, desde hoy, desde esta tribuna, hago dosÂ peticiones muy claras: una de ellas, que sea usted claro en sus respuestas; la segunda, que la nueva ComisiÃ³n Europea mantenga y refuerce la gobernanza multinivel con las regiones y que no se alinee comprÃ¡ndole el discurso con una extrema derecha recentralizadoraÂ que se olvida de la eficacia de las polÃ­ticas de proximidad y de la realidad diaria a la que se enfrentan las ciudades y regiones que conforman Europa.

Â Â Carla Tavares (S&D). â Senhor Presidente, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio, nas Ãºltimas dÃ©cadas, as regiÃµes europeias tÃªmâse desenvolvido e convergido. Com esta abordagem de base local, a polÃ­tica de coesÃ£o trouxe aos cidadÃ£os uma perspetiva econÃ³mica positiva para a sua regiÃ£o, melhorando a competitividade e contribuindo para a transiÃ§Ã£o verde e digital, sem deixar ninguÃ©m para trÃ¡s. Â Â Mireia BorrÃ¡s PabÃ³n (PfE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, seÃ±orÃ­as, nos venden aquÃ­ un desarrollo verde, social y prÃ³spero mientras el 90Â % de los pueblos en EspaÃ±a se vacÃ­an, convertidos en autÃ©nticos desiertos por los ataques constantes de este Parlamento âconcretamente toda la bancada de centroâizquierdaâ con la complicidad, por supuesto, del Gobierno espaÃ±ol: hemos llegado al extremo de que el propio ministro de Agricultura, Pesca y AlimentaciÃ³n de EspaÃ±a prioriza los intereses de Marruecos por encima de los intereses de nuestros agricultores, ignorando incluso la sentencia del Tribunal de Justicia de la UniÃ³n Europea.

Â¿Hasta cuÃ¡ndo van a sacrificar ustedes el sector primario en el altar de su agenda globalista? Los insumos agrÃ­colas se han disparado un 30Â % en toda Europa, el agua se ha convertido en un lujo por la falta de infraestructuras en las zonas rurales, y, mientras, aquÃ­ hablan de polÃ­ticas sostenibles y de cohesiÃ³n, mientras el 50Â % de las zonas rurales en Europa estÃ¡ en riesgo de despoblaciÃ³n. Â¿Y saben por quÃ©? Porque casi el 40Â % de los agricultores en Europa tienen mÃ¡s de 55Â aÃ±os, sin opciones de relevo generacional.

En EspaÃ±a tenemos regiones, como Zamora, Soria o Lugo, que han perdido mÃ¡s del 30Â % de su poblaciÃ³n, asÃ­ que les dirÃ© que, si quieren prosperidad, si quieren cohesiÃ³n en Europa, entonces dejen de asesinar el medio de vida de aquellos que mantienen vivo nuestro campo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Barry Cowen (Renew). â Mr President, colleagues, the expansion and growth of cities are fundamental to regional development across Europe. However, too often restrictive and protracted planning procedures in many Member States stand in the way of infrastructure progress. These bottlenecks prevent us from meeting the needs of our citizens and regions.

In my own constituency, the city of Galway faces exactly that challenge. The development of a new ring road, vital for easing decades of road congestion and promoting the city's growth, has been hamstrung by ridiculous planning delays. Similarly, the development of Galway's port holds enormous potential, especially for offshore wind energy along the West coast. This investment could play a significant role in Europe's energy transition, reducing our dependency on Russian gas.

At a time when energy prices in Europe are five times higher than that of the US, such investments are not just important, they are essential for our future. To address these challenges, we need to identify a threshold which caters for a streamlined process of infrastructure planning permissions, notwithstanding or compromising best practices.

Then we will be responding to Draghi's cry in his recent report to president von der Leyen, highlighting but addressing, and maybe overturning, Europe's present diminishing competitiveness and growth trajectory.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â RenÃ© Repasi (S&D). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Kommunen und die EuropÃ¤ische Union sitzen in einem Boot. Die Mitgliedstaaten geben uns immer mehr Aufgaben, aber kein Geld. In so einer Situation muss man besonders gut haushalten. Es war eine besonders schlechte Idee der Kommission, das Geld aus dem EU-Haushalt in ein GesamtpÃ¤ckchen zu packen und dannÂ â schwuppÂ â rÃ¼ber an die Mitgliedstaaten zu schickenÂ â ohne weitere Spezifikationen.

Die Folge davon ist, dass ein zielgenauer Einsatz von EU-Geldern durch BÃ¼rgermeisterinnen und BÃ¼rgermeister, durch Landesregierungen nicht mehr mÃ¶glich ist. Noch schlimmer ist das AushÃ¶hlen von Parlamentsrechten. Wenn das kommt, kÃ¶nnen wir hier im Haus demokratisch nicht mehr definieren, wofÃ¼r europÃ¤ische Gelder in der KohÃ¤sionspolitik eingesetzt werden.

Wir mÃ¼ssen die Kommission aufgrund von seichten Wasserstandsmeldungen entlasten; der Fall Ungarn hat gezeigt, dass sich die Kommission leider nicht mit Ruhm bekleckert, wenn sie politisch willkÃ¼rlich Gelder freigibt. Das ist unakzeptabel! Deswegen sorgen Sie bitte in der Kommission sehr dafÃ¼r, dass diese Idee niemals, niemals das Licht eines Vorschlags erblicken wird.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nikolina Brnjac (PPE). â PoÅ¡tovani predsjedavajuÄi, povjereniÄe, drage kolegice i kolege, viÅ¡e od dvije treÄine europskog stanovniÅ¡tva Å¾ivi u gradovima.

Oni su epicentri inovacija, stvaranja novih radnih mjesta i tranzicije prema zelenoj ekonomiji, a kroz razvoj gradova potiÄemo i razvoj okolnih ruralnih podruÄja. Zato je iznimno vaÅ¾no usmjeriti naÅ¡e napore na urbani razvoj. Moja zemlja Hrvatska ima dobru praksu kroz svoju novu urbanu agendu, gdje je, koristeÄi mehanizam integriranih teritorijalnih ulaganja, udvostruÄila sredstva te utrostruÄila broj obuhvaÄenih gradova. Kako bismo potaknuli ravnomjeran razvoj gradova kljuÄno je nastaviti jaÄati kohezijsku politiku i osigurati da se u predstojeÄem razdoblju smanji birokratsko optereÄenje te pojaÄa usmjerenost na strateÅ¡ke ciljeve, kao Å¡to je na primjer pristupaÄno stanovanje.

MeÄutim, treba jasno reÄi da to pojednostavljenje ne smije iÄi u smjeru smanjenja nadzora nad troÅ¡enjem europskih sredstava. Vrijeme je da svi shvatimo da regije i gradovi nisu samo administrativne jedinice, veÄ nositelji buduÄnosti Europske unije, naroÄito za naÅ¡e mlade kojima moramo osigurati dostojanstven Å¾ivot.

Â Â

Â Â Javi LÃ³pez (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, hoy hablamos sobre el importante papel que tienen las ciudades y las regiones en Europa, con dos mensajes.

En primer lugar, la necesidad de implicar y trabajar de la mano con las grandes ciudades y los grandes centros urbanos europeos. Â¿Por quÃ©? Porque se han convertido en bastiones de la tolerancia frente a las ideas extremistas, porque concentran y viven los grandes cambios que se estÃ¡n produciendo âtambiÃ©n que legislamos hoy aquÃ­, como la agenda verdeâ y porque, ademÃ¡s, hoy estÃ¡n reclamando ayuda para algunos de los problemas que son compartidos en toda Europa, como el acceso a la vivienda. Grandes alcaldÃ­as europeas, como la de Roma o la de Barcelona, han reclamado ayuda durante las Ãºltimas semanas y tenemos que dar respuesta en este mandato.

Y, en segundo lugar, el papel de las regiones. Ahora que circula esta idea tan peligrosa y tÃ³xica de que vamos a centralizar la gestiÃ³n de los fondos de cohesiÃ³n, hemos de enviar un mensaje de rechazo absoluto, por tratarse de una idea ineficiente y estÃºpida: porque rompe el principio de subsidiariedad y porque, ademÃ¡s, no permitirÃ­a estructurar, como se debe hacer, el trabajo que tienen que hacer las regiones, que son quienes realmente estructuran nuestra economÃ­a y nuestra sociedad. AsÃ­ que trabajemos con las regiones, no les demos la espalda.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marco Falcone (PPE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, nel momento storico che viviamo si sta affermando in maniera sempre piÃ¹ diffusa la necessitÃ  di rafforzare la competitivitÃ  dei Paesi dell'Unione. Lo ha detto anche il Presidente Mario Draghi, sentito qualche giorno fa in quest'Aula.

In questo senso Ã¨ bene ricordare che la Commissione europea e il Parlamento devono perÃ² guardare a un ruolo strategico delle cittÃ  e delle regioni d'Europa. Lo devono fare, perÃ², guardando all'importanza del governo locale, nello spirito e nell'armonia di uno sviluppo coerente, armonico, che possa riportare equilibrio in una crescita sostenibile, equilibrata e socialmente omogenea.

Ma in questo senso noi possiamo arrivare a questo obiettivo soltanto se non lasciamo nessuno indietro e mi riferisco a una politica che deve guardare alle isole, all'affermazione del principio dell'insularitÃ , cioÃ¨ di quei 20 milioni di europei che vivono nelle isole, con le difficoltÃ , i disagi, i collegamenti che sono messi a dura prova rispetto al resto del nostro continente.

Ecco perchÃ© noi, in questi cinque anni, dobbiamo rafforzare la politica locale e quindi lo sviluppo delle nostre regioni, tenendo conto di quella che deve essere una politica di coesione a cui il nostro trattato sul funzionamento dell'Unione europea guarda con l'articolo 174.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Camilla Laureti (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, nel 1968 Jean Rey, allora Presidente della Commissione, disse che la politica regionale era come il cuore del corpo umano: doveva portare la vita economica in tutti i territori.

Da allora la politica regionale Ã¨ diventata la politica di coesione e vale un terzo del bilancio. Questo cuore pulsante ha funzionato â a volte con difficoltÃ  â ma ha funzionato e dovrÃ  continuare a farlo, perchÃ© mai come oggi ci serve.

Divergenze e disuguaglianze aumentano e gli investimenti nella coesione sono fondamentali. NeiÂ left behind places si annidano populismi e disaffezione per l'Europa. La politica di coesione puÃ² aiutare il senso di appartenenza europea ma Ã¨ essenziale la centralitÃ  di regioni e comuni. Senza regioni e cittÃ , il cuore delle politiche di coesione non arriva in tutte le parti del corpo europeo.

La missione essenziale per i comuni e le regioni Ã¨ quella di costruirsi come istituzioni per la crescita, un tema scelto non a caso nella motivazione del premio Nobel per l'economia di quest'anno, sulla rilevanza delle istituzioni per la crescita economica.

No alla centralizzazione della spesa europea; sÃ¬ al coinvolgimento di comuni e regioni.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Antonio Decaro (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, prima di avere l'onore di essere qui con voi in quest'Aula, sono stato sindaco di una cittÃ  del sud Italia: una cittÃ , Bari, in una regione, la Puglia, che grazie ai fondi di coesione, ha potuto cambiare il proprio destino e il destino dei propri abitanti.

Oggi la scritta "finanziato dall'Unione europea" accompagna gli interventi piÃ¹ importanti presenti nelle cittÃ  dei nostri Paesi: asili nido, parchi, scuole, infrastrutture. Le cittÃ  sono il cuore di ogni cambiamento possibile: ambientale, sociale ed economico.

Nella mia terra, con i fondi di coesione, migliaia di giovani hanno potuto completare la loro formazione e le attivitÃ  produttive hanno avviato un percorso per essere piÃ¹ competitive. Questo Ã¨ stato possibile grazie ai fondi di gestione di queste risorse, che sono state delegate agli enti locali, istituzioni vicine ai territori.

Accentrare e allontanare questo processo dai territori significa aumentare la distanza tra i cittadini e l'Europa. Il nostro compito, invece, Ã¨ quello di essere vicini ai territori, ascoltarli per comprenderne specificitÃ  ed esigenze, per costruire insieme un'Europa piÃ¹ verde, piÃ¹ forte e piÃ¹ unita.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rosa Serrano Sierra (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, fortalecer la cohesiÃ³n de la UniÃ³n Europea es una tarea prioritaria que debe comenzar desde nuestros pueblos y nuestras regiones. Europa empieza en lo local, en los pueblos, y solo asÃ­ podemos garantizar un desarrollo verdaderamente equilibrado, sin dejar a ningÃºn territorio atrÃ¡s. Nuestras regiones pueden y deben ser motores de progreso.

Tenemos que recordar que es en las zonas rurales donde garantizamos nuestra producciÃ³n y nuestra seguridad alimentaria, donde se gestionan los recursos naturales, donde se mitiga el cambio climÃ¡tico y se protege el patrimonio cultural. Y, todo ello, a pesar de los grandes desafÃ­os que afrontan: despoblaciÃ³n, escasez de infraestructuras y difÃ­cil acceso a servicios bÃ¡sicos.

Ya existe una agenda urbana para la UniÃ³n Europea que aborda los desafÃ­os de las ciudades en la transiciÃ³n ecolÃ³gica. Â¿Por quÃ© no desarrollamos una agenda rural europea, una estrategia europea respaldada por planes rurales, regionales y nacionales, que ayude a nuestros territorios a superar las dificultades de la transiciÃ³n y que les ofrezca tambiÃ©n oportunidades para un futuro mejor?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dario Nardella (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, lo storico Jacques Le Goff ha scritto che l'identitÃ  europea nasce nel Medioevo ed Ã¨ nel Medioevo che nascono i primi comuni. Ã nel Medioevo che le cittÃ  affondano le loro radici: le nostre cittÃ , dove troviamo i monumenti, le chiese, le universitÃ , dove nascono le lingue che costituiscono il patrimonio culturale di identitÃ  dell'Europa.

Proprio per questo le cittÃ  sono oggi la vera sfida di un'Europa moderna, sostenibile e forte. E se Ã¨ vero che l'Europa si costruisce dal basso, signor Commissario, noi non possiamo accettare che venga smantellata la politica di coesione.

Non possiamo accettare che le cittÃ  e le regioni non siano protagoniste delle decisioni che si prendono per i nostri cittadini. Non possiamo accettare una centralizzazione del bilancio europeo e una sospensione dell'agenda urbana perchÃ©, come diceva David Sassoli, quando il cantiere dell'Europa chiuderÃ  e avremo l'Europa unita e forte, ci saranno le cittÃ  con la loro innovazione, con la loro pace e la loro forza.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sabrina Repp (S&D). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Keine Mitsprache fÃ¼r die Regionen? In diesen Tagen zeigt sich, wie ernst es Ursula von der Leyen mit einem Europa der Regionen meint. Laut einem internen Papier plant die Kommission, wesentliche FÃ¶rdermittel in der KohÃ¤sionspolitik zu zentralisieren; die Regionen bleiben auÃen vor. Diese Entscheidung betrifft zentrale Bereiche wie Landwirtschaft, Fischerei, regionale Entwicklung. Gerade lÃ¤ndliche Gebiete sind besonders auf diese FÃ¶rdermittel angewiesen. Ich komme von der OstseekÃ¼ste aus Mecklenburg-Vorpommern. Themen wie demografischer Wandel, Infrastruktur, Energiewende beschÃ¤ftigen uns seit Jahrzehnten. Wie sollen wir den Zusammenhalt in der EU stÃ¤rken, wenn Menschen vor Ort auÃen vor gelassen werden?

Wenn wir die Entscheidung den Nationalstaaten Ã¼berlassen, riskieren wir, die Interessen der lÃ¤ndlichen RÃ¤ume gegen industrielle Zentren auszuspielen. Hinzu kommt, dass auch das Parlament als HaushaltsbehÃ¶rde den Einfluss Ã¼ber diese Mittel verlieren wÃ¼rde.

Eine Ã¶kologische, soziale und lokale Entwicklung geht nicht von oben herab. Wir mÃ¼ssen die Regionen stÃ¤rken, statt ihnen ihre Mitsprache zu entziehen, fÃ¼r ein Europa der Regionen, das zusammenwÃ¤chst und den Menschen gerecht wird: Das ist unsere Vision!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Raffaele Topo (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il futuro della politica di coesione deve restare nelle competenze delle cittÃ  e delle regioni. Le istituzioni territoriali devono essere strumenti chiave: vanno usati per aiutare l'Europa ad affrontare la transizione verde, digitale e inclusiva.

Chi, come me, ha avuto esperienza in questi settori â sono stato sindaco, consigliere regionale â sa perfettamente quanto Ã¨ importante questa scelta, che ha anche un impatto positivo sull'opinione dei nostri cittadini nei confronti di questa stessa istituzione. Noi dobbiamo prestare attenzione all'opinione che la cittadinanza europea ha su di noi. Tante conquiste si sono realizzate con questi fondi: non c'erano grandi alternative.

E la fiducia dei cittadini nelle autoritÃ  regionali e locali Ã¨ in aumento negli ultimi anni. E questo perchÃ© le regioni e le cittÃ  sono protagoniste dirette dell'attuazione di gran parte della legislazione europea, degli investimenti pubblici, della spesa pubblica, solo per dare qualche elemento.

Tutti questi dati confermano che la politica di coesione va riformata, certo, ma nella direzione della semplificazione delle procedure, dell'accelerazione della spesa, magari della previsione di qualche controllo. Centralizzarla Ã¨ un errore.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marko VeÅ¡ligaj (S&D). â PoÅ¡tovani predsjedavajuÄi, kolege, ako nam je stalo do svakoga stanovnika i svakoga podruÄja Europske unije, tada moramo posvetiti jednak znaÄaj manjim urbanim srediÅ¡tima, ruralnim jedinicama, brdskim podruÄjima, otocima, pograniÄnim podruÄjima, kao Å¡to to posveÄujemo, primjerice velikim gradovima.

Danas, naÅ¾alost, to nije sluÄaj i mi nemamo ravnomjerni regionalni razvoj. Hrvatska se tu posebno istiÄe, jer sva ova navedena podruÄja suoÄavaju se s ozbiljnim problemima, od nedostatka priuÅ¡tivih stanova, loÅ¡eg javnog prijevoza koji oteÅ¾ava pristup obrazovanju, zdravstvenim uslugama i trÅ¾iÅ¡tu rada pa sve do manjkave infrastrukture. Mladi napuÅ¡taju ova podruÄja, a lokalne samouprave nemaju fiskalni kapacitet da rijeÅ¡e ove izazove. Dobro to znam kao gradonaÄelnik Pregrade. Zato kohezijska politika mora ponuditi rjeÅ¡enja kroz financiranje lokalnih i regionalnih projekata. Suradnja svih razina vlasti i pojednostavljen pristup tim sredstvima mogu znaÄajno poboljÅ¡ati stanje.

NaÅ¡a manja urbana srediÅ¡ta, ruralne zajednice regije su rjeÅ¡enje, a ne problem. Vrijeme je da im osiguramo potrebna financijska sredstva.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AodhÃ¡n Ã RÃ­ordÃ¡in (S&D). â Mr President, I am from Europe's greatest city, Dublin. I love Dublin's people, its history. I love its energy. I ran for this office because of a racist riot that took place in my native city last November. Like many cities in Europe, Dublin is facing some very serious challenges. The city feels less safe. We have drug issues, we have transport problems and our city centre is in urgent need of revitalisation.

I was interested to read the Dublin City Task Force report this week. I hope it can be a catalyst for a conversation about the city we want.

We want all European cities to be places where people can socialise, go out and watch a match, have access to culture and nightlife but most importantly to be a place where people can afford to live. A place to be proud of.

Dublin needs affordable housing. Europe needs affordable housing. It needs to be a place where people on ordinary incomes can raise their families. We must deliver an EU affordable housing plan to tackle the housing crisis and one that ends homelessness.

And we do this for the love of Europe, but also for the love of Dublin.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Stefano Bonaccini (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, regioni e comuni si trovano stretti tra due trend allarmanti: gli effetti dell'emergenza climatica, che li colpisce con frequenza impressionante, e un drammatico calo della natalitÃ  e forza lavoro sempre piÃ¹ anziana.

Di fronte a questo scenario, i governi locali dovranno rimanere protagonisti nelle decisioni e disporre di maggiori fondi per coesione sociale e sviluppo sostenibile. In Emilia Romagna, che ho guidato gli ultimi dieci anni, grazie ai fondi europei, siamo l'unica regione italiana oggi a garantire asili nido gratuiti in tutti i comuni delle aree interne, nei comuni montani.

CosÃ¬ come abbiamo acquistato il super computer Leonardo, che in un secondo processa 250 milioni di miliardi di operazioni di calcolo per conto del Centro Meteo Europeo e dell'UniversitÃ  delle Nazioni Unite. Contribuiremo cosÃ¬ allo studio e al contrasto al cambiamento climatico.

Diventeremo la principale data valley europea, attraendo nuove imprese, ma soprattutto studenti, ricercatori e docenti da tutto il mondo, attraverso imponenti investimenti su nuove tecnologie e intelligenza artificiale.

Per questi motivi, Commissario, ci schieriamo al fianco di regioni e cittÃ  per scongiurare di vederle estromesse dal prendere decisioni cruciali per il loro futuro.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

VystÃºpenia na zÃ¡klade prihlÃ¡senia sa o slovo zdvihnutÃ­m ruky

Â Â Nina Carberry (PPE). â Mr President, Commissioner, in Ireland, we have witnessed first hand the transformative power of cohesion funding. It has fostered innovation, strengthened local economies and empowered communities.

As we engage in discussions about the next programming period of cohesion policy, we must remember the fundamental principles that underpin this essential framework: economic, social and territorial cohesion. They reflect our collective commitment to ensuring that every citizen has the right to stay in the place they call home, supported by robust public services, digital connectivity and opportunity for skill development.

We must ensure that these successes are not merely fleeting achievements, but become the foundation for our future. It is our responsibility to ensure that every region, every local authority, has better access to the funding necessary to foster growth and development.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Èi noi vrem sÄ ne gÃ¢ndim acum, la orice (cÃ¢teva cuvinte care nu se aud) cÄ avem probleme Ã®n oraÈele mai mici, dar Èi Ã®n comune. Evident cÄ avem probleme, dar haideÈi sÄ ne gÃ¢ndim la modele de finanÈare ÈintitÄ, directÄ, pe sÄnÄtate, pe Ècoli, pe transport Ã®n localitatea respectivÄ Èi, de ce nu, pe meÈteÈuguri, pe dezvoltarea turismului rural.

Zonele, oraÈele mici nu vor avea Èi cetÄÈenii n-au aceeaÈi ÈansÄ Èi este nedrept pentru cÄ Ästa este sloganul Uniunii Europene: nimeni sÄ nu fie lÄsat Ã®n urmÄ.

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). â Arvoisa puhemies, EU ei elÃ¤ pelkistÃ¤ kaupungeista, vaan harvaan asutut alueet ovat tÃ¤rkeÃ¤ osa jÃ¤senvaltioiden menestystÃ¤. Erityisesti EU:n itÃ¤isellÃ¤ ulkorajalla tÃ¤mÃ¤ on myÃ¶s turvallisuuskysymys. Ne pÃ¤Ã¤tÃ¶kset, joita teemme esimerkiksi ilmastopolitiikan osalta, vaikuttavat suoraan siihen, millaiset edellytykset vaikkapa ItÃ¤-Suomessa on elÃ¤Ã¤.

TÃ¤mÃ¤ on syytÃ¤ ymmÃ¤rtÃ¤Ã¤. Alueet eivÃ¤t pysy elinvoimaisina vain sillÃ¤, ettÃ¤ vuodesta toiseen kanavoimme ympÃ¤ri Eurooppaa uusia aluetukia. Varsinkaan Suomen kaltaisten nettomaksajien kannalta nykyisessÃ¤ jÃ¤rjestelmÃ¤ssÃ¤ ei ole jÃ¤rjen hÃ¤ivÃ¤Ã¤. Maksamme jÃ¤senmaksuna valtavan summan, josta vain murto-osa tulee takaisin omaan aluekehitykseemme. Sitten eri projektien varteen pystytetÃ¤Ã¤n kylttejÃ¤, joissa kerrotaan, miten EU on kyseisen projektin mahdollistanut. Ei se nÃ¤in ole. JÃ¤senmaat sen ovat mahdollistaneet, ja juuri siksi suurempi osa asioista pitÃ¤Ã¤ pÃ¤Ã¤ttÃ¤Ã¤ jÃ¤senvaltioissa, ei BrysselissÃ¤.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Niels Geuking (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Eine direkte StÃ¤rkung von StÃ¤dten und Regionen muss fÃ¼r uns Europapolitiker einen sehr hohen Stellenwert haben. Neben dem Abgeordnetenmandat bin ich noch kommunalpolitisch verwurzelt und Ã¼be weiterhin mein Stadtrats- und Kreistagsmandat in meiner Heimat aus und kann Ihnen aus erster Hand die Sorgen, Ãngste und NÃ¶te der Kommunalpolitik nÃ¤herbringen.

Es ist wirklich unerlÃ¤sslich, dass wir die Regionen aktiv mitnehmen und fÃ¶rdern, so dass die Menschen vor Ort sehen, dass die EU einen positiven und sichtbaren Einfluss auf das Leben der Menschen vor Ort hat. Ohne Ã¶rtliche Sichtbarkeit ist es sehr einfach fÃ¼r die extremen RÃ¤nder in unserer Gesellschaft, die EU zu diffamieren und letztendlich als Ã¼berflÃ¼ssig darzustellen.

Verlieren wir die Bindung zu den Regionen, unseren Regionen, verlieren wir letztendlich auch womÃ¶glich die EU. Eine starke Regionalpolitik kreiert WachstumsmÃ¶glichkeiten und MÃ¤rkte und sichert die Zukunft von Millionen Familien und die Perspektive der nachfolgenden Generationen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Fernando LÃ³pez Aguilar (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, cuando hablamos del papel de las regiones en el desarrollo de la UniÃ³n Europea, yo hablo de regiones ultraperifÃ©ricas: nueve, entre ellas Canarias, las Ãºnicas mencionadas por su nombre en el Tratado de Lisboa, que tiene una base especÃ­fica para protegerlas y singularizar la respuesta.

Y los diputados nacidos o residentes en regiones ultraperifÃ©ricas hemos dirigido una carta a la seÃ±ora Von der Leyen diciÃ©ndole claro que la idea peregrina de centralizar los fondos de cohesiÃ³n es inaceptable y particularmente lesiva para las regiones ultraperifÃ©ricas, que tienen que ser decisivas en la transiciÃ³n de sus modelos econÃ³micos dependientes de algÃºn monocultivo âen la agriculturaâ o del turismo hacia la economÃ­a azul, la economÃ­a circular y, por supuesto, su incorporaciÃ³n âa travÃ©s de la innovaciÃ³n, la conectividad, con ayudas europeas y las energÃ­as renovablesâ al futuro de la UniÃ³n Europea, protegiÃ©ndolas tambiÃ©n frente a acuerdos comerciales desiguales que requieren clÃ¡usulas especÃ­ficas con respecto de productos sensibles o vulnerables.

Eso es hacer polÃ­tica regional y eso es contar tambiÃ©n con el potencial de las regiones ultraperifÃ©ricas para hacer Europa.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Maravillas AbadÃ­a Jover (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, si de verdad queremos un avance de nuestras regiones y ciudades y que sean motor de desarrollo del proyecto europeo, debemos darles su merecido rol en la polÃ­tica europea. Nuestros ayuntamientos y gobiernos regionales estÃ¡n viendo cÃ³mo, en muchas ocasiones, se les estÃ¡ obstaculizando su acceso a la cofinanciaciÃ³n, bien porque deben anticipar los fondos que no tienen, bien porque el Gobierno central no los tiene en cuenta.

La UniÃ³n Europea no necesita un Next GenerationÂ 2.0. Lo que nuestras regiones y ayuntamientos necesitan es autonomÃ­a y flexibilidad para gestionar los fondos de cohesiÃ³n de manera eficiente, pues no podemos depender de las intenciones ideolÃ³gicas de un Gobierno central como el de EspaÃ±a para que nuestras regiones prosperen.

La centralizaciÃ³n de los fondos europeos es contraproducente en Estados tan descentralizados como los nuestros. Es fundamental que demos a las autoridades locales el poder que necesitan para desarrollar, diseÃ±ar e implementar estos proyectos. Solo asÃ­ daremos a nuestras ciudades y regiones la importancia que se merecen en el futuro del proyecto europeo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Koniec vystÃºpenÃ­ na zÃ¡klade prihlÃ¡senia sa o slovo zdvihnutÃ­m ruky)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members, I want to thank you for giving this important topic the attention it deserves and requires. I also want to thank you for your positive appreciation for the action of the Commission after the recent floods in some Member States. I also want to thank you for the many ideas and concrete proposals, but I take notice of your concerns and fears, and I want to answer maybe to some questions concerning the partnership, the necessity to have a partnership with the cities and and the regions.

Regions and cities will remain at the centre of our work, as we ensure that the EU cohesion and growth policies address the existing and new challenges that are arising as we go forward. The partnership principle is already at the core of programming and implementation of cohesion policy, and helps bringing the European Union closer to citizens.

I would like to highlight that we need genuine partnerships with regional and local level at the core to ensure high quality and impactful investments. With a strong and well-functioning partnership, we also make our investment more inclusive, more transparent and closer to the people and communities directly affected by them.

It is clear that we need to work with the regions and the cities. As stated in the political guidelines of the President-elect. We will need to address regional and social disparities and ensure all citizens have an effective right to stay in the place they call home. As part of this, we will need to mobilise reforms and investments to help build what the community needs to thrive. Public services and private activities, education and skills, transport and digital connectivity.

As for cities, the mission letter of the Executive Vice-President designate, Mr Fitto, tasks him to put forward an ambitious policy agenda for cities. This agenda should provide a clear vision for the future of the cities, looking at issues such as housing, climate action, digitalisation, mobility, social inclusion and equality.

To conclude, the importance of cities and regions is recognised at European level. European policies and in particular cohesion policy and various European initiatives already support local development. It is clear that the accumulation of challenges requires a stronger policy framework, built together with cities and regional authorities, and we are grateful for the support we will see from this House in such a kind of policy.

So thank you for that, and we will continue to have a real partnership with the regions and the cities.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â PredsedajÃºci . â TÃ¡to rozprava sa skonÄila.

Â 

15. Foreign interference and hybrid attacks: the need to strengthen EU resilience and internal security (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PredsedajÃºci . â ÄalÅ¡Ã­m bodom programu je vyhlÃ¡senie Komisie o zahraniÄnom zasahovanÃ­ a hybridnÃ½ch Ãºtokoch: potreba posilniÅ¥ odolnosÅ¥ EÃ a medzinÃ¡rodnÃº bezpeÄnosÅ¥ (2024/2884(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, as PresidentÂ von der Leyen said in our political guidelines, all democratic systems and institutions are under attack and the threats to our democracies are rising. These threats undermine our security and target the foundations of all democracies. We must remain vigilant to these threats, build our own resilience and be ready to respond.

With this in mind, in DecemberÂ 2023, we presented the defence of democracy package. As a key element, we proposed a directive laying down common transparency and accountability standards for interest-representation activities on behalf of third countries. Thanks to this proposal, citizens will know who is behind different interest representations. We count on Parliament's support on this text.

The Commission also issued a recommendation on inclusive and resilient elections. In March this year, we published guidelines for elections under the Digital Services Act. This set out what the largest online players should do to mitigate systemic risks that may impact the integrity of elections. In April, the regulation on the transparency of political advertising entered into force, giving citizens the possibility to see who is behind political advertisements and how much they cost.

We are in the process of analysing information operations carried out during the European elections. This information will be part of an extensive Commission report on the 2024 European Parliament elections, which will be published next year. For now, we can say that the elections were successfully organised and the data that is available now provides no evidence to indicate major incidents with a decisive impact on the election results. But online information interference operations were very much present.

We will continue strengthening the protection of all democratic systems and institutions and improve their resilience. The political guidelines set out plans for the new European Democracy Shield. This will represent a new phase in countering foreign information manipulation, interference and disinformation. The aim is to strengthen situational awareness and bolster our collective ability to detect, analyse and proactively counter threats. To boost societal resilience, we will step up support for digital and media literacy and kerb disinformation through debunking and awareness-raising projects. We will further advance the work the External Action Service has built up over the past ten years to better understand and address foreign information manipulation and interference, including through the FIMI toolbox, the EU Rapid Alert System, and with our international partners, the G7 Rapid Response Mechanism.

We will also consolidate our ongoing enforcement of the Digital Services Act. The next step is to quickly convert the Code of Practice on Disinformation into a code of conduct under the DSA. In protecting our democracy, we will always respect our enduring commitment to preserving freedom of expression and media freedom. In this context, we will facilitate the swift implementation of the European Media Freedom Act and we will further support independent media and journalists. The democracy shield will articulate with the priorities announced in the political guidelines on the rule of law, security and preparedness.

A new internal security strategy will set out our approach to all threats online and offline, and ensure that security is integrated in EU legislation and policies by design. The pressing need for strong cyber security became most obvious when critical infrastructure was targeted in cyber attacks, with theft of valuable data and espionage. Common rules to improve the cybersecurity of all essential and important entities and streamlining incident reporting and requirements are set out in the next two directives. These are complemented by the Critical Entities Resilience Directive for physical risk to critical infrastructure.

With the Cyber Resilience Act, we have a new law mandating security by design for all digital products to be sold on our market. The Cyber Solidarity Solidarity Act â in the final stages of adoption â will be a game changer for cyber-threat detection. A whole-of-government and a whole-of-society effort is needed to increase our preparedness for future crises, in full alignment with fundamental rights.

We have stepped up our actions against Russia's hybrid activities, most recently with the adoption of a dedicated sanction regime. We also have new tools in place, such as the EU hybrid toolbox and the hybrid rapid response teams, which provide a quick response to hybrid threats and campaigns.

Dear President, honourable Members, we have done a lot to protect our democracy from foreign interference and hybrid threats, but there is still a lot to be done. So I'm looking forward to the debate on this issue, but also to your proposals, your remarks and your hard work to advance on the different pieces of legislation that are on your table.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lena DÃ¼pont, im Namen der PPE-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar! Seit drei Jahren kÃ¤mpfen Ukrainer um ihr Leben, ihr Land, ihre SouverÃ¤nitÃ¤t. Dank ihres Kampfes kommt Russland mit seinen Zielen bisher nicht voran und reagiert mit immer heftigeren Angriffen auf zivile Einrichtungen.

Weil sie nicht vorankommen, setzen sie auch auf hybride Attacken in der EUÂ â hybride Attacken gegen alle, die die Ukraine unterstÃ¼tzen, gegen alle, die freiheitliche Werte vertreten, gegen alle, die den demokratischen europÃ¤ischen Weg gehen wollen, innerhalb und auÃerhalb der EU, mit VerbÃ¼ndeten anderer Regime, die ihre Ablehnung von Freiheit und Demokratie teilen.

Wie sich das Ã¤uÃert? Im GPS-Jamming, das den zivilen Luftverkehr gefÃ¤hrdet, Ã¼ber Nacht verschobene Grenzbojen, nur um mal die Reaktionen zu testen, DoppelgÃ¤ngerkampagnen als Teil des Informationskrieges; wir sehen diese so zynische und ausgeklÃ¼gelte Instrumentalisierung von Migration, das Nutzen der Strukturen organisierter KriminalitÃ¤t entweder als Proxys oder fÃ¼r Dienstleistungen; wir sehen Angriffe auf Logistikdienstleister, auf Bahninfrastruktur, auf Trinkwasserversorgung, Cyberangriffe auf kritische Infrastruktur, Unternehmen und Verwaltung; wir sehen Desinformationskampagnen, die auf das Herz unserer Gesellschaft zielen: den Zusammenhalt und demokratische Prozesse.

All das geht nahezu lehrbuchmÃ¤Ãig entlang der Kriterien Desinformation, TÃ¤uschung, StÃ¶rung, Destabilisierung und ZerstÃ¶rung. So let's face it, folks: Wir sind bereits mittendrin, wir sind auch angegriffen.

Wir mÃ¼ssen dagegen aufstehen und uns verteidigen. Wie? Indem wir die Ukraine endlich konsequent unterstÃ¼tzen; indem wir unseren Worten konsequent Taten folgen lassen; indem wir unsere SicherheitsbehÃ¶rden national und europÃ¤isch in die Lage versetzen, rechtlich und mit ausreichenden Ressourcen Sicherheit zu gewÃ¤hrleisten; indem wir unsere Infrastruktur schÃ¼tzen; indem wir die zivil-militÃ¤rische Zusammenarbeit straffen, ausbauen und besser miteinander verzahnen; indem wir all diejenigen, die im Katastrophen-, im Zivil- und BevÃ¶lkerungsschutz eine Verantwortung tragen, stÃ¼tzen, sie schÃ¼tzen und sie bestmÃ¶glichst ausstatten; indem wir uns als Gesellschaft, als Individuen schÃ¼tzen und unsere Resilienz hochfahren.

Denn am Ende steht: Better safe than sorry.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hannes Heide, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar! Hybride Bedrohungen, Cyberangriffe nehmen zu, ebenso Desinformationskampagnen und Angriffe auf demokratische Wahlen. Die anhaltende Einflussnahme von Drittstaaten bedroht unsere Demokratie und zielt darauf ab, unsere freie Gesellschaft in Europa zu destabilisieren. Und auch wir im EuropÃ¤ischen Parlament erleben, wie konkret diese Bedrohung ist.

Tausende Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter unseres Hauses waren betroffen. Personenbezogene Informationen wurden verÃ¶ffentlicht, nachdem das People Portal gehackt wurde. Cyberangriffe auf die Tschechische Republik, auf Estland oder auf eine Webkonferenz mit hochrangigen Offizieren tragen die gleiche Handschrift von Hackergruppen aus dem Umfeld des russischen MilitÃ¤rgeheimdienstes.

Im PEGA-Untersuchungsausschuss haben wir eineinhalb Jahre lang akribisch an LÃ¶sungen gearbeitet, den unrechtmÃ¤Ãigen Einsatz von Spyware in der EU zu unterbinden. Aber allein in diesem Jahr sind sieben neue Angriffe dokumentiert. Das verdeutlicht, wie wichtig es ist, dass Europa koordiniert handelt und diesen Angriffen auf unsere Grundwerte, auf unsere Lebensweise in Rechtsstaatlichkeit und Freiheit konsequent entgegentritt.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AndrÃ¡s LÃ¡szlÃ³, a PfE kÃ©pviselÅcsoport nevÃ©ben. â Tisztelt ElnÃ¶k Ãr! 2022-ben MagyarorszÃ¡g a legnagyobb kÃ¼lfÃ¶ldi beavatkozÃ¡st tapasztalta meg a rendszervÃ¡ltoztatÃ¡s Ã³ta. A magyar baloldal gyanÃºs NGO-kon Ã©s kÃ¶zvetÃ­tÅkÃ¶n keresztÃ¼l tÃ¶bb, mint tÃ­zmilliÃ³ dollÃ¡r kÃ¼lfÃ¶ldi tÃ¡mogatÃ¡st kapott a parlamenti vÃ¡lasztÃ¡si kampÃ¡nyÃ¡hoz az amerikai baloldaltÃ³l. Mindannyian Ã¶sszeÃ¡lltak Ã©s haszonÃ©lvezÅi voltak: a szocialistÃ¡k, a liberÃ¡lisok, a zÃ¶ldek, Ã©s bevettÃ©k maguk kÃ¶zÃ© mÃ©g a rasszista Jobbikot is.

A koalÃ­ciÃ³juk vezetÅje mÃ¶gÃ© pedig beÃ¡llt az EPP kÃ©t vezetÅje, Manfred Weber Ã©s Donald Tusk is. HatalomvÃ¡gytÃ³l vezÃ©relve a legalapvetÅbb demokratikus elveket is felrÃºgtÃ¡k. Ãs amikor kiderÃ¼lt, Ã¶sszevissza hazudoztak arrÃ³l, hogy honnan jÃ¶tt a pÃ©nz, a magyar emberek pedig joggal voltak felhÃ¡borodva. A kutatÃ¡sok szerint tÃ¶bb mint 60 szÃ¡zalÃ©k gondolta azt, hogy elfogadhatatlan az, hogy politikai kampÃ¡nyokat kÃ¼lfÃ¶ldrÅl finanszÃ­rozzanak.

Az Ãºjabb vÃ¡lasztÃ¡si beavatkozÃ¡s megelÅzÃ©se Ã©rdekÃ©ben MagyarorszÃ¡g, a magyar parlament elfogadta a szuverenitÃ¡svÃ©delmi tÃ¶rvÃ©nyt. TudjÃ¡k, mi tÃ¶rtÃ©nt? Az EurÃ³pai BizottsÃ¡g kÃ¶telezettsÃ©gszegÃ©si eljÃ¡rÃ¡st indÃ­tott Ã©s bÃ­rÃ³sÃ¡g elÃ© vitte az Ã¼gyet. De a NÃ©ppÃ¡rt sem tanult a botrÃ¡nybÃ³l. NÃ©hÃ¡ny hete Manfred Weber Ã©pp itt, pont ezen a helyen Ã¡llva kijelÃ¶lte, hogy BrÃ¼sszel Ãºj jelÃ¶ltje a mentelmi joga mÃ¶gÃ¶tt bujkÃ¡lÃ³ Magyar PÃ©ter.

Az 1956-os forradalom Ã©vfordulÃ³ja van ma. Ez alkalombÃ³l is nyomatÃ©kosÃ­tom: nem fogunk eltÅ±rni semmilyen kÃ¼lfÃ¶ldi beavatkozÃ¡st, Ã©s MagyarorszÃ¡g szuverenitÃ¡sÃ¡t mindig Ã©s mindenkitÅl meg fogjuk vÃ©deni!

Â Â Beata SzydÅo, w imieniu grupy ECR. â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Szanowny panie komisarzu! JeÅ¼eli rozmawiamy dzisiaj o dezinformacji i o prowokacjach, to ja czekam, aÅ¼ ktoÅ wreszcie przeprosi polskie sÅuÅ¼by, ktÃ³re zamknÄÅy ruskiego szpiega Gonzaleza. AleÅ¼ to wtedy byÅ rwetes i wrzask w wielu Årodowiskach, ktÃ³re rzuciÅy oskarÅ¼enia, Å¼e Polacy przetrzymujÄ i gnÄbiÄ niewinnego dziennikarza.

Sprawa Gonzaleza pokazuje bardziej zÅoÅ¼onÄ sytuacjÄ, jeÅ¼eli chodzi o dezinformacjÄ i rosyjskie wpÅywy w poszczegÃ³lnych krajach Unii Europejskiej. My koncentrujemy siÄ i mÃ³wimy tutaj przede wszystkim o tej pÅaszczyÅºnie cyfrowej. Niezwykle istotnej oczywiÅcie, i musi to byÄ pod szczegÃ³lnÄ kontrolÄ, ale Gonzalez byÅ pupilem wielu salonÃ³w liberalno-lewicowych w Polsce i wtedy, kiedy zostaÅ zamkniÄty â tak jak juÅ¼ wczeÅniej wspomniaÅam â natychmiast pojawiÅy siÄ oskarÅ¼enia, Å¼e niewinny dziennikarz, guru dla wielu tych elit zostaÅ niesÅusznie zatrzymany. Natomiast kiedy wylÄdowaÅ w Moskwie i byÅ witany przez Putina, te gÅosy zamilkÅy, ale teÅ¼ nikt sÅuÅ¼b nie przeprosiÅ. To pokazuje, Å¼e nie powinna nas interesowaÄ tylko ta przestrzeÅ cyfrowa, ale trzeba rÃ³wnieÅ¼ skoncentrowaÄ siÄ na tym, co dzieje siÄ wÅrÃ³d elit liberalno-lewicowych, tych mediÃ³w, ktÃ³re dyktujÄ teÅ¼ opinii publicznej pewne standardy i narzucajÄ narracjÄ.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Helmut BrandstÃ¤tter, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Mr President, Mr Commissioner, we Europeans, we have an idea of freedom and democracy based on enlightenment. Dictators have a plan to destroy our life, to destroy our values. And part of the values, Mr LÃ¡szlÃ³, is media freedom.

Putin attacked Ukraine with rockets, but he also attacks us by other means. Also Russia, China, Iran and other illiberal actors, they are waging a hybrid war against our democracy. This information in the media, bribing governments, bribing people, as we saw in Moldova, sabotaging our infrastructure, these are their weapons.

So, ladies and gentlemen, we also have to have a plan. We have to have a plan to defend our democracy. What do we need? You mentioned it, Mr Commissioner, it's a cyber security. I think it's cooperation of the different countries, also of the services, cooperation with countries we can trust. And I hope that the new Austrian Government will be a government where you also can trust. We already had the Interior minister, where we know other services did not trust him.

And we need, of course, a hub of exchange. We have to talk to each other. We have to inform each other about the attacks against our freedom and our democracy. So we have a plan. We need a plan. We have to work together. Freedom, democracy, our values. We will save it for the children and their children.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alexandra Geese, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, sehr verehrter Herr Kommissar, sehr verehrte Damen und Herren! TrÃ¤gt eigentlich die moldawische PrÃ¤sidentin Maia Sandu ein Kopftuch? Nein, natÃ¼rlich nicht. Aber in russischen Desinformationskampagnen im Internet wird sie stÃ¤ndig so dargestellt, und das ist einer der GrÃ¼nde, warum letzte Woche das Referendum zugunsten der EU nur so knapp gewonnen wurde. Denn Deepfakes und VerschwÃ¶rungsideologien erreichen die Menschen in Moldawien viel mehr als jede Form von objektiver Berichterstattung, gepusht von den Algorithmen der sozialen Netzwerke, die LÃ¼gen genauso belohnen wie rassistische oder sexistische Stereotype, aber seriÃ¶se Presse und echte Fakten ausbremsen. Und das ist leider nicht nur ein moldawisches Problem.

Das passiert in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union ganz genausoÂ â mit einem Unterschied: Wir kÃ¶nnen es Ã¤ndern. Auf der Grundlage des Gesetzes Ã¼ber digitale Dienste kÃ¶nnen und mÃ¼ssen wir Desinformation als systemisches Risiko fÃ¼r Ã¶ffentlichen Diskurs und Wahlen definieren. Und dann mÃ¼ssen wir dafÃ¼r sorgen, dass auf dieser Grundlage die Tech-Unternehmen ihre Algorithmen so gestalten, dass die Nutzerinnen und Nutzer auswÃ¤hlen, was in ihren Timelines landet, was sie im Internet sehen. Denn heute entscheiden das Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg oder bei TikTok die BefehlsempfÃ¤nger der chinesischen Regierung. Und das ist an uns jetzt zu Ã¤ndern.

Und ich mÃ¶chte noch auf eines hinweisen: Der Fall Maia Sandu zeigt einen Aspekt, der immer unterbelichtet ist. Frauen sind nÃ¤mlich besonders betroffen von Desinformationskampagnen, sind hÃ¤ufiger Zielscheibe von russischen und rechtsextremen Kampagnen, und die Algorithmen verbreiten Angriffe auf Frauen besonders. Und auch das muss imÂ Democracy Shield berÃ¼cksichtigt werden.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

ÐÑÐ¾Ð±ÐµÐ½Ð¾ Ð°ÐºÑÐ¸Ð²Ð½Ð¾ ÑÑÑÐ±Ð²Ð° Ð´Ð° ÑÐµ Ð±Ð¾ÑÐ¸ ÑÑÐµÑÑ Ð¾Ð½Ð°Ð·Ð¸ Ð´ÐµÐ·Ð¸Ð½ÑÐ¾ÑÐ¼Ð°ÑÐ¸Ñ, ÐºÐ¾ÑÑÐ¾ ÑÐ¾Ð±ÑÑÐ²ÐµÐ½Ð¸ÑÐµ Ð¼Ñ Ð»Ð¸Ð´ÐµÑÐ¸ ÑÐ°Ð·Ð¿ÑÐ¾ÑÑÑÐ°Ð½ÑÐ²Ð°Ñ.
