Â Â MirosÅawa Nykiel (PPE). â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Jak dobitnie pokazaÅy wydarzenia w MoÅdawii, zagroÅ¼enia w postaci zewnÄtrznych ingerencji, w tym przypadku Rosji, ataki hybrydowe sÄ realne i wpÅywajÄ na losy obywateli, a czasem mogÄ zmieniÄ bieg historii. Na szczÄÅcie, pomimo wysiÅkÃ³w Federacji Rosyjskiej i przeznaczenia znacznych nakÅadÃ³w na finansowanie propagandy i kupowanie gÅosÃ³w wyborcÃ³w, MoÅdawia wybraÅa kierunek proeuropejski. Brawa dla MoÅdawii.

Ale ten przykÅad to waÅ¼na lekcja dla wszystkich paÅstw czÅonkowskich, bo pokazuje, jak zewnÄtrzne interwencje umoÅ¼liwiajÄ przeciwnikom politycznym wpÅywanie na opiniÄ publicznÄ, destabilizacjÄ politycznÄ oraz erozjÄ zaufania do instytucji paÅstwa, a w konsekwencji mogÄ prowadziÄ do powaÅ¼nych turbulencji politycznych, gospodarczych i spoÅecznych. Kluczowym krokiem w przeciwdziaÅaniu tym zagroÅ¼eniom jest stworzenie wspÃ³lnego systemu mechanizmÃ³w wymiany informacji dla wszystkich paÅstw czÅonkowskich.

WaÅ¼ne jest takÅ¼e zwiÄkszenie inwestycji w cyberbezpieczeÅstwo, aby chroniÄ infrastrukturÄ krytycznÄ oraz systemy informatyczne. Ponadto edukowanie obywateli na temat dezinformacji i umiejÄtnoÅci rozpoznawania faÅszywych informacji powinno staÄ siÄ priorytetem, aby wzmocniÄ spoÅecznÄ odpornoÅÄ na manipulacjÄ.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PRESIDENZA: ANTONELLA SBERNA
Vicepresidente

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tobias Cremer (S&D). â Madam President, dear colleagues, we've just heard that disinformation is vague and not really a threat to our society, that we can't measure it, and that we might not be able to see that it's all a fluke made up by Eurocrats.

But I just came back from the electoral mission in observing the elections in Moldova, and I can tell you it's real. What we saw there, not just on Election Day, but over the weeks and months ahead, is a whole new level of very concrete Russian hybrid warfare, of large scale disinformation, illicit funding of pro-Russia candidates and, most staggeringly, unprecedented levels of vote buying.

And let's not be naive, this is not just about Moldova. This is not just about Georgia in its upcoming elections. This is about our very own countries. It's about Germany's elections next year. And it's about every single election in Europe, because Putin is scared of every single society which is democratic and therefore successful, and he will do his utmost to sabotage it. And this is why, colleagues, we must do our utmost to strengthen our democracies, to strengthen our resilience, and to demonstrate to Putin that he is no match for the free societies of Europe.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Reinier Van Lanschot (Verts/ALE), blue-card question. â Thank you for this speech. You ended by saying that you want to strengthen our democracy. Can you give a few examples of what you think we should concretely do?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tobias Cremer (S&D), blue-card answer. â Thank you very much for the blue card. And I think that's the absolutely essential question. I think the first thing is to stop being naive and to be honest to our populations, to tell them that this is happening. I have full trust in our societies. I don't want to fight propaganda with counter propaganda.

What I want is to tell people what is happening and they can make up their own minds. The Russians are playing with foul game, but we stand above that. We must be very clear. And this is what we should do as politicians, what the media should do, what civil society should do and it is to show these cases, these blatant cases of Russian interference.Â And to be honest in the debate about that, and I'm very confident that this will be the best way to challenge that.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Aleksandar Nikolic (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, en maiÂ 2024, la France a Ã©tÃ© secouÃ©e par des Ã©meutes insurrectionnelles en Nouvelle-CalÃ©donie. Nous avons assistÃ© Ã  de vÃ©ritables scÃ¨nes de guerre. On y a dÃ©plorÃ© dix morts, dont deux gendarmes. Ces Ã©meutes portent la marque de Bakou. Nous le savons, lâAzerbaÃ¯djan a soutenu et financÃ© les mouvements indÃ©pendantistes avec la volontÃ© de dÃ©stabiliser la France.

Aujourdâhui, alors que la Martinique sâembrase, lâinfluence azerbaÃ¯djanaise est avÃ©rÃ©e. Le 20Â juillet de cette annÃ©e, des indÃ©pendantistes martiniquais sont allÃ©s Ã  Bakou pour se mutualiser sous lâÃ©gide de lâAzerbaÃ¯djan. Le rÃ©gime dâAliyev ne se cache pas de vouloir punir la France pour son soutien Ã  lâArmÃ©nie. Et quelle est la rÃ©action de lâUnion europÃ©enne face Ã  ce pays qui agresse la France et menace de commettre un nouveau gÃ©nocide contre les ArmÃ©niens? Eh bien, la signature de nouveaux contrats gaziers Ã  Bakou par MmeÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen et les propos indignes de MmeÂ Simson, qui sâest fÃ©licitÃ©e officiellement de la dÃ©pendance europÃ©enne accrue au gaz azerbaÃ¯djanais.

Mais quelle honte! Les dirigeants actuels de lâUnion nâont dÃ©finitivement aucune dignitÃ©. Entre deux leÃ§ons de morale, ils se soumettent au pire des autocrates.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rihards Kols (ECR). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, Freedom House has warned that disinformation, globally, will be the greatest threat for freedom in the next five years and we are already seeing this unfold. Foreign actors Russia, China and Iran are weaponising information to destabilise the EU, erode trust and weaken our institutions.

AI-driven disinformation, like deepfakes, spreads false content at an alarming rate. 15 years ago, social media platforms became battlegrounds for malign actors and we missed our chance to properly regulate them. Now, disinformation spreads unchecked, threatening elections and deepening polarisation. Russian campaigns have aimed to undermine European support for Ukraine and divide us.

Europe was once mocked for the GDPR, but now others are adopting our standards. It is time for Europe to lead again, pushing for global rules in and on cyberspace, a space where conflict increasingly plays out. We must act now to counter foreign interference and protect our democracies.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Reinier Van Lanschot (Verts/ALE). â Madam President, dear colleagues, a room with thousands of smartphones, from the floor to the ceiling, rows upon rows. This is what a bot farm looks like. Where fake content is created to influence the way we think. Scary, right?

But in reality, the effect is often overestimated. People are smart and not that easily influenced. The bigger threat comes from leaders in our own society. Bait to spread lies, as we saw in the voice of Europe and Qatargate scandals.

Uncovering such scandals is more difficult because the work of our intelligence agencies is fragmented. Countries need to share intelligence, but this is risky when they cannot trust everyone on our own team. That's why Volt aims to make sure we can trust each democracy in our Union and empower the European Intelligence and Situation Centre, because we are stronger when we are united.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Ana Miguel Pedro (PPE). â Senhora Presidente, ciberataques a infraestruturas crÃ­ticas, manipulaÃ§Ã£o da opiniÃ£o pÃºblica, instrumentalizaÃ§Ã£o de crises migratÃ³rias, campanhas de desinformaÃ§Ã£o â estamos a enfrentar uma guerra de ataques hÃ­bridos e interferÃªncia estrangeira. Esta batalha nÃ£o se trava com armamento convencional, mas sim com desinformaÃ§Ã£o e manipulaÃ§Ã£o. Durante anos, o Kremlin tem orquestrado operaÃ§Ãµes de desinformaÃ§Ã£o para promover os seus objetivos geopolÃ­ticos, desde a anexaÃ§Ã£o ilegal da Crimeia em 2014 atÃ© Ã  recente desestabilizaÃ§Ã£o da MoldÃ¡via.

Os riscos associados Ã s ameaÃ§as hÃ­bridas Ã  UniÃ£o Europeia estÃ£o a aumentar, alimentados por um contexto geopolÃ­tico volÃ¡til e pelas novas tecnologias que facilitam a disseminaÃ§Ã£o de mentiras. A instrumentalizaÃ§Ã£o de migrantes proporciona uma oportunidade para redes criminosas, especialmente no que toca ao trÃ¡fico de pessoas. Grupos de cibercrime conseguem perturbar as nossas redes internas, comprometendo a seguranÃ§a e a estabilidade.

Precisamos de enviar uma mensagem inequÃ­voca a todos que querem tentar sabotar as nossas democracias e corroer os alicerces da nossa sociedade. Temos que intensificar a cooperaÃ§Ã£o europeia em matÃ©ria de seguranÃ§a, especialmente na partilha de informaÃ§Ãµes, para identificar e neutralizar estas ameaÃ§as. Temos que construir uma sociedade mais informada, crÃ­tica e resiliente. Uma populaÃ§Ã£o capaz de distinguir entre factos e ficÃ§Ã£o Ã© muito menos suscetÃ­vel Ã  manipulaÃ§Ã£o.

Onde houver interferÃªncia, as sanÃ§Ãµes devem ser rigorosas contra aqueles que financiam e promovem estas ameaÃ§as. Enfrentamos um adversÃ¡rio que transforma a informaÃ§Ã£o em arma, que explora divisÃµes sociais e polÃ­ticas e que tenta minar a confianÃ§a dos cidadÃ£os nas instituiÃ§Ãµes e na democracia. NÃ£o podemos permitir que isto aconteÃ§a. A luta pela verdade e pela integridade das nossas sociedades Ã© uma prioridade que nÃ£o podemos adiar.

Â Â Brando Benifei (S&D). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, Ã¨ di pochi giorni fa la notizia di interferenze senza precedenti e di disinformazione da parte della Russia in occasione del referendum pro Unione europea e delle elezioni presidenziali in Moldavia: fake news e deep fake proliferano.

L'Unione europea, perÃ², ha giÃ  gli strumenti per garantire piÃ¹ integritÃ  ai nostri processi elettorali, grazie al regolamento sui servizi digitali, grazie alla legge europea sulla ciberresilienza, grazie anche alla nuova proposta di uno scudo europeo contro le ingerenze per colmare le lacune nell'affrontare minacce ibride e disinformazione.

Abbiamo gli strumenti, dobbiamo procedere perÃ² a metterli in campo. Dobbiamo essere chiari, non bastano i proclami. La nuova Commissione europea non deve avere paura e non deve ridimensionare quanto di ambizioso Ã¨ stato fatto per pressioni politiche o di alcuni Stati membri. Quanto Ã¨ stato approvato va implementato. Le leggi per difendere i principi fondamentali della nostra democrazia ci sono, vanno rafforzate. Insieme dobbiamo procedere per proteggere il nostro futuro.

Â Â Javier Zarzalejos (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±or comisario, hoy es Moldavia, maÃ±ana serÃ¡ Georgia, como antes lo fue el referÃ©ndum del Brexit o el apoyo a la intentona secesionista en CataluÃ±a.

Sin embargo, no nos equivoquemos: no estamos ante una simple utilizaciÃ³n oportunista de conflictos o situaciones puntuales que Rusia quiere explotar en su favor.

Afrontamos una estrategia permanente y, por tanto, hablamos de desinformaciÃ³n, de captura de Ã©lites, de financiaciÃ³n de organizaciones que amplifican y refuerzan la injerencia rusa, de instrumentalizaciÃ³n de la inmigraciÃ³n, tambiÃ©n, de ciberataques: todos son modalidades de un antagonismo radical que Putin ha definido como parte de la identidad de esa Rusia imperial que quiere restaurar.

Estamos ante una nueva guerra frÃ­a. Aunque tal vez hablar de guerra frÃ­a a los ucranianos no les resulte muy convincente. Estamos ante un conflicto sistemÃ¡tico y estructural, en el que Rusia, bajo el liderazgo de Putin, quiere destruir los fundamentos de nuestros sistemas democrÃ¡ticos.

En la legislatura que acaba de iniciarse este Parlamento tiene que seguir trabajando en el conjunto de medidas y estrategias para prevenir la injerencia ilegÃ­tima. Pero no se trata ya de responder a crisis, sino de afianzar y fortalecer una estrategia de respuesta permanente a la intromisiÃ³n rusa, que tiene que incluir la capacidad para atribuir con claridad los ataques, las sanciones a sus responsables, la investigaciÃ³n a fondo de las organizaciones que actÃºan como instrumento de influencia rusa y la financiaciÃ³n que MoscÃº provee a estas organizaciones.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Mathilde AndrouÃ«t (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, les ingÃ©rences Ã©trangÃ¨res en Europe proviennent en partie dâacteurs dÃ©jÃ  implantÃ©s sur notre sol, soutenus par nos institutions et agissant le plus souvent Ã  notre demande. Oui, le loup est dans la bergerie. Citons lâentrisme islamiste des FrÃ¨res musulmans et de leur satellite, le Femyso, rÃ©guliÃ¨rement accueilli par la Commission europÃ©enne. Citons aussi les Ãtats-Unis, qui, Ã  travers lâextraterritorialitÃ© de leur droit, leur domination numÃ©rique et leur omniprÃ©sence politique, maintiennent lâEurope sous leur coupe. Bien quâils aient Ã©coutÃ© nos dirigeants europÃ©ens, ils ont encore des contrats de prestations sur nos domaines stratÃ©giques.Â Jamais de sanctions ni de prÃ©caution.

Le pantouflage dâanciens hauts responsables europÃ©ens dans leurs structures est une menace. Exemple rÃ©cent, lâex-directeur de la DGSI franÃ§aise PatrickÂ Calvar vend dÃ©sormais ses services Ã  une sociÃ©tÃ© amÃ©ricaine spÃ©cialisÃ©e dans lâapplication des sanctions Ã©conomiques infligÃ©es par les Ãtats-Unis. Cet homme a eu la vie dâespions franÃ§ais entre ses mains et il part travailler pour les Ãtats-Unis contre nous. Deux ans aprÃ¨s le Â«QatargateÂ», qui a sÃ©vi dans ce Parlement, aucune leÃ§on nâa Ã©tÃ© tirÃ©e.

Je vous propose de remettre au goÃ»t du jour une valeur que vous trouvez dÃ©suÃ¨te sinon dÃ©testable: le patriotisme. Quand on aime son pays, on ne le trahit pas pour un plat de lentilles.

ÐÐ°ÑÐ¾Ð²Ð° Ð½ÐµÐºÐ° Ð´Ð° Ð·Ð°Ð¿Ð°Ð·Ð¸Ð¼ Ð½Ð°ÑÐ¸Ð¾Ð½Ð°Ð»Ð½Ð¸Ñ ÑÑÐ²ÐµÑÐµÐ½Ð¸ÑÐµÑ Ð² Ð¿ÑÐ°Ð²Ð¾ÑÐ¾ Ð´Ð° ÑÐµ ÑÐµÑÐ°Ð²Ð°Ñ ÑÐ¾ÑÐ¸Ð°Ð»Ð½Ð¸ÑÐµ Ð¿Ð¾Ð»Ð¸ÑÐ¸ÐºÐ¸, Ð·Ð° Ð´Ð° Ð½Ðµ ÑÑÐ·Ð´Ð°Ð²Ð°Ð¼Ðµ ÑÐ°Ð¼Ð¸ ÑÑÐµÐ´Ð° Ð·Ð° Ð¶Ð¸Ð²Ð¾Ñ Ð½Ð° ÑÐ¸Ð±ÑÐ¸Ð´Ð½Ð¸ÑÐµ Ð°ÑÐ°ÐºÐ¸ Ð½Ð° Ð½Ð°ÑÐ¸ÑÐµ Ð½ÐµÐ¿ÑÐ¸ÑÑÐµÐ»Ð¸.

Â Â Pekka Toveri (PPE). â Madam President, dear colleagues, Russia is a dictatorship that is currently too weak to threaten us by military means. Therefore, it uses hybrid warfare against us. We have seen how the number of Russian sabotage attempts, cyberattacks and information operations has increased drastically within a year. And that is a fact: not a matter of opinion about political views. Only luck has saved us from serious loss of life so far, as the case of flammable parcels in cargo planes showed last week.

We need to defend ourselves better. As the war in Ukraine drags on, these kinds of activities will continue to grow. Moreover, why wouldn't they grow? There is no attribution to Russia: as long as there is no price for the ill deeds of Russia, there is no need for the Kremlin to slow down.

Yes, we need to improve our internal security and resilience. However, we need also to start to think how to punish those countries and actors that conduct or support these attacks. Russia uses all kinds of proxies for its activities, including social media platforms and third countries. We need to put a price on those hostile activities in the form of economic sanctions and other forms of restrictions. Otherwise, the hybrid attacks will continue to grow, especially when other authoritarian countries copy these practices. We have already seen how Iran is increasingly using Russian tactics in information operations against us.Â You won't win wars just by defending â not even hybrid wars.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Instrumentalizuota nelegali migracija, bandymai paveikti kritines informaciniÅ³ technologijÅ³ sistemas, dezinformacijos skleidimas ir kita â ir tai ne baigtinis sÄraÅ¡as grÄsmiÅ³, su kuriomis susiduria Europos SÄjungos Å¡alys. ValstybiÅ³ nariÅ³ institucijÅ³ pasirengimas tas grÄsmes identifikuoti bei jas suvaldyti tampa kritiÅ¡kai svarbus. TaÄiau riba yra labai plona, ir tai neturi tapti pretekstu atÅ¡aukimo arba vadinamai cancel kultÅ«rai, kuomet tiesiog kitos nuomonÄs turÄjimas tampa pakankamu pretekstu nutildyti oponentÄ, nes tai neprisideda prie pilietinÄs visuomenÄs ugdymo.

Â Â Salvatore De Meo (PPE). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, negli ultimi anni abbiamo assistito a tentativi sempre piÃ¹ audaci di influenzare le nostre democrazie e minacciare la nostra sicurezza attraverso la disinformazione e gli attacchi ibridi.

Questo Parlamento si Ã¨ giÃ  espresso con una forte preoccupazione per i tentativi di ingerenza da parte di soggetti esterni, tentativi ritenuti pericolosi tanto quanto gli attacchi bellici tradizionali, a tal punto da ipotizzare anche l'utilizzo della clausola di assistenza reciproca e del principio di solidarietÃ  ai sensi dei trattati. La stessa cosa ha fatto la NATO, richiamando il famoso articolo cinque del trattato.

Ã evidente che questi fenomeni minano la fiducia dei cittadini nelle istituzioni e mettono a rischio la stabilitÃ  dei nostri Stati. Ecco perchÃ© sono necessari investimenti in tecnologie di sicurezza informatica, cosÃ¬ come dobbiamo favorire la piÃ¹ ampia e diffusa alfabetizzazione mediatica dei nostri cittadini, perchÃ© siano in grado di distinguere informazioni false da quelle veritiere.

Dobbiamo lavorare per una piÃ¹ stretta collaborazione con gli Stati membri per condividere informazioni e risorse ed avere un approccio unito e coordinato nella lotta contro le minacce esterne, perchÃ© soltanto una societÃ  informata puÃ² diventare ovviamente la miglior risposta contro qualsiasi tipo di tentativo di divisione.

Credo sia indispensabile promuovere anche una cooperazione rafforzata con le organizzazioni internazionali come la NATO, che sta giÃ  necessariamente adattando la propria capacitÃ  di preparazione e deterrenza e difesa per fronteggiare i sempre piÃ¹ numerosi attacchi alle infrastrutture critiche e alle istituzioni democratiche dei Paesi alleati.

Â Â Juan Fernando LÃ³pez Aguilar (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±or comisario, cuando estamos hablando de ataques hÃ­bridos a la seguridad de la UniÃ³n Europea, bajo esa referencia crÃ­ptica estamos hablando de Rusia: de sus campaÃ±as de desinformaciÃ³n, de intoxicaciÃ³n, de sabotaje, de injerencia en procesos electorales, ademÃ¡s de financiaciÃ³n de discursos, formaciones y candidaturas y plataformas antieuropeas y eurÃ³fobas, o inspirÃ¡ndolas; por ejemplo, lamentablemente hemos escuchado al primer ministro hÃºngaro, Viktor OrbÃ¡n, que ejerce la Presidencia rotatoria, hablar de la UniÃ³n Europea como una nueva UniÃ³n SoviÃ©tica, convocando contra el Estado de Derecho europeo un movimiento como el del aÃ±o 1956 âun autÃ©ntico disparateâ.

Pues bien, frente a ello tenemos instrumentos: el Reglamento de Servicios Digitales, el Plan Director de la UE de Infraestructuras CrÃ­ticas. Pero la prÃ³xima ComisiÃ³n tiene el trabajo de ordenar todo ese instrumental y financiarlo adecuadamente, para hacer a la UniÃ³n Europea mÃ¡s fuerte frente a las amenazas contra su seguridad interior interpuestas por quien desprecia y pisotea los valores europeos: Rusia.

Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, I want, first of all, to thank you very much for today's debate. I'm pleased to agree with many of you â maybe not all, but with many of you â on the need to protect our democracy and our values from meddling outside forces, in particular, as was repeated by many of you, from Russia.

The European Parliament has been very active on all these challenges, also via the work of the Special Committee on foreign interference in all democratic processes in the EU, including disinformation. I thank you again for all your input on this topic.

'Let us keep working together' was said by many of you on all these trends, taking into account all the information collected during the last European elections â and, as I have said, we will publish next year a report about all the information collected during the elections â but also during all democratic processes, like the referendum and the election now in Moldova, because we have seen, also there, real foreign influence.

I said during my introductory remarks that we need to implement and to enforce the new tools that we have, like the Digital Services Act. It may be possible to work on other ideas, but first of all we have to work on the possible real enforcement of the tools that we have put into place, like the DSA.

I'm hoping that you will work on this in the near future.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â 

16. Proposals for Union acts
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â Prima di procedere al prossimo punto, desidero annunciare che, a norma dell'articolo 47, paragrafo due, del regolamento del Parlamento europeo, la Presidenza ha dichiarato ammissibili tre proposte di atto dell'Unione:

una relativa alla classificazione dell'attivitÃ  del personale militare, degli agenti di polizia, degli agenti penitenziarie e delle guardie di sicurezza private come professioni pericolose nell'Unione, deferita alla commissione per l'occupazione e gli affari sociali;

una relativa alla necessitÃ  di modificare la legislazione dell'Unione per proteggere le famiglie, le imprese e i lavoratori autonomi dall'aumento dei prezzi del carburante in Europa, deferita alla commissione per i problemi economici e monetari, competente per il merito, e alla commissione per l'industria, la ricerca e l'energia, autorizzata a formulare un parere;

una relativa alla necessitÃ  di ridurre i costi di accesso all'alloggio, deferita alla commissione per i problemi economici e monetari, competente per il merito, e alla commissione per l'occupazione e gli affari sociali, autorizzata a formulare un parere.

Â 

17. EU actions against the Russian shadow fleets and ensuring a full enforcement of sanctions against Russia (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â L'ordine del giorno reca la discussione sulla dichiarazione della Commissione sul tema "Interventi dell'Unione europea contro le flotte ombra russe a garanzia della piena applicazione delle sanzioni contro la Russia" (2024/2885(RSP))

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, thank you for the opportunity to take part in this debate on the Russian shadow fleet.

Weakening Russia's ability to pursue its illegal war of aggression against Ukraine is an absolute priority for the European Union. Our sanctions have sought to cut Russia's sources of income, notably in the energy sector. The impact of the oil import ban on Russia is significant, as it covers 90Â % of EU oil imports from Russia. Losing this major, lucrative market has had a significant effect on Russia, whose budgets rely substantially on oil revenues. The price caps on crude oil and refined petroleum products agreed with the G7+ Price Cap Coalition has further reduced the revenues Russia earns from all.

Had the EU not taken decisive measures to wean itself off Russian oil, Russia would be in a significantly better financial position to fuel its war machine. As a result of sanctions, Russian tax revenue from oil was down 30Â % in 2023. We have been successful in striking a balance between our own needs and the imperative to cripple Russia's war machine.

I must also acknowledge that there are still issues for the European Union. There is an understandable concern over the heightened risk and exposure for the EU Member States within the direct vicinity of Russia, given the trading routes passing along their coastlines. But this concern is not new, and it is also a global one. A serious incident or accident can occur anywhere. In fact, we are already seeing incidents in the Baltic Sea, but also further away from Europe. For example, illegal oil discharges or collisions because vessels turn off their navigation Irrigation tracking systems.

For these reasons, it is important to understand whether these vessels are insured and, if they claim to be insured, to understand what kind of insurance providers are behind this trade and whether the coverage is worth the paper it is written on in the case of a spill. We have strong doubts about the financial solidity of the insurers that are currently insuring the shadow fleet. We are also aware that these insurers rely on sanctions exclusion clauses, which they can leverage to refuse to compensate for damages they are responsible for.Â We face the risk that the cost of possible oil spills and other environmentally significant incidents would not be covered, and would place an unfair burden on states.

Confronted with this reality, the European Union has taken meaningful steps to tackle the dangers created by Russia's shadow fleet, while ensuring that the oil price cap continues impacting Russian revenues. Firstly, to address the maritime safety and environmental risks, the Commission is working closely with its Member States for concerted action at the International Maritime Organization and at EU level.

The EU supported the resolution adopted by the General Assembly of the International Maritime Organization in DecemberÂ 2023, which addressed the illegal practices of shadow fleet vessels. Additionally, at the European Political Community summit in JulyÂ 2024, the European Union and its Member States signed up to a call to action to collaborate on addressing the use of illegitimate vessels, which present serious security and environmental risk to European waterways.

The European Union also fully supports the current work of the International Oil Pollution Compensation Funds to address the issue of uninsured and unsafe ships. At EU level, the Commission is working with the Member States to strengthen and promote strict enforcement of EU and international maritime safety obligations against shadow fleet vessels. The Commission and the Member States can also count on the instrumental support of the European Maritime Safety Agency.

Secondly, in our efforts to tackle the shadow fleet, we are speaking with key interlocutors: flag states. Flag states have the primary responsibility to ensure that their ships are fit to sail and comply with safety and pollution prevention requirements. These are obligations to which flag states have subscribed at the International Maritime Organization.

We instructed EU delegations to reach out to those countries whose flag registers are hosting and providing services to the shadow fleet. Flag states must be held accountable, and these ships must no longer escape international oversight and regulation. We are also speaking with countries that may be using the services of shadow fleet vessels. For instance, EU sanctions envoy David OâSullivan is visiting India this week, and this topic will be on the agenda.

Finally, the European Union has also leveraged its sanctions powers to contain the shadow fleet. We do not want to see these vessels enter our ports. For this reason, in the 11th package of sanctions, the EU prohibited port access to vessels engaging in dangerous shipping practices while transporting Russian oil. In the 12th package, the EU reinforced the compliance regime for the oil price cap, asking for more detailed information from those engaging in Russian oil trade to ensure better enforcement and further enable Member States' action.

We have also worked hard with our G7+ partners to provide support to industry to better implement this policy, in particular through common guidance and regular contacts to gather their feedback on the challenges they are facing in complying with the oil price cap.

We are also aware that Russia is sourcing these ageing tankers from ship owners willing to sell them for such a problematic trade. To make more transparent the trade of second hand tankers, the European Union therefore introduced in its 12th package a notification rule for the sale of a tanker to any third country. This allows the Commission to closely monitor the tankers which may be added to the shadow fleet.

With the adoption of the 14th package of sanctions on Russia in June 2024, the European Union has a new legal tool to designate specific vessels whose activities support Russia's war against Ukraine. As a result, the European Union can now target tankers transporting Russian oil that pose real and high risk of incidents because they carry out dangerous operations, such as ship-to-ship transfers or turning off again their tracking systems.

Once listed, the vessels are prohibited from accessing Member States' ports and locks and from a broad range of services related to maritime transport. So far, the European Union has placed 27 vessels on this list, including 17 tankers transporting Russian oil. Most of these operate close to Member States' coastlines. The European Union has also listed entities involved in the Russian shipping sector, in particular operating tankers supporting the export of its oil. By going after these ships, especially those operating close to Member States' coastlines, the European Union is putting the spotlight on specific actors at the service of Russia and calling out their behaviour.

Like-minded countries have been adopting similar measures, and we see that our efforts are having a sizeable impact on the shadow fleet's activities. These ships have had their names, flag and ownership change in an attempt to obscure their identities. Nonetheless, as a result of sanctions, the ships become tainted for the other market players and cannot continue operating as usual. This also increases the cost of Russia to operate such a fleet.

At the same time, the designations have not negatively affected oil prices. Vessel designations are certainly something to be pursued further in the next sanctions package. The Commission is in active contact with Member States, international partners and the private sector to identify further targets, so we will continue to increase all the possible measures at EU level with the Member States, but also with international partners to fight against such a practice.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Thijs Reuten, namens de S&D-Fractie. â Voorzitter, commissaris, de afgelopen maanden voer olietanker Reus ongehinderd langs de Europese kusten, met aan boord zevenhonderdduizend vaten olie. Een oude verroeste tanker, helemaal vol met Russische olie. Het risico op een enorme milieuramp is groot, terwijl Poetins olie blijft vloeien, die zijn agressieoorlog financiert.

Ondanks alles wat er wÃ©l is gebeurd, ontbreekt de urgentie om Rusland echt te verlammen. Waar blijven de maatregelen om deze schepen van zee te halen, als ze onze havens aandoen voor bevoorrading en gevaar veroorzaken? Poetin bouwde zijn schaduwvloot al op vÃ³Ã³r de sancties en het olieprijsplafond, en inmiddels varen er honderden tankers. Sommige lidstaten leggen schepen aan de ketting voor een kleinigheid. Waarom kan dat niet bij de varende rampen van Poetin?

Update lijsten elke dag met fotoâs. Handhaaf op uitgeschakelde signalering, op overschilderen en op overpompen. Laat ze maar klagen als we ze van zee halen. We moeten Poetin te slim af zijn, onze zeeÃ«n beschermen en alles doen om OekraÃ¯ne te steunen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AndrÃ¡s LÃ¡szlÃ³, on behalf of the PfE Group. â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, do you remember what Ursula von der Leyen promised in FebruaryÂ 2022? She announced sanctions which would cripple Putin's war machine. Two and a half years and 14 sanctions packages later, here we are discussing this humiliating failure.

Picking energy as a battlefield against a global energy giant was arrogant and foolish. Ukrainians are paying the ultimate price because of this. The Russian shadow fleet of oil tankers is more than a sanctions evasion issue. It's also worth looking at why sanctions in general ain't working. Because beyond Western countries, no country in the world joined our sanctions. None. They rather trade with Russia.

But let's not the point the finger at other countries. Let's look at European countries. EU countries imported EURÂ 8.5Â billion worth of seaborne Russian oil through intermediaries in India and TÃ¼rkiye. European countries have boosted trade towards Central Asian countries. Import and export data make it obvious that these products' destination is Russia. Western components were also found in Russian missiles that hit Ukraine.

Despite the obvious failed sanctions of Ursula von der Leyen, she was re-elected by the EPP and its leftist allies. Do you really expect change when you vote for more of the same? Ukraine deserves our help. We need to help them bring a negotiated end to this war. This is also what the majority of European citizens want.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Reinis PozÅaks, ECR grupas vÄrdÄ. â CienÄ«tÄ sÄdes vadÄ«tÄja! Labdien, kolÄÄ£i! KomisÄra kungs! Un mÄs esam nonÄkuÅ¡i situÄcijÄ, kur pa jÅ«rÄm klÄ«st simtiem vecu, sarÅ«sÄjuÅ¡u kuÄ£u, kuri ne tikai baro Å¡o te Krievijas kara maÅ¡Ä«nu ar miljardiem dolÄru ik dienas, bet tÄs bÅ«tÄ«bÄ ir arÄ« bumbas ar laika degli, kas agrÄk vai vÄlÄk novedÄ«s arÄ« pie ekoloÄ£iskas katastrofas.

TÄ saucamÄ Änu flote - un pats jocÄ«gÄkais visÄ Å¡ajÄ ir tas, ka mÄs Å¡os kuÄ£us redzam, â mÄs zinÄm, ko viÅi dara. Tie kÄdam pieder, kÄds viÅus fraktÄ, kÄds par to maksÄ. TaÄu izrÄdÄs, ka mÄs ar to neko nespÄjam izdarÄ«t vai arÄ« varbÅ«t negribam pietiekami labi. Pie Eiropas Parlamenta ir brÄ«niÅ¡Ä·Ä«gi uzraksti "DemokrÄtija darbÄ«bÄ", bet tas, kas mums ir jÄsaprot, ka demokrÄtijÄm ir arÄ« jÄveic aktÄ«vas darbÄ«bas, lai tÄs turpinÄtu pastÄvÄt un nosargÄtu savas vÄrtÄ«bas.

DabÄ, kÄ mÄs zinÄm, izdzÄ«vo tas, kurÅ¡ pielÄgojas labÄk. Un diemÅ¾Äl, sporta terminoloÄ£ijÄ runÄjot, Å¡obrÄ«d Å¡is rezultÄts ir 0:1 agresora un diktatora labÄ. Tagad bumba ir mÅ«su pusÄ. Ja mÄs savu gÄjienu nedarÄ«sim, tad mÄs Å¡o spÄli varam arÄ« zaudÄt. Un, iespÄjams, arÄ« saprotu, ka tÄ nav tikai Eiropas SavienÄ«bas problÄma â tÄ tieÅ¡Äm ir visas Rietumu demokrÄtijas problÄma un izaicinÄjums. TÄpÄc es aicinu visu Å¡o Rietumu demokrÄtiju nÄkt kopÄ un darÄ«t kaut ko pa Ä«stam, lai mÄs savas vÄrtÄ«bas nosargÄtu.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Madam President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, a fleet of over 600 old, uninsured oil tankers are sailing through our European waters carrying sanctioned Russian oil.

This so-called shadow fleet transports more than EURÂ 80 billionâworth of crude oil, putting money illegally into Russia's war coffers â reason enough to do everything to stop this.

On top of that, the tankers pose an inconceivable environmental hazard. It's not a question of 'if' but 'when' disaster will strike. Here in France, many will recall the biggest oil spill ever, the Amoco Cadiz in Brittany in 1978. It was also the biggest loss of marine life ever. It took decades to recover from that. Again, we should do everything to avoid another disaster.

To make it even worse, these ships are not insured, and it's European taxpayers who, in the end, will bear the costs when disaster eventually strikes. We do not allow a single passenger car to drive around uninsured, but we do allow these potential environmental disasters to pass through our waters.

I call on the Commission and the Member States to enforce our European sanctions and protect our seas and shores.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Francisco JosÃ© MillÃ¡n Mon (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, en junio de 2022 prohibimos la importaciÃ³n a la UniÃ³n Europea de petrÃ³leo ruso por vÃ­a marÃ­tima y, tambiÃ©n, el transporte por esa vÃ­a de petrÃ³leo ruso a terceros paÃ­ses por un precio superior al fijado por el G7, pero, en las Ãºltimas semanas, la prensa ha denunciado ampliamente que mÃ¡s del 70Â % del petrÃ³leo ruso transportado por vÃ­a marÃ­tima utiliza la llamada Â«flota en la sombraÂ» rusa. El pasado septiembre, esta flota habÃ­a transportado mÃ¡s crudo que nunca. Las consecuencias son preocupantes: por un lado, se incumplen las medidas impuestas para recortar los ingresos de la economÃ­a rusa, en particular de su presupuesto, mediante las cuales se trataba de debilitar la maquinaria de guerra rusa. Por otro lado, estos barcos, viejos y mal asegurados, representan un peligro para la seguridad marÃ­tima y el medio ambiente. Ya se han denunciado incidentes, derrames procedentes de esta flota. Un incidente grave podrÃ­a causar una gran catÃ¡strofe.

Yo celebro las medidas que ha puesto en marcha la ComisiÃ³n en los Ãºltimos meses y que hoy nos ha explicado el comisario, y veo que quiere seguir reforzÃ¡ndolas. Son, desde luego, muchos los buques que todavÃ­a estÃ¡n operativos. Tenemos que vigilar y limitar en todo lo posible la actividad de esta flota. Es seguro que su cobertura, los trasbordos, los cambios de bandera, los incumplimientos de legislaciÃ³n medioambiental, etc., deben vigilarse.

Debemos mejorar tambiÃ©n la coordinaciÃ³n entre los paÃ­ses europeos, que deben reforzar sus medios para investigar los incidentes y las actividades de los buques de esta flota que transitan por sus aguas. SegÃºn la prensa internacional, ha habido este aÃ±o accidentes en aguas europeas con este tipo de buques que no se han investigado.

TambiÃ©n me parece Ãºtil presionar a los Estados que prestan su bandera a los llamados Â«buques en la sombraÂ» o dan facilidades para los sucesivos abanderamientos.

Â Â Li Andersson (The Left). â Arvoisa puhemies, meidÃ¤n tulee Euroopassa tehdÃ¤ kaikkemme, jotta sotiminen tulee Putinille mahdollisimman kalliiksi. Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Pekka Toveri (PPE). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, Russia funds its continuing illegal attack against Ukraine by selling oil and gas. This year, it is estimated that Russia exports around 240Â million metric tonnes of oil, worth about USDÂ 240Â billion, an increase of about 5Â % compared to 2023. Almost half of the Russian Government's income is based on oil and gas revenue.

In addition, a lot of this oil is exported using Russia's so-called 'shadow fleet'. Last year, around 330 of these shadow tankers â rusty single hull ships without adequate insurances â sailed through the contested and ecologically vulnerable Baltic Sea and now some of these have stopped using AIS data, further increasing the risks. This is not only a way for Russia to fund its criminal war, it's also an ecological catastrophe waiting to happen.

For these reasons, we need to take action to stop the Russian shuttle fleet as soon as possible. And we just heard from MrÂ Reynders that there is a wide range of tools for this. Let's start using them.

At the same time we see that the EU sanctions don't slow down Russia's capability to continue the war fast enough. One big reason being that Russia is able to bypass the sanctions through third countries. Moreover, our own companies often help this to happen. Making money is more important to some than preventing dictatorship killing innocent people. Many companies try to escape their responsibility with lame explanations about not knowing where their products products end up. The EU needs to find ways to prevent these activities by taking third countries under sanctions, and by working with Member States in finding out ways to stop businesses to support Russia.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â SÃ©rgio GonÃ§alves (S&D). â Senhora Presidente, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio, o uso pela RÃºssia de navios obsoletos e sem condiÃ§Ãµes de seguranÃ§a, conhecidos como a shadow fleet russa, apresenta dois grandes problemas. Primeiro, permite que a RÃºssia contorne as sanÃ§Ãµes, contrariando o nosso objetivo de apoiar a UcrÃ¢nia e de garantir que a RÃºssia sofra consequÃªncias pela sua invasÃ£o ilegal. Segundo, estes navios nÃ£o seguem os padrÃµes internacionais, nem tÃªm seguro, representando por isso um risco para o meio ambiente, para a seguranÃ§a dos outros navios e para a vida de todos os que neles navegam.

Os dados que temos indicam que estes navios circulam na zona marÃ­tima europeia e inclusive jÃ¡ entraram, atÃ©, em Ã¡guas portuguesas. Ã imperativo que as autoridades europeias e nacionais atuem para combater esta ameaÃ§a. Temos de rever os mecanismos para implementar sanÃ§Ãµes mais eficazes, reforÃ§ar a fiscalizaÃ§Ã£o de navios suspeitos, evitar possÃ­veis desastres marÃ­timos e ambientais e, caso ocorram, assegurar uma resposta rÃ¡pida com meios adequados.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Arkadiusz Mularczyk (ECR). â Pani PrzewodniczÄca! Panie Komisarzu! W tym miejscu chcÄ przypomnieÄ, Å¼e Polska zainwestowaÅa duÅ¼e Årodki budÅ¼etowe w infrastrukturÄ morskÄ, aby zmniejszyÄ rosyjskie wpÅywy na BaÅtyku, tak aby polskie jednostki nie musiaÅy prosiÄ o zgodÄ Federacji Rosyjskiej, by mÃ³c wpÅynÄÄ na polskie wody terytorialne. ZrobiliÅmy to pomimo zastrzeÅ¼eÅ Komisji Europejskiej co do tej inwestycji. RzÄd Prawa i SprawiedliwoÅci, realizujÄc przekop Mierzei WiÅlanej, zapewniÅ Polsce suwerennoÅÄ na BaÅtyku.

TakÅ¼e teraz, zwÅaszcza w kontekÅcie przystÄpienia Finlandii i Szwecji do NATO, wspÃ³Åpraca miÄdzynarodowa i unijna solidarnoÅÄ sÄ niezbÄdne, by skutecznie egzekwowaÄ sankcje i osÅabiÄ rosyjskÄ machinÄ wojennÄ. Dlatego Unia Europejska i NATO powinny podjÄÄ wszelkie moÅ¼liwe Årodki w odniesieniu do obszarÃ³w BaÅtyku naleÅ¼Äcych do paÅstw czÅonkowskich Unii Europejskiej, aby wyegzekwowaÄ unijne sankcje poprzez kontrole morskie i koordynacjÄ miÄdzynarodowych dziaÅaÅ.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ivars Ijabs (Renew). â Madam President, MrÂ Reynders, of course the activities of the shadow fleet is really a serious loophole in our sanctions policy.

Let me remind you that the cap on Russian oil was introduced in September 2022. Now, more than two years later, it turns out that this cap is easily circumvented by the shadow fleet, which has been going on for all those two years.

So what should we do right now? First of all, what are the countries that sell those uninsured ships to Russia? I think that Europeans deserve to know that, especially if we are dealing with a Member State.

Secondly, we should use all the arsenal of international maritime law to prevent those ships from moving through our territorial waters. Especially, I'm talking about the Baltic Sea, which is particularly vulnerable to possible ecological disaster. Many of those steps have already been taken by the UK and the US. I think that Europe should follow suit as soon as possible.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Og det burde alle kunne bakke op om. SÃ¥ kÃ¦re kolleger, kÃ¦re Kommission og kÃ¦re medlemslande, beskyt vores miljÃ¸, beskyt Ukraines civile, skrid ind mod skyggeflÃ¥den. Â Â Mika Aaltola (PPE). â Arvoisa puhemies, VenÃ¤jÃ¤ on siirtÃ¤nyt hiilivetyjensÃ¤ kuljetuksia vakuuttamattomiin ja usein huonokuntoisiin laivoihin. Varjolaivasto onkin seilaava ekologinen aikapommi herkÃ¤ssÃ¤ ympÃ¤ristÃ¶ssÃ¤.

KansainvÃ¤linen oikeus mahdollistaa laivojen merikelpoisuuden tarkastamisen kansainvÃ¤lisellÃ¤ merialueella. TÃ¤hÃ¤n tulisi ryhtyÃ¤ viipymÃ¤ttÃ¤ ja tulisi asettaa myÃ¶s selkeÃ¤t sanktiot satamille, jotka vastaanottavat varjolaivaston aluksia, yhtiÃ¶ille, joiden laivat vaihtavat lastia nÃ¤iden alusten kanssa merellÃ¤. EU:n tulee tehdÃ¤ lÃ¤pinÃ¤kyvÃ¤ksi VenÃ¤jÃ¤n metkut ja Ã¶ljyn virta maailmalle, jotta tiedettÃ¤isiin, kuka energiaa kÃ¤yttÃ¤Ã¤, ja vahvistettaisiin kansalaisten ja kuluttajien reaktiot sekÃ¤ tarkemmat sanktiot. Aikaa ei ole hukattavaksi.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Emma Wiesner (Renew). â Fru talman! 600 spÃ¶kskepp â flytande miljÃ¶katastrofer rakt in i Putins krigskassa. Det sÃ¤ger sig sjÃ¤lvt, det hÃ¤r Ã¤r helt oacceptabelt. Oregistrerade, ofÃ¶rsÃ¤krade och opÃ¥litliga. Vi mÃ¥ste agera, och vi mÃ¥ste agera nu. LÃ¥t mig dÃ¤rfÃ¶r vara konkret. Det har varit mycket snack och lite verkstad hÃ¤r iÂ dag.

Till att bÃ¶rja med mÃ¥ste vi stÃ¤nga av europeiskt vatten fÃ¶r de hÃ¤r fartygen. StÃ¤ng Engelska kanalen, gÃ¶r det obligatoriskt med lotsar om du ska in i ÃstersjÃ¶n.

FÃ¶r det andra Ã¤r den nuvarande Ã¶vervakningen alldeles fÃ¶r otillrÃ¤cklig. Satelliter duger inte. Vi mÃ¥ste punktmarkera de hÃ¤r fartygen med drÃ¶nare.

FÃ¶r det tredje mÃ¥ste vi banta marknaden: utbudet fÃ¶r spÃ¶kskeppen. EU kan ta pÃ¥ sig att kÃ¶pa in och skrota de hÃ¤r skuggfartygen, sÃ¥ att de inte hamnar i Rysslands hÃ¤nder.

FÃ¶r det fjÃ¤rde mÃ¥ste vi anvÃ¤nda svartlistan â se till att fylla pÃ¥ den med mycket fler fartyg.

Och fÃ¶r det femte mÃ¥ste vi ha en beredskap och bÃ¶rja koordinera oss fÃ¶r nÃ¤r miljÃ¶katastrofen Ã¤r ett faktum. Stoppa Ryssland, stoppa skuggflottan! Slava Ukraini!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â OndÅej KolÃ¡Å (PPE). â Madam President, dear colleagues, I have to be honest that I hate listening to lamentations about things being too complicated or impossible. We cannot on one hand assert electromobility and on the other overlook floating ecological time bombs which Putin sails across the seas. We repeatedly put ourselves one step behind those who endanger us and our friends by being unable to adopt solutions that may seem harsh or even radical at the moment, but which are in fact very much needed. While we hesitate to effectively prevent or even stop Russia from smuggling oil and whatever else around the world, the UK has imposed sanctions on another 22 Russian shadow tankers last week.

Let me quote a firstâranked Ukrainian Navy captain, Andryi Ryzhenko, who said that it is necessary to identify the shipping companies and vessels, impose sanctions on them that will prohibit them from insuring these tankers, accepting them in ports and paying them. Wouldn't it be nice to come up with one bold solution instead of fabricating hundreds of resolutions? It would not only help us, but also Ukraine, where Putin is continuing his killing spree, financed, among others, with the money he earns on smuggled oil.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Mandl (PPE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Das Thema der russischen Schattenflotten zeigt, dass sich Putin-Russland einfach an gar keine Regeln hÃ¤lt. Ich will das sagen heute, 28 Tage bevor es leider schon 1Â 000Â Tage sein werdenÂ â 1Â 000Â Tage, an denen Putin-Russland diesen bestialischen Krieg fÃ¼hrt.

Viele Menschen sind mÃ¼de davon zu hÃ¶ren. Ich weiÃ nicht, wie viele Debattenstunden wir im EuropÃ¤ischen Parlament schon investiert haben, um unsere Zivilisation hochzuhalten gegen diesen Putin-Russland-AngriffskriegÂ â aber er geht weiter. Putin-Russland hÃ¤lt sich nicht an VÃ¶lkerrecht, an internationales Recht, an Kriegsrecht, hat das Kriegsverbrechen des Angriffskrieges begangen und im Angriffskrieg weiter Kriegsverbrechen begangen, und es geht weiter.

Wir dÃ¼rfen nicht mÃ¼de werden, unsere Zivilisation hochzuhalten, die Freiheit hochzuhalten und die Einhaltung von Regeln einzufordern, besonders in der Woche, in der sich Brasilien, Russland, Indien, China und SÃ¼dafrika treffen. Wir mÃ¼ssen sehen, wo unsere Partner sind, und die sind auch in diesen Staaten vorhanden; mit denen mÃ¼ssen wir zusammenarbeiten.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tom Berendsen (PPE). â Voorzitter, stelt u zich eens voor: een stormachtige dag, flinke golven en dichte mist. Een oud en slecht onderhouden schip vaart over de Noordzee. Aan boord 100 miljoen liter olie. Het schip is niet of nauwelijks verzekerd en vaart onder de vlag van een land als Liberia of Sierra Leone, waar de controles op veiligheid te wensen overlaten. De bemanning is slecht opgeleid en spreekt geen Engels. Dit is geen scÃ¨ne uit een actiefilm, dit is de dagelijkse praktijk. Deze varende schroothopen zijn onderdeel van de Russische schaduwvloot, een vloot waarmee Rusland onze sancties omzeilt.

Tientallen van deze schepen varen wekelijks door Europees water. Kustwacht merken dit op, instanties maken melding, maar niemand grijpt in. Want wie is aansprakelijk? De eigenaar van het schip bevindt zich via een schimmige constructie in Dubai of op de Seychellen. De opdrachtgever is onbekend en het schip wisselt onderweg van eigenaar, vlag, lading of bestemming. De scheepvaart lijkt op dit gebied soms op het Wilde Westen en als we effectief sancties willen opleggen, moeten we ze ook effectief handhaven. Dat moet beter. Maar er is meer.

Onderzoeksjournalisten uit Nederland vonden uit dat tankers uit de schaduwvloot bij onze Nederlandse kust tanken, bunkeren. Ik vind het onacceptabel dat bedrijven hieraan meewerken. Een excuus zoals "Ik weet niet precies wie de eigenaar is", is echt onacceptabel. Bij twijfel trek je aan de bel en ga je niet voor het snelle geld over de rug van OekraÃ¯ense burgerslachtoffers. Als we de Russische oorlogskas echt pijn willen doen, moeten we allemaal onze verantwoordelijkheid nemen: politiek, instanties en de bedrijven die nu nog meewerken.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Procedura "catch-the-eye"

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). â Gerbiamas Pirmininke, gerbiamas komisare, iÅ¡ties Äia visi pabrÄÅ¾Ä labai akivaizdÅ¾iai bÅ«tinybÄ greitai ir efektyviai sukurti kovos priemoniÅ³ planÄ. Äia neabejotinai mes turime aiÅ¡kiai atsakyti sau Ä¯ klausimÄ, kaip sustiprinti Europos SÄjungos jÅ«rÅ³ agentÅ«ros veiklÄ, kaip Å¡alis nares paskatinti dirbti, kadangi Äia yra ir Å¡aliÅ³ nariÅ³ reikalas, jÅ³ uostÅ³ ir jÅ³ uostÅ³ valdymo, taip pat jÅ³ registrÅ³, taip pat jÅ³ inspekcijÅ³ ir neabejotinai privataus sektoriaus, kuris neabejotinai dalyvauja Å¡iame Å¡eÅ¡Äliniame sandoryje ir jis yra tarptarpininkaujantis. Juo labiau taip pat diplomatinÄs priemonÄs turi bÅ«ti taikomos, nes mes turime suprasti, kad Äia tas pats kaip ir su ofÅ¡orinÄmis firmomis ir pinigÅ³ plovimu. Taip ir tos treÄiosios Å¡alys, kurios parduoda savo vÄliavas ir sudaro sÄlygas, jos turi bÅ«ti taip pat bÅ«ti Ä¯trauktos Ä¯ juoduosius sÄraÅ¡us, kad bÅ«tÅ³ galima kompleksiÅ¡kai, greitai ir efektyviai iÅ¡sprÄsti Å¡iÄ labai sudÄtingÄ problemÄ. Bet, aiÅ¡ku, Å¡aliÅ³ nariÅ³ atsakomybÄ Äia yra ypatingai didelÄ.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, as I have already underlined, maybe, in my introduction, we are hard at work to sustain the impact of the oil price cap and counteract the Russian shadow fleet. I have explained the different measures taken in the different sanctions package and we try to enforce, of course, all those sanctions.

The environmental and maritime safety risks posed by the shadow fleet are enormous and this is not a topic we take lightly â I know that you are in the same position. I have listened to many comments about the risk of such a shadow fleet. By ensuring that sanctions and maritime safety policies are leveraged together, we can ensure that all policy against the shadow fleet reaches its full potential.

Of course, we need to continue to work with international partners, but maybe more than that â and it was said also by some of you â we need Member States to play their part and the Commission will continue to support them in such a way. Many thanks for giving the Commission the opportunity to take part in this important exchange. But again, we need to continue to put into place some measures to enforce those measures, but to do that, not alone, but with the real support of the Member States.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â La discussione Ã¨ chiusa e comunico che la votazione si svolgerÃ  durante la prossima tornata.

Dichiarazioni scritte (articolo 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Adam Bielan (ECR), in writing. â Despite the EU's 14th sanctions package strengthening measures against Russia, we see that Russia still has room to manoeuvre. Russian shadow fleets â clandestine operations transporting oil â are funding the very war we aim to halt. Every barrel these covert ships deliver fuels the war machinery that destroys homes, separates families and shatters dreams. This isn't just a violation of sanctions; it's a breach of trust in what we stand for and a threat to our credibility and effectiveness.

Sanctions are only as effective as their enforcement. The EU and the Member States must act decisively: enhance maritime surveillance, tighten shipping controls and impose stricter penalties on those facilitating these illicit activities.

Let us ensure our actions match our words and bring an end to this unjust war.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â MichaÅ Dworczyk (ECR), na piÅmie. â Szanowna Pani PrzewodniczÄca, KoleÅ¼anki i Koledzy, Pomimo trwajÄcej juÅ¼ trzeci rok peÅnoskalowej wojny oraz naÅoÅ¼onych na RosjÄ przez UE kolejnych czternastu juÅ¼ pakietÃ³w sankcji, ktÃ³re miaÅy m.in ograniczyÄ sprzedaÅ¼ rosyjskiej ropy, dziÄki stworzonej âflocie cieniâ, prowadzi ona wciÄÅ¼ jej eksport, skutecznie omijajÄc sankcje. Proceder ten umoÅ¼liwia rÃ³wnieÅ¼ pranie brudnych pieniÄdzy, dziaÅalnoÅÄ przestÄpczÄ, przemytniczÄ i terrorystycznÄ. WedÅug danych licznych instytutÃ³w i firm analitycznych wielkoÅÄ âflotyâ w poszczegÃ³lnych miesiÄcach oscyluje wokÃ³Å 160â200 tankowcÃ³w, ktÃ³re obsÅugujÄ ok. 78% morskiego eksportu rosyjskiej ropy. Tylko na BaÅtyku miesiÄcznie obserwowany jest ruch okoÅo 70 statkÃ³w. I choÄ ich fatalny stan techniczny oraz brak ochrony ubezpieczeniowej stanowiÄ realne zagroÅ¼enie dla Årodowiska naturalnego, to dodatkowo poprzez montaÅ¼ na nich odpowiedniego sprzÄtu wykorzystywane sÄ przez RosjÄ do dziaÅalnoÅci szpiegowskiej, zagraÅ¼ajÄc tym samym bezpieczeÅstwu paÅstw UE. Musimy, wspÃ³ÅpracujÄc z innymi paÅstwami G7, podjÄÄ zdecydowane dziaÅania wobec podmiotÃ³w zaangaÅ¼owanych w ten proceder, aby powstrzymaÄ rosyjskÄ dziaÅalnoÅÄ. Potrzebne jest zatem: realne wyeliminowanie sprzedaÅ¼y przez podmioty z UE zuÅ¼ytych tankowcÃ³w firmom rosyjskim i anonimowym; ÅcisÅe monitorowanie realnych cen sprzedaÅ¼y rosyjskiej ropy oraz reagowanie w przypadku naruszeÅ puÅapu cenowego UE; wzmocnienie wspÃ³Åpracy w dziedzinie zapobiegania i zwalczania zanieczyszczeÅ morza ropÄ i chemikaliami oraz kontrolÄ posiadanego przez statki ubezpieczenia.

Â 

18. Need to strengthen rail travel and the railway sector in Europe (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â L'ordine del giorno reca in discussione la dichiarazione della Commissione sul tema "NecessitÃ  di rafforzare il trasporto ferroviario e il settore delle ferrovie in Europa" (2024/2896(RSP))

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, on behalf of my colleague, Commissioner Hoekstra, I would like to thank Parliament for inviting the Commission to speak today in plenary about the importance of the European rail sector.

Let me start by reminding you of the key role that railways play within the transport system and our economy in general, the crucial role demonstrated during recent times of crisis. I am thinking about Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine and the contribution of railways in providing a lifeline for Ukrainian exports and imports within the solidarity lanes. I'm thinking further back in time, when rail kept our economies alive during the pandemic, moving the goods we all needed and helping to keep the supply chains functioning.

Rail also has the potential to become the backbone of a sustainable European transport system. Its virtues are much needed every day to meet our policy goals and address some of the most pressing problems in society: greenhouse gas emissions; overall energy consumption; air pollution; congestion; access to transport services; and connectivity.

Under the Commission's mandate that is now coming to an end, a number of important steps have been taken to strengthen rail. Let me highlight some of them in particular.

First of all, we have continued our work to fully and correctly implement the single European railway area, making sure we are on a good track towards a single open market with clear rules, clear roles and, to the extent possible, harmonised standards.

What we are witnessing today is that, in general, market opening and competition in the sector bring positive effects, passengers have more choice, ticket prices go down and the quality of rail services goes up. Customers are welcoming this positive improvement. In several countries, passenger numbers have fully recovered after the COVID pandemic. We can observe that when rail passenger services are attractive, customers are moving away from less sustainable modes in very significant numbers.

We have also been making progress on the infrastructure side. This is critical to having a competitive and attractive railway system. For example, in July this year, the revised TenâT Regulation entered into effect. It mandates enhanced railway infrastructure including, by 2040, that passenger lines will have to allow trains to travel at a minimum 160Â km/h on main lines. This will make it possible to connect Prague and Warsaw in three hours instead of the eight currently, or Copenhagen and Berlin in four hours, instead of seven.

When it comes to rail safety and interoperability, we intend to accelerate the full role of all state of the art European common signalling and speed control systems across the entire TenâT network.

In addition, the new regulation requires the major airports of the network to be connected to longâdistance rail. Urban nodes will need to better integrate railway stations with other modes such as bus stations. This will make rail services more attractive to citizens and promote multimodal travel.

We also don't forget about freight. In order to be competitive, we need long trains and lines ready to carry intermodal containers. Our regulation tackles this with specific requirements, such as rail lines that can accommodate 740âmetreâlong trains and other relevant technical standards. Together with the complete electrification of the network and the expansion of the capacity of terminals, this should make it more attractive to transport goods by rail.

Another important step is the Commission proposal to modernise the allocation process of rail network capacity. For this file, the interinstitutional negotiations should start soon, so the Commission counts on all of you to ensure its good progress.

To enable the growth of rail, we must change current capacity allocation practice, which does not allow us to get the most out of the existing infrastructure. Before spending billions on additional tracks, let us make sure we optimise the use of the existing ones.

Let me also address funding. The Connecting Europe Facility has substantially supported railway infrastructure. It focuses on cross-border bottlenecks and missing links. This allows the construction of new lines and the upgrading of existing lines, as well as the the deployment of interoperability and rail traffic management.Â It also ensures rail connectivity between major European cities.

Since 2014, this programme has supported more than 670 rail projects, amounting to roughly EURÂ 26.8 billion out of a total of EURÂ 49.86 billion. This corresponds to more than 54Â % of the financing programme. Out of these, 63 projects address highâspeed infrastructure, for an overall EU contribution of EURÂ 5.4 billion.

However, despite the contribution that the CEF has brought to all these achievements, the needs of investment remain only partially covered. This is an important challenge where further discussions will be required.

Let me conclude: connecting the EU by fast, comfortable and reliable rail services is key to achieving the ambitious goals of the Commission's sustainable and smart mobility strategy.

It is an expectation of millions of citizens and companies who are looking for more sustainable ways to travel and make the most of European integration.

So we will continue to work in such a way. I thank you for the opportunity to explain it to you, and I look forward now to your comments.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PRESIDE: JAVI LÃPEZ
Vicepresidente

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dariusz JoÅski, w imieniu grupy PPE. â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Politycy partii KaczyÅskiego wpadli na taki pomysÅ, aby tutaj odegraÄ przed nami w Parlamencie pewien spektakl. Tutaj dzisiaj na widowni jest prezes tej spÃ³Åki, ktÃ³ry chce naprawiÄ tÄ spÃ³ÅkÄ a winni, ktÃ³rzy doprowadzili do tego zniszczenia polskiej dumy, muszÄ za to odpowiedzieÄ.

Â Â FranÃ§ois Kalfon, au nom du groupe S&D. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, le fret ferroviaire est lâoutil principal de dÃ©carbonation du transport de marchandises. Il connaÃ®t pourtant une crise profonde. En France, il y a plus dâun demi-siÃ¨cle, 60Â % des marchandises Ã©taient transportÃ©es par rail, contre 10Â % seulement aujourdâhui.

Ce rÃ©sultat, câest aussi la Commission europÃ©enne qui en porte la responsabilitÃ©. Face aux difficultÃ©s rencontrÃ©es par FretÂ SNCF, la France projetait dâÃ©ponger les dettes de lâentreprise. La Commission europÃ©enne lâen a empÃªchÃ©e. Elle a lancÃ© une enquÃªte Ã  son encontre pour des aides prÃ©tendument illÃ©gales, et cela a abouti au dÃ©mantÃ¨lement de FretÂ SNCF en deux entitÃ©s distinctes, Ã  la suppression deÂ 500Â emplois et Ã  lâobligation pour lâentreprise de cÃ©der 20Â % de ses activitÃ©s Ã  des compagnies privÃ©es.

Pendant ce temps-lÃ , que fait la Commission? Elle introduit des mÃ©gaâcamions sur nos routes et abaisse lâÃ¢ge requis pour les conduire, accroissant les risques dâaccident. Pour rÃ©aliser le pacte vert, nous avons soutenu financiÃ¨rement lâachat des vÃ©hicules Ã©lectriques. Câest une bonne chose. Pourquoi, dÃ¨s lors, la Commission ne fait-elle pas de mÃªme pour le fret ferroviaire?

Il faut plus de financements europÃ©ens. Laissons les Ãtats membres soutenir ces entreprises qui effectuent une mission de service public. Le transport routier, polluant, accidentogÃ¨ne, doit Ã  prÃ©sent payer sa part pour permettre le dÃ©veloppement du fret ferroviaire, lequel ne pourra parvenir Ã  lâÃ©quilibre Ã©conomique sans soutien public.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Margarita de la Pisa CarriÃ³n, en nombre del Grupo PfE. â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, seÃ±orÃ­as, reforzar el sector ferroviario en Europa. En EspaÃ±a no llegamos ni a que funcione; la gestiÃ³n actual del Gobierno es desastrosa. Renfe, nuestra principal operadora, ha dejado atrÃ¡s su prestigio y ahora es sinÃ³nimo de caos e ineficiencia e, incluso, de peligro para las personas.

La semana pasada se descarrilÃ³ un tren en Madrid y nadie se ha hecho responsable. Hay servicios cancelados sin explicaciÃ³n, retrasos crÃ³nicos, falta de mantenimiento, no hablemos de inversiÃ³n... Esto deja a miles de ciudadanos varados: les priva de un servicio que es bÃ¡sico en su vida diaria y clave para la cohesiÃ³n territorial.

Los fondos europeos destinados al transporte ferroviario estÃ¡n siendo mal gestionados. Los ciudadanos ven cÃ³mo se tira el dinero de sus impuestos. Tanto hablar de Pacto Verde, de las emisiones, de restringir la entrada de coches a las ciudades, y medios de transporte como este âque muchos de ustedes considerarÃ­an limpioâ se dejan abandonados. Es una tremenda incoherencia.

Muestra cÃ³mo a nivel polÃ­tico ha habido mÃ¡s preocupaciÃ³n por empujar una ideologÃ­a que por conservar, construir, lo que debe ser patrimonio de todos. Es imprescindible que se tomen medidas urgentes para restaurar la eficacia y la fiabilidad del sector ferroviario.

Necesitamos un compromiso claro para gestionar adecuadamente los recursos y garantizar un servicio que cumpla con las expectativas de los ciudadanos de estar interconectados, tanto a nivel nacional como entre las principales ciudades europeas.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marlena MalÄg, w imieniu grupy ECR. â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! SÅaba koniunktura oraz pogarszajÄce siÄ wyniki finansowe wielu spÃ³Åek paÅstwowych w branÅ¼y kolejowych przewozÃ³w towarowych wymagajÄ gÅÄbokiej analizy, dokapitalizowania jako wsparcia inwestycyjnego. Niestety, przywoÅana przez pana posÅa spÃ³Åka PKP Cargo w restrukturyzacji z Polski idzie na ÅatwiznÄ, zwalnia masowo pracownikÃ³w, a nowy zarzÄd komentuje to w ten sposÃ³b, Å¼e uwalnia pracownikÃ³w na rynek pracy.

Jak informujÄ zwiÄzki zawodowe, na bruk wyrzucane sÄ kobiety w ciÄÅ¼y, osoby w wieku przedemerytalnym, a takÅ¼e dziaÅacze zwiÄzkowi. Na to nie moÅ¼e byÄ zgody. ZÅamane sÄ takÅ¼e zasady dialogu spoÅecznego. Dlatego pokrzepiajÄce jest to, Å¼e w czÄÅci dyskryminowani pracownicy znajdujÄ sprawiedliwoÅÄ w sÄdach, w ktÃ³rych zapadajÄ korzystne dla nich wyniki i wyroki.

Nie ma i nie moÅ¼e byÄ zgody na takie dziaÅanie. JesteÅmy solidarni z branÅ¼Ä kolejowych przewozÃ³w towarowych.

MÃ¡ tÃ¡imid dÃ¡irÃ­re faoin gcÃ³ras iarnrÃ³id a chur chun cinn, nÃ­ mÃ³r dÃºinn a bheith dÃ¡irÃ­re faoi airgead a chaitheamh air. In Ãirinn, mar shampla, tÃ³gann sÃ© trÃ­ uair go leith chun taisteal i gcarr Ã³ ChiarraÃ­ go Baile Ãtha Cliath, ach ar an taobh eile de, sa traein, tÃ³gann sÃ© ceithre uair an chloig. Â Â

Â Â Kai Tegethoff, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MrÂ President, we all agree: we want to strengthen Europe's rail sector. And yet, talking about this topic, we're standing at 9.30 in the evening on Wednesday in front of an almost empty hemicycle. We all agree: we want to have a fully interoperable European railway network. And still the Council will always water down any good demand coming from this Parliament. We all agree: we want to have an attractive train system. We want to have trains full of happy passengers. And yet, after years of trying, I am not even able to book a ticket across borders or across different operators. We finally need to have a multi-modal booking and ticketing system that operates according to the system: if it is one journey, it needs to be one ticket.

We need to increase the funding for the Connecting Europe Facility to EURÂ 100Â billion in the next Multiannual Financial Framework, we finally need to stop just talking the talk and we need to start walking the walk.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Arno Bausemer, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, meine sehr verehrten Damen und Herren! Im Dezember 2022 wollte der ukrainische Botschafter in Deutschland meine Heimatstadt Stendal im Norden von Sachsen-Anhalt besuchen. Er erreichte zwar den Bahnhof in Berlin, konnte von dort aber aus technischen GrÃ¼nden nicht mit dem Zug abfahren. OberbÃ¼rgermeister und GÃ¤ste warteten im Stendaler Rathaus vergebensÂ â der Botschafter blieb in Berlin.

VerspÃ¤tungen und AusfÃ¤lle sind fÃ¼r Zugfahrer keine Ausnahme, sondern die Regel. Deutschland hat mit 40Â 000Â Kilometern zwar das grÃ¶Ãte Schienennetz in Europa, allerdings belaufen sich die jÃ¤hrlichen Pro-Kopf-Investitionen in Deutschland nur auf 115Â Euro. In Ãsterreich ist es dreimal so viel, Luxemburg investiert sogar pro Einwohner viermal so viel in das Schienennetz.

Besonders Ã¤rgerlich ist dieses Versagen der rot-grÃ¼n-gelben Bundesregierung fÃ¼r Reisende, denn trotz jÃ¤hrlich steigender Ticketpreise steigt die Zahl der VerspÃ¤tungen und AusfÃ¤lle. Bahnfahren ist daher fÃ¼r die meisten Menschen eher ein MobilitÃ¤tshindernis denn ein VergnÃ¼gen. Und auch wenn die Bahn als sauberes Verkehrsmittel gilt, so werden Sie doch nach dem Besuch von Zugtoiletten die Worte Bahnfahren und Sauberkeit nicht mehr im gleichen Satz verwenden.

Teuer, unzuverlÃ¤ssig und schmutzigÂ â leider hat die Bahn zu Recht ein schlechtes Image. Dabei kÃ¶nnte man mit der Bahn nicht nur schnell von A nach B fahren; man kÃ¶nnte auch die Seele baumeln lassen, die Landschaft genieÃen oder ein Buch der Kollegin Sibylle Berg lesen.

Die EuropÃ¤ische Union sollte den BÃ¼rgern in Europa zur Seite springen, generell mehr Investitionen in den Schienenverkehr auf den Weg bringen. Dass gerade eine Regierung mit Beteiligung der GrÃ¼nen den Bahnverkehr behindert, ist ein weiterer Beleg fÃ¼r deren praktische UnfÃ¤higkeitÂ â oder anders gesagt, etwas Lyrik am Abend: Es steht ein GrÃ¼ner auf der Schiene und glaubt, er regelt den Verkehr. Noch steht er da mit ernster Miene, doch kommt der Zug, steht er nicht mehr.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sophia Kircher (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, meine lieben Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Eine Autofahrt von Innsbruck nach Schwaz ist 34Â Kilometer lang und dauert, je nach Verkehrslage, rund 30Â Minuten. Genauso lange dauert jede gezwungene und kontrollbedingte Pause eines Zuges am Brenner. Der Grund dafÃ¼r: In Ãsterreich und in Italien gelten nicht die gleichen Regeln im Bahnverkehr. WÃ¤hrend die LKW auf der Autobahn vorbeirauschen und sich durch die TÃ¤ler stauen, steht die Bahn buchstÃ¤blich am Abstellgleis. Immer noch hemmen zu viele nationale Regelungen den grenzÃ¼berschreitenden Bahnverkehr, und die Schiene als Alternative zur transitgeplagten StraÃe bleibt daher unattraktiv und auch ungenutzt.

FÃ¼r viele ist Tirol eine reine TransitachseÂ â fÃ¼r uns ist es unser wertvoller Lebensraum und unsere Heimat. Nur mit der Verlagerung des GÃ¼terverkehrs auf die Schiene kÃ¶nnen wir solche Regionen entlasten. Dazu brauchen wir wichtige Weichenstellungen. Zum einen mÃ¼ssen wir die rechtlichen HÃ¼rden abbauen; LokfÃ¼hrer mÃ¼ssen immer noch Bremstests an den GrenzÃ¼bergÃ¤ngen durchfÃ¼hren. Zum anderen mÃ¼ssen wir die notwendige Infrastruktur ausbauen; besonders notwendig ist hier das europÃ¤ische Kernnetz und vor allem die Zulaufstrecken des Brennerbasistunnels.

Es darf nicht sein, dass Regionen wie meine Heimat unter den VersÃ¤umnissen der EU-Verkehrspolitik leiden. Daher rufe ich Sie alle zur Zusammenarbeit auf fÃ¼r einen einheitlichen und ausgebauten europÃ¤ischen Bahnraum.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Vivien Costanzo (S&D). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Wir sprechen viel Ã¼ber Nachhaltigkeit, Ã¼ber den GrÃ¼nen Deal. Wichtig ist dabei, das Abstrakte konkret zu machenÂ â hier bei uns der Ausbau und die AttraktivitÃ¤t der Schiene. SchlieÃlich, wir haben es eben gehÃ¶rt, kann allein ein GÃ¼terzug bis zu 52Â LKW ersetzen. Wichtig ist dabei ein europaweiter GÃ¼terstreckenausbau und die Harmonisierung fÃ¼r lange GÃ¼terzÃ¼ge bis zu 740Â Metern, denn das schafft mehr KapazitÃ¤ten mit gleichzeitig mehr QualitÃ¤t bei niedrigeren Kosten.

Auch im Personenverkehr sind wir leider von einer Harmonisierung weit entfernt. Wir brauchen einen neuen Vorschlag der Kommission fÃ¼r ein multimodales TicketingÂ â ein System, eine App fÃ¼r die ganze EU. Aber diese App reicht nicht; wir brauchen auch das transeuropÃ¤ische Schienennetz. Dabei noch ein Hinweis: Am Ende darf das alles nicht zulasten der Arbeitnehmerinnen und Arbeitnehmer im Transportwesen gehen. Im Gegenteil: Deren Schutz und Rechte sind zentral.

Also: Wenn wir unsere Klimaziele erreichen wollen, den GÃ¼tertransport optimieren und in der LebensrealitÃ¤t vieler Pendlerinnen und PendlerÂ â und da gilt auch der NahverkehrÂ â und der Menschen in Europa ankommen wollen, rennt uns die Zeit tatsÃ¤chlich langsam weg.

Â Â Ana Vasconcelos (Renew). â Senhor Presidente, Caros Colegas, Caro ComissÃ¡rio, sustentabilidade ambiental e competitividade econÃ³mica sÃ£o prioridades para a Europa. RazÃµes hÃ¡ muitas, mas vÃ£o todas dar ao mesmo. A ferrovia nÃ£o Ã© atrativa para consumidores. Â Â JoÃ£o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ». â Senhora Deputada Ana Vasconcelos, trouxeânos aqui preocupaÃ§Ãµes com a ferrovia, e eu queria perguntarâlhe: porque Ã© que nÃ£o faz a crÃ­tica â oÂ mea culpa â Ã s polÃ­ticas de liberalizaÃ§Ã£o do setor, que tÃªm forÃ§ado as privatizaÃ§Ãµes e a concentraÃ§Ã£o do setor ferroviÃ¡rio nas mÃ£os das multinacionais? Â Â Ana Vasconcelos (Renew), Resposta segundo o procedimento Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ». â Caro Colega, se estiver atento ao Ãºltimo relatÃ³rio que foi publicado pela ComissÃ£o Europeia, que menciona precisamente que o aumento de competitividade e liberalizaÃ§Ã£o, sobretudo nas ligaÃ§Ãµes de alta velocidade, contribuÃ­ram para baixar tarifas, para melhorar a qualidade do serviÃ§o e, acima de tudo, aumentar a percentagem de utilizaÃ§Ã£o por consumidores.

Portanto, sim, acho que o caminho Ã© por aÃ­. Acho que tambÃ©m temos muitas questÃµes a colocar sobre o domÃ­nio do Estado neste setor dos transportes. AliÃ¡s, somos os dois portugueses. Sabemos que as greves constantes â porque tambÃ©m o Estado falha aos seus trabalhadores â tÃªm sido uma das causas prioritÃ¡rias deste problema. E, jÃ¡ agora, se calhar tambÃ©m Ã© altura de devolver aos passageiros, que tÃªm direitos, aquilo que perdem quando nÃ£o tÃªm o serviÃ§o que contrataram.

Â Â Tilly Metz (Verts/ALE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, lâUnion europÃ©enne vise Ã  augmenter la part du transport ferroviaire dans sa stratÃ©gie de mobilitÃ©. Mais, pour atteindre nos objectifs climatiques et rÃ©ussir la transition vers le rail, il faut Ã©laborer une politique de transport cohÃ©rente et donner la prioritÃ© Ã  lâaugmentation des investissements dans le transport ferroviaire.

Il est dÃ¨s lors incomprÃ©hensible que la Commission encourage ce transfert modal tout en proposant, en mÃªme temps, dâautoriser des mÃ©ga-camions sur les routes et en permettant de subventionner les Ã©nergies fossiles pour les aÃ©roports.

Des fonds suffisants doivent Ãªtre allouÃ©s au rail, et surtout Ã  lâinfrastructure et Ã  la numÃ©risation, grÃ¢ce Ã  une taxation adaptÃ©e des Ã©missions des autres modes de transport et Ã  la tarification du CO2. Un changement de cadre est nÃ©cessaire pour pouvoir, au sens propre du terme, nous remettre sur les rails.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Arash Saeidi (The Left). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, la Commission europÃ©enne ne dÃ©fend pas une politique ferroviaire ambitieuse, mais un dogme. Depuis des annÃ©es, elle favorise la libÃ©ralisation et la concurrence au dÃ©triment du service public, des usagers et de lâenvironnement. Pour quel rÃ©sultat? Nous ne disposons plus que dâun rÃ©seau sous-investi de capacitÃ©s ferroviaires limitÃ©es. Le fret ferroviaire ne cesse de reculer, les trains du quotidien sont dÃ©laissÃ©s.

OÃ¹ est la grande ambition pour le rail? Seuls 18Â % des marchandises circulent par le rail en Europe, bien en deÃ§Ã  des objectifs annoncÃ©s. La Commission nâa pas su imposer une politique forte de transfert modal, faute dâinvestissements et de volontÃ©. Pis, elle ambitionne aujourdâhui de faire rouler des mÃ©ga-camions alors que nous devrions tout faire pour rÃ©duire les Ã©missions de CO2 en donnant la prioritÃ© au rail.

La part du ferroviaire dans le transport de voyageurs sâÃ©tablit, elle, Ã  prÃ¨s de 6Â % Ã  peine. Les interconnexions europÃ©ennes sont un dÃ©sastre. PlutÃ´t que de miser sur la concurrence, nous devons impulser la coopÃ©ration entre opÃ©rateurs historiques. Pour dÃ©velopper le rail, il faut rompre avec cette logique de marchÃ© aveugle, dont mÃªme nos amis britanniques sont revenus. Il nous faut des investissements publics massifs, une rÃ©elle harmonisation des infrastructures et un soutien dÃ©terminÃ© au secteur.

Le rail est un bien commun. Il est lâun des principaux leviers sur lesquels nous appuyer pour rÃ©duire notre empreinte, et non une marchandise quelconque.

Â Â Carmen Crespo DÃ­az (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, estamos hablando del futuro del ferrocarril en Europa, pero ahora tenemos un presente y, precisamente, en nuestro paÃ­s, donde hay un caos ferroviario sin precedentes. Y eso afecta a la forma precaria con que se ve la conexiÃ³n europea.

Aislamiento de regiones como Extremadura, Cantabria, zonas importantes de Galicia o provincias como Huelva o la mÃ­a, AlmerÃ­a, que estÃ¡n absolutamente aisladas.Â Y el Ãºltimo incidente en ChamartÃ­n, en Madrid, donde se ha descarrilado un tren que ha puesto en peligro a los usuarios al circular un convoy sin control.Â Quiero decir que hay retrasos, cancelaciones, trenes obsoletos, nudos saturados.... Estos hechos no son aislados y por eso estamos pidiendo que la ComisiÃ³n actÃºe.

Europa no se puede permitir esa imagen, tiene que intervenir, y apostemos realmente tambiÃ©n por ejes ferroviarios importantÃ­simos en la penÃ­nsula ibÃ©rica. Estamos hablando de Sevilla-Huelva-Faro, de Madrid-Lisboa, de Lisboa-Oporto-Vigo o del corredor central y el corredor mediterrÃ¡neo, que en estos momentos lleva un retraso acumulado hasta AlmerÃ­a y que necesitamos que, con fondos europeos, estÃ© controlado, para que vayamos mÃ¡s rÃ¡pido en todo ello.

Por tanto, nuestro paÃ­s en este momento, en el presente, tiene un negativo muy importante en el que la ComisiÃ³n Europea debe participar e intervenir.

DostÄpnoÅÄ to kolejny kluczowy obszar. KaÅ¼da stacja i pociÄg powinny byÄ wyposaÅ¼one tak, aby umoÅ¼liwiÄ podrÃ³Å¼ osobom z niepeÅnosprawnoÅciÄ. DziÄki temu podrÃ³Å¼owanie kolejÄ bÄdzie dostÄpne dla wszystkich.

PodsumowujÄc, wspieranie praw pasaÅ¼erÃ³w kolei nie polega tylko na ulepszaniu usÅug. Chodzi o zapewnienie uczciwoÅci i godnoÅci kaÅ¼demu podrÃ³Å¼nemu. WspÃ³Åpracujmy, aby podrÃ³Å¼ kolejÄ byÅa pozytywnym doÅwiadczeniem dla wszystkich w caÅej Europie. To waÅ¼ne wyzwanie dla nowej Komisji Europejskiej.

Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fin de las intervenciones con arreglo al procedimiento de solicitud incidental de uso de la palabra (Â«catch the eyeÂ»))

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Didier Reynders, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, thank you for this debate, and I've taken note of all your comments and questions. Of course, I will convey them to CommissionerÂ Hoekstra, but I want to say that I have taken note, in particular, of the different comments on passenger rights, because that was a specific topic for many of you.

Also: more efficient ticketing, access to trains for all, but also the needs of more investment and maybe a specific national situation, because I have listened to many comments about Spain, Portugal, Romania, Poland and maybe other Member States. It's very important to take care of all of those comments.

There is a clear willingness among all of us to make sure that travellers can use a high-quality network of long-distance rail services in the European Union. We also want to create enabling conditions for rail freight services to thrive. It is important to think about the persons and also goods in the different investment that will be needed to programme.

I am confident that the current regulatory framework, including the new TEN-T Regulation, will contribute to a safer and more integrated European rail system. Therefore, its implementation will be key. Reinforced governance provisions in the new regulation will help us get there. This includes a strengthened role for European coordinators and the possibility to adopt implementing acts for the realisation of the corridors.

Having said that, Member States' commitment and ambition will of course be the main factor for the completion of the network. So thank you very much for your comments and remarks and we will have to work on this in the next years.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â El presidente. â Se cierra el debate.

No hay explicaciones de voto porque nadie ha solicitado realizar una explicaciÃ³n de voto.

Declaraciones por escrito (artÃ­culo 178 del Reglamento interno)
